title: [Phase 1 — Investigate] Audit League / Contest / Scoring / Provider data flow
description:
First phase of the umbrella epic (pool-master-rop.78).

## Goal

Produce a single document — `docs/league-contest-substrate-audit.md` — that catalogs the current state of the live-scoring data flow with enough depth to anchor a coherent Phase 2 design.

## Deliverable structure

The doc has these sections:

1. **Data-flow overview** — how a stat event from a provider lands in entry score and ranking, with file pointers
2. **Domain model snapshot** — entities, relationships, lifecycle states; what's typed vs what's opaque
3. **Contract surface inventory** — every league/contest/entry/scoring/provider DTO; which are well-typed, which use `JsonObjectSchema`, which have mappers, which don't
4. **State mirroring patterns** — every place server data is duplicated into Zustand or local component state
5. **Scoring pipeline mechanics** — how recalc, rollup, and stat-event consumption interact; transaction boundaries; concurrency hazards
6. **Provider integration** — adapters, registry, normalization gaps
7. **Test coverage map** — what's tested at each layer, what's mocked away, what isn't tested at all
8. **Cross-reference table** — produced AFTER sections 1-7 are complete. Audit findings on the LEFT; existing `pool-master-rop.*` defect IDs on the RIGHT; mapping shows which existing defects are symptomatic of the substrate issues found, which are independent, and which might be misfiled. The audit's findings are written FIRST so the existing defect cluster doesn't bias the analysis (per user direction 2026-05-04: "I worry that it might corrupt or influence the analysis").
9. **Open questions for design phase** — explicit list of decisions Phase 2 must make

## Acceptance

- The doc covers all 9 sections.
- Every section is anchored with file paths + line numbers (or commit SHAs) where current behavior lives.
- Section 8 maps audit findings to existing `pool-master-rop.*` defects (not the other way around — findings come first).
- The user reads it and signs off (or pushes back) before Phase 2 starts.

## Process notes

- The audit is FRESH analysis. Existing `pool-master-rop.*` defect descriptions are prior investigations into specific symptoms; reading them up-front would bias the framing toward those symptoms. Read the code, the rules, and the data flow first; cross-reference defects only at section 8.
- Relevant rules to anchor expected-shape framing: `rules/architecture-rules.md §2`, `rules/service-rules.md §4`, `rules/react-ui-rules.md §3`, `rules/model-change-rules.md`.
- Pause for user review after the doc lands; do NOT auto-roll into Phase 2.

## Ownership

- **Archie** leads (architecture)
- **Dom** contributes the domain-model section (entities, relationships, lifecycle)
- **Brad** contributes the service-layer + scoring-pipeline sections (transaction boundaries, concurrency, provider integration)
- Test-coverage map is a section in the audit; does not require Tess as a separate owner.

## Out of scope for Phase 1

- Designing the redesigned shape — that's Phase 2.
- Re-parenting or restructuring `pool-master-rop.68` / `rop.70` epics — those stay as-is until Phase 3 review decides whether to.
close_reason: Phase 1 audit doc landed at docs/league-contest-substrate-audit.md (513 lines, 9 sections + 31 sub-sections). Original audit found 24 distinct JsonObjectSchema / z.record(z.unknown()) sites across DTOs; PR #21 typed rop.14.1 (LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema), leaving 23 remaining. rop.14 covers 4 of those open sites (leagues + contests area); 19 are unfiled (admin / history / ingestion / scoring / participants / common DTOs). Other findings: routes that emit domain objects without a mapper (dashboard, bulk-ops, history, drafts, scoring); per-contest scoring lock isn't a standalone defect (implicit in rop.7 / .8 / .11 / .12); cross-provider LatestPerformanceSnapshotDto normalization needed at the seam (rop.14.5 + rop.16 + rop.1 converge); inline queryKey arrays in 68 places (q8h.A2 planned); ProviderStatEvent not validated at the bus boundary. Pass 1 Riley self-check: 1 MED + 2 LOW addressed in commit 9685e642. Pass 2 Codex stale-reference findings (§2.3, §3.1, §3.4, §7.3, §8.1) addressed in commit c465848c. Doc framing: §§1-7 written without consulting existing rop.* defects to avoid bias; §8 cross-references audit findings to existing defects; §9 captures 17 open questions (Q1/Q2/Q3/Q8/Q11/Q12 since answered by PR #22's typed-end-to-end rules — §9 retained as Phase 1 snapshot, Phase 2 design plan supersedes). Phase 2 (the design plan) does NOT auto-start — user review is the explicit gate.

title: [EPIC] League / Contest / Scoring substrate redesign — investigate → design → review → implement
description:
4-phase coordinator for the architectural work that the recurring defect cluster in League / Contest / Scoring / Provider lanes points at. Per the 2026-05-04 user direction: "Continued issues in the league and contest areas. And that's the meat of this application. ... I can't make forward progress on Contests, Contest Entry, and updates to contests via live scoring with all of these model/binding issues."

## Triggering defect cluster

Open defects whose root cause appears to be the same substrate, currently filed independently. Listed for awareness; the audit (Phase 1) will produce its own findings independently and only cross-reference these at the end:

- **DTO contract gaps**: rop.14.2, rop.14.3, rop.14.4, rop.14.5
- **Frontend state mirroring server data**: rop.18, rop.20 (closed but symptomatic)
- **Component / page coupling**: rop.62
- **Scoring / standings / live-update pipeline**: rop.1 (P0), rop.7, rop.8, rop.11, rop.12
- **Provider integration**: rop.16, rop.4

Existing parent epics `pool-master-rop.68` (sync & scoring pipeline integrity) and `pool-master-rop.70` (contract-first enforcement) **stay as-is** through Phases 1–3. Re-parenting or restructuring those epics is a Phase 3 decision, not a precondition for the audit.

## Scope (wide, per user direction)

Whole live-scoring data flow from sport-data ingestion through ranking:

1. Sport-data ingestion — providers, registry, event scheduling, live-score polling
2. Domain entities — Sport, SportEvent, SportEventParticipant, SportEventResult, ProviderStatEvent
3. League domain — membership, commissioner, dashboard, audit log, bulk ops, invitations
4. Contest domain — creation, lifecycle, scoring config
5. Contest Entry domain — picks, drafts, owner, lifecycle, latestPerformance
6. Scoring — recalc service, standings rollup, entry scoring result, stat-event consumption, transaction boundaries, dedup
7. Standings + ranking — rollup vs recalc paths, persistence, frontend display
8. Frontend state — React Query, Zustand, form drafts, page state for League/Contest/Entry views
9. Test coverage — unit / integration / FAPI / E2E + mocking patterns

## Phases

- **Phase 1 — Investigate** (rop.78.1): catalog current data flow, contract gaps, state-mirror patterns, scoring races, provider integration gaps, test coverage holes. Deliverable: `docs/league-contest-substrate-audit.md`. Owner: Archie (lead) + Dom (domain model) + Brad (service / scoring pipeline).
- **Phase 2 — Design plan**: based on audit, produce design with canonical DTO shapes, mapper layer, scoring pipeline architecture, provider normalization, frontend state-layer split, slice-by-slice rollout plan. Deliverable: `plans/<NN>-league-contest-substrate-redesign.md`. Owner: Archie.
- **Phase 3 — Review / discuss** (gate — no story): user reads plan, pushes back, agrees on direction. Phase 3 also re-evaluates whether `rop.68` / `rop.70` get re-parented or restructured.
- **Phase 4 — Implement** (N slices, defined by Phase 2): each slice = its own PR, multi-pass review, narrow blast radius. Existing in-scope defects get re-parented under this epic (or stay independent, per Phase 2's recommendation) with `blocked-by` against the relevant slice.

## Note on rop.14.1

`rop.14.1` (Type LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema) is independently tractable and can ship NOW per user decision (2026-05-04). The audit (Phase 1) will examine the shipped code as part of section 3 to verify it complies with the redesigned pattern that emerges in Phase 2. If non-compliant, a follow-up Phase 4 slice re-shapes it.

## Acceptance

- Phase 1 audit doc reviewed and signed off by user.
- Phase 2 design plan reviewed and signed off by user.
- Phase 4 slices each pass the standard multi-pass review gate.
- All in-scope existing defects either: closed by a Phase 4 slice, redefined, or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- The "I can't make forward progress on Contests, Contest Entry, and updates to contests via live scoring" condition is no longer true — feature work in those domains lands without tripping on the substrate.

## Out of scope

- Webapp UX / page layout work that doesn't intersect with the data flow (theming, navigation, decorative components).
- Auth / session / security work unless the audit surfaces a substrate-level auth gap.
- Mobile clients (iOS / Android — currently archived).

## Companion design plan

Will live at `plans/<NN>-league-contest-substrate-redesign.md` after Phase 2; the audit doc lands at `docs/league-contest-substrate-audit.md` after Phase 1.

title: JWT_SECRET fallback ships a deterministic dev signing key in production code
description:
**Severity: CRITICAL** if it ever ships to production with the fallback active. A deterministic JWT signing key whose value is in source code (and identical across deployments) is exploitable: anyone who reads the source can forge tokens for any deployment that didn't override the `JWT_SECRET` env var. The fallback masks misconfiguration — the app will run successfully in production with no JWT secret set, just silently signing with the published default.

Surfaced by Sage Pass 3 review on PR #8 (`pool-master-rop.2`) on 2026-05-04. Pre-existing pattern; not introduced by that PR. Filed under the deferred security epic per the existing scope decision (revisit before external launch).

## File-anchored locations

The fallback appears in **three production files** with the same literal string:

1. `packages/core-api/src/plugins/admin-auth.ts:19`
   ```ts
   const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET ?? 'poolmaster-dev-secret-change-in-production';
   ```

2. `packages/core-api/src/plugins/auth-guard.ts:74`
   ```ts
   const jwtSecret = process.env.JWT_SECRET ?? 'poolmaster-dev-secret-change-in-production';
   ```

3. `packages/core-api/src/modules/auth/auth-service.ts:88`
   ```ts
   this.jwtSecret = process.env.JWT_SECRET ?? 'poolmaster-dev-secret-change-in-production';
   ```

The duplication compounds the risk:
- Three independent fallback sites mean three independent ways to silently ship dev secrets.
- Per `service-rules.md §1` *Banned Backend Patterns*, this is exactly the "duplicated `??` fallback" pattern the rule forbids — single bootstrap, fail fast, no per-module fallbacks.

## Rule violations

- `service-rules.md §1` *Banned Backend Patterns* — no env-var fallbacks; secrets must come from a single bootstrap source that throws on missing values.
- Adjacent: `pool-master-rop.69` (auth-surface coding hygiene) covers duplicated auth-related constants, but the security severity belongs here, not there.

## Suggested fix

1. **One bootstrap module** (`packages/core-api/src/core/config.ts` or similar) that reads `JWT_SECRET` once at startup and throws if undefined. No fallback string — production must fail loud.
2. **All three call sites** import the resolved secret from the bootstrap module instead of reading `process.env.JWT_SECRET` directly.
3. **Test infrastructure** sets `JWT_SECRET` explicitly in test setup (the unit test for ingestion auth already does this — `tests/unit/core-api/ingestion-routes-authz.test.ts:11`). Production has no excuse to read a default.
4. **Rule-enforcement scanner** (already part of `npm run rules:check`) should be extended to flag any `process.env.X ?? ...string-literal...` pattern in `packages/core-api/`. This makes the banned-pattern rule self-enforcing.

## Trigger to revisit

Per the parent epic (`pool-master-rop.76`):
- Before opening the app to anyone outside the build team.
- Before any deployment that doesn't sit behind another layer (e.g., a private VPN, a mock-only environment).
- Before any non-mock provider connection that brings real-money or real-PII context into scope.

## Out of scope for this defect

- Rotating the JWT signing key on the existing dev/test environments. Once the fallback is removed, rotation is a deployment-config concern, not a code change.
- Migrating to asymmetric signing (RS256). Symmetric `HS256` with a strong env-supplied secret is sufficient for the current deployment shape; revisit if signing keys ever need to be exposed to a verifier outside the issuing service.

title: Sync & scoring pipeline integrity
description:
Cross-cutting epic for the mock-feed → ingestion → participant → contest recalc → entry score → standings pipeline. This is the highest-risk surface in the system and the least defended.

## Goals
- Make the live-score path actually move standings (today it doesn't — see rop.1).
- Get rid of mock-only provider hardcoding and dead/duplicate orchestration.
- Add atomicity, idempotency, and backpressure to recalculation.
- Cover the whole flow with at least one end-to-end FAPI test before declaring it shippable.
- Stop swallowing errors / synthesizing fallback scores.

## Key decisions to make
- Where do live-score writes land so the recalc service can read them? (One persisted shape, not two.)
- Event-driven recalc (debounced per contest) vs periodic rollup — pick one path and delete the other.
- Idempotent upsert vs delete-then-recreate for entry scoring rows.
- Provider registry: factory keyed off providerId, with strict-runtime guard against mock-contest-feed.

## Recommended sequencing (failing-test-first per rules/testing-rules.md §3)
1. rop.6 — synthetic zero-score fix + test rewrite (one slice).
2. rop.1 — converge the two ingestion writes onto one persisted shape that scoring reads.
3. rop.7 — kill the dead StandingsRollup or wire it; unify rank assignment.
4. rop.11 — outer transaction around recalculateContest entry loop.
5. rop.8 — replace delete-then-recreate with idempotent upsert + per-contest serialization.
6. rop.12 — debounce stat-event-driven recalc per contest.
7. rop.5 — provider factory + strict-runtime guard.
8. rop.15 — FAPI test that walks mock-feed → ingestion → recalc → entry score end-to-end.
9. rop.13, rop.26, rop.32, rop.33, rop.34, rop.36, rop.42 — supporting cleanup.

## Children
See child defects for file-anchored locations and suggested fixes.

title: ScoringService.getEntryScore returns synthetic zero-score for missing entries (locked in by test)
description:
**Severity:** CRITICAL · **Category:** mock-data-in-prod + testing · **Reviewer:** Brad (B-3 + B-20)

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/service.ts:115-123 (the synthetic fallback)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/handler.ts:43-56 (handler ships the fabricated 200 response)
- tests/unit/core-api/scoring-service.test.ts:4-40 (test that locks in the wrong behavior)

**Problem:** When the entry is missing or belongs to another contest, getEntryScore returns { entryId, contestId, totalScore: 0, timeline: [] } — a fabricated default object. The handler ships this to the client as a 200 response. The unit test at scoring-service.test.ts:4 asserts this fabricated shape, codifying the bug.

**Why it's wrong:** Direct violation of testing-rules §1B 'Synthetic fallbacks — returning a fabricated default object when the real lookup fails' and service-rules §2 'If a dependency is missing, surface a real error. Do not fake the response.' Hides programmer errors and contest-mismatch bugs behind a believable-looking score. The locked-in test also violates AGENTS.md Non-Negotiables ('weaken tests to match known-wrong service behavior').

**Suggested fix:** Throw ContestEntryNotFoundError (already declared in error-handler) when the entry is missing or contest mismatches; return 404 with CONTEST_ENTRY_NOT_FOUND. Replace the existing test with negative-path coverage that asserts the thrown error and the 404 response, referencing the relevant UC/BR ID. Ship as one defect-fix slice with failing-test-first proof per rules/testing-rules.md §3.
notes: Starting slice pool-master-rop.6 on branch pool-master-rop.6-synthetic-score-not-found. Planned approach: replace synthetic zero-score fallback in ScoringService.getEntryScore with a typed not-found/domain error, ensure handler returns the standard 404 error envelope, and update/add unit or route coverage that references pool-master-rop.6. Validation plan: write the regression test first and observe it fail on current behavior, then implement fix and run focused scoring tests plus rules/api checks as applicable. Risk callouts: no production fakes or fallback score objects; preserve existing valid-score behavior.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.6. Files changed: packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/service.ts; packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/routes.ts; packages/shared/generated/openapi.json; packages/shared/generated/hey-api/types.gen.ts; tests/unit/core-api/scoring-service.test.ts; tests/integration/core-api/scoring-read.integration.ts. Defect proof: the regression test was first changed to expect ContestEntryNotFoundError and failed on the broken code because getEntryScore resolved with a fabricated zero-score DTO. Fix: missing or contest-mismatched entries now throw ContestEntryNotFoundError and the route advertises the 404 error envelope. Gates: focused scoring unit test passed; focused scoring integration test passed; api:refresh/api:check/api:validate passed; core-api typecheck passed; touched-file eslint passed; rules:check passed with existing warn-only baselines; CI on PR #4 is green. Residual risk: none for this defect slice.

title: Sport-data provider registry is hardcoded to mock-contest-feed only; real adapters unreachable
description:
**Severity:** CRITICAL · **Category:** mock-data-in-prod · **Reviewers:** Brad (B-7) + Archie (A-1)

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/provider-bindings.ts:82-92
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/index.ts:1-5

**Problem:** registerConfiguredProviders() throws on any provider id that isn't 'mock-contest-feed'. Four other adapter classes (OpenF1Adapter, OddsApiAdapter, EspnAdapter, PgaTourAdapter) are exported and built but unreachable by the registration path. isStrictRuntimeEnvironment(env) is computed but only logged — there is no production guard preventing the mock from being the active provider in QA/staging/prod. In prod, resolved_sport_data_default_provider is empty so ingestion silently does nothing.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates rules/architecture-rules.md §3 No Mock Data in Application Code and AGENTS.md Non-Negotiables. The mock service itself is a clean external HTTP boundary, but provider-bindings.ts cements a mock-only world inside packages/core-api/src. The other adapters are dead code.

**Suggested fix:** Either (a) introduce a clean provider factory keyed off providerId that supports the existing real adapters and reject mock-contest-feed in strict runtimes, or (b) remove the unused real adapter classes until there is a wiring story. Don't both ship the adapters and silently exclude them.
notes:
Starting parallel remediation from worker Curie while main agent works pool-master-rop.6. Planned approach: add failing-test-first provider binding coverage for pool-master-rop.5, replace mock-only provider registration with an explicit provider factory for existing adapters, reject mock-contest-feed in production/staging unless an explicit emergency override is configured, keep QA/local mock use through the same registry/config path, and validate missing required provider credentials such as ODDS_API_KEY. Validation plan: focused provider-binding unit test, core-api typecheck, touched-file eslint, rules/api checks. Risk callouts: no test-mode production branches; no silent fallback provider.
PR #5 follow-up locked: QA/staging/prod/production now fail fast when the default sport-data provider is missing; staging/prod mock-provider overrides now require SPORT_DATA_MOCK_PROVIDER_OVERRIDE_REASON with a meaningful log-safe reason. Added provider-binding unit coverage and updated rules/service-rules.md no-silent-config policy. Local gates passed: provider-bindings focused Jest, core-api typecheck, touched-file ESLint, full turbo typecheck, full ESLint, backend Jest, PoolMaster unit tests, service functional API with DATABASE_URL, rules:check, api:check, git diff --check.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.5 in PR #5. Files changed: packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/provider-bindings.ts; tests/unit/core-api/provider-bindings.test.ts; rules/service-rules.md; .beads/issues.jsonl. Fix: provider registration now uses an explicit factory for the configured real adapters, rejects unsupported provider IDs, blocks mock-contest-feed in staging/prod/production unless an explicit emergency override is supplied, requires SPORT_DATA_MOCK_PROVIDER_OVERRIDE_REASON with a meaningful log-safe reason for restricted-runtime mock overrides, and fails fast in QA/staging/prod/production when no default provider is configured. QA may still use an explicitly configured mock provider; local/dev/test may keep ingestion disabled only as an observable opt-out. Defect proof: provider-binding tests cover real adapter registration, production mock rejection, missing default provider failure in deployed runtimes including QA, required override reason, QA mock allowance, production override with reason, ODDS_API_KEY validation, and local unconfigured disabled behavior. Gates: focused provider-binding Jest passed; core-api typecheck passed; touched-file ESLint passed; full turbo typecheck passed; full ESLint passed; backend Jest passed; PoolMaster unit tests passed; service functional API passed with DATABASE_URL; rules:check passed with existing warn-only baselines; api:check passed; git diff --check passed; PR #5 CI is green. Riley refreshed review with no findings. Residual risk: external provider endpoint behavior and broader ingestion pipeline verification remain outside this provider-binding slice.

title: Generated SDK is mocked away with vi.mock('@/lib/api') in 30+ frontend tests; no MSW anywhere
description:
**Severity:** CRITICAL · **Category:** testing · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/root-admin-manage-leagues-page.test.tsx:27
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-detail-page.test.tsx:36
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/leagues-page.test.tsx:21
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/teams-page.test.tsx:52
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx:27
- plus ~25 more

**Problem:** Every page test replaces @/lib/api (the generated hey-api SDK) at module level with hand-rolled vi.fn() factories returning hand-shaped fixture payloads. Request construction, auth-retry interceptors, CSRF injection, and trace-id headers (all defined in lib/api.ts) never run. Whole tests verify no real wiring. `grep -RlE "msw|setupServer|http\." clients/poolmaster/src` returns 0 — MSW is nowhere in the frontend test tree.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §7 Banned Test Patterns explicitly forbids vi.mock('@/lib/api-client') or 'equivalent module-level replacement of the runtime API client'. rules/testing-rules.md §5 mandates MSW for tests where request wiring matters.

**Suggested fix:** Replace vi.mock('@/lib/api', ...) with MSW handlers under a shared tests/handlers/ directory. Each handler imports the generated request/response Zod schemas (or DTO types) so fixture drift surfaces immediately.

title: Ingestion mutating routes are unauthenticated — any user can trigger sport-wide ingestion
description:
**Severity:** CRITICAL · **Category:** authz/authn placement · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/routes.ts:32-217 (no isRootAdmin or adminAuth registration)
- packages/core-api/src/index.ts:271-276 (registered outside the /api/v1/admin prefix)

**Problem:** The ingestion module exposes 6 mutating routes (POST /sync/:sport, POST /events/:sport/:eventId/sync, POST /scores/:sport/:eventId, POST /results/:sport/:eventId, POST /odds/:sport) under /api/v1/ingestion/*. They go through authGuard only — no root-admin gate. Any authenticated member can trigger sport-wide ingestion, score polling, and result fetch. Routes are tagged 'Admin' in OpenAPI but unprotected at runtime.

**Why it's wrong:** Comparable admin operations under /api/v1/admin/* are gated by adminAuth (packages/core-api/src/plugins/admin-auth.ts). Direct violation of auth/authz placement cross-cut and rules/service-rules.md route-level guard expectations.

**Suggested fix:** Register the ingestion module under /api/v1/admin/ingestion behind adminAuth, or attach adminAuth as a preHandler to the ingestion module specifically.
notes:
Starting pool-master-rop.2. Planned approach: add a backend regression test proving an authenticated non-root user is rejected from a mutating ingestion route, observe it fail on current origin/main behavior, then apply the smallest route-level/admin-prefix protection for ingestion mutating routes. Primary files expected: packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/routes.ts, packages/core-api/src/index.ts if registration moves, and backend route/functional/integration tests only. Risk: auth middleware placement must not create public route drift or break root-admin ingestion access; avoid frontend or unrelated Beads/files. Validation: focused failing-then-passing regression first, then required local gates from testing-rules §3 including rules:check, api:check, typecheck, eslint, Jest, service functional API with DATABASE_URL when needed, poolmaster unit, and service merged coverage.
Scope correction applied: preserved the existing /api/v1/ingestion route surface and protected only mutating ingestion POST routes with route-level adminAuth. Read-only GET /providers remains on the existing surface without new root-admin enforcement. Failing proof observed before the fix: tests/unit/core-api/ingestion-routes-authz.test.ts failed with Expected 403, Received 200 for an authenticated non-root user calling POST /api/v1/ingestion/sync/GOLF; after the fix the same focused test passes and root-admin access remains allowed.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.2 in PR #8. Files changed: packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/routes.ts; packages/shared/generated/openapi.json; packages/shared/generated/hey-api/sdk.gen.ts; packages/shared/generated/hey-api/types.gen.ts; tests/integration/core-api/contract-verification-root-admin.integration.ts; tests/unit/core-api/ingestion-routes-authz.test.ts; .beads/issues.jsonl. Decision: preserved the existing /api/v1/ingestion route surface and attached route-level adminAuth only to mutating ingestion POST routes; GET /providers remains unchanged. Defect proof: tests/unit/core-api/ingestion-routes-authz.test.ts was added before the fix and failed on broken code with Expected 403, Received 200 for authenticated non-root POST /api/v1/ingestion/sync/GOLF; after the fix it passed and root-admin access remained allowed. Gates: npm run rules:check PASS with existing warn-only baselines; npm run api:refresh PASS; npm run api:check PASS; npm run api:validate PASS; npx turbo typecheck --force PASS; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0 PASS; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit PASS; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api PASS; npm run test:poolmaster:unit PASS; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:coverage:service:merged PASS; focused root-admin contract integration PASS outside sandbox for local Postgres. Riley: PR comment posted with no findings and PR marker updated. Residual risk: none known for this scoped child defect; broader pool-master-rop.76 remains deferred/open for other children.

title: Production scoring path is decoupled from live score ingestion
description:
**Severity:** CRITICAL · **Category:** integration · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/consumer/stat-event-consumer.ts:97-100
- packages/core-api/src/modules/contest-scoring/contest-scoring-recalculation-service.ts:74-86,128-143
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/persistence/ingestion-persistence.ts:560-583

**Problem:** publishStatEvents only emits stat.received events; it never updates SportEventParticipantSourceData. ContestScoringRecalculationService re-scores entries by reading the latest sportEventParticipant.sourceData[0].normalizedData, which is written exclusively by persistEventDetail (the participant-sync feed, not the live-score feed). Result: every stat event triggers a costly contest recalculation that re-uses stale normalized data, so live score polling cannot actually move a contest's standings.

**Why it's wrong:** Defeats the entire score-update pipeline. The 'source of truth' for scoring is unreachable from the live-score path.

**Suggested fix:** Either (a) write a fresh SportEventParticipantSourceData row from publishStatEvents/onLiveScores so findParticipantSourceData sees the new normalized data, or (b) have the recalculation pull stat events directly from the StatEvent ledger rather than sourceData. The two ingestion writes must converge on the same persisted shape that scoring reads.

title: Contest creation still lands in DRAFT instead of going live for entries
description: The implemented contest flow still assumes an intermediate DRAFT state, which conflicts with the current product requirement that a contest becomes live as soon as the commissioner creates it. Backend contest creation writes ContestStatus.DRAFT, functional tests still expect DRAFT, create-contest page says configuration is editable while still in draft, and contest detail only allows entry creation when status === OPEN. This is a core blocker for the commissioner->member flow. Evidence: packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/service.ts lines 79-84 create contests with ContestStatus.DRAFT; tests/functional/contests.functional.ts lines 149-182 assert DRAFT after creation; clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/create-contest-page.tsx lines 892-903 and clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-detail-page.tsx lines 391-395 still gate behavior around DRAFT vs OPEN.
close_reason: The commissioner web flow uses createManagedContest, which already creates OPEN contests. This issue was a triage misdiagnosis based on the generic ContestService path, not the active webapp path.

title: [Phase 4 — Implement] Substrate Prisma migration

3. Rename roster_picks → contest_entry_picks + add columns (contest_format NOT NULL with backfill 'ROSTER', period?, slot?, tier?, cost?, is_auto_picked)

5. Create new tables: contest_sport_events, sport_event_participant_golf_round
6. Create contest_entry_pick_golf_roster_contribution (FK to contest_entry_picks — table now exists)
7. Drop participants.metadata column, sports.stat_schema column
8. Drop participant_season_records, sport_event_participant_source_data tables
9. Backfill legacy SINGLE_EVENT → ROSTER on contest_config_templates and contest_timing_policies

No backfill of cross-table data. Per no-data clean rework rule — dev/test DBs reseed; CI runs migration on fresh DB.

Dependencies: foundation slice — no Phase 4 prereqs. Schema-first per the design conversation correction (plans/117 §16.1). Blocks rop.78.3 (typed adapter contracts emit into the typed substrate columns) and rop.78.5/.6/.7.

title: [Phase 2 — Design] League / Contest / Scoring substrate redesign plan
description:
Phase 2 of pool-master-rop.78. Phase 1 (rop.78.1) audit landed via PR #18; this slice produces the design plan.

Deliverable: plans/117-league-contest-substrate-redesign.md

Plan must cover (per the rop.78 substrate-audit conversation):
- Reference model: typed entity hierarchy (Sport / SportEvent / SportEventParticipant / SportEventParticipant<Category>Detail / Contest / ContestEntry / ContestEntryPick / ContestEntryPick<Category><ContestType>Contribution)
- Sport detail configuration: per-category detail tables (golf-roster current scope; tennis / basketball / NFL / F1 / NASCAR / soccer specs as future-work)
- Pick model: unified ContestEntryPick with optional period / slot / tier / cost; how each contest type populates it
- Contribution model: per-(category × contestType) tables (lean (b) decided in design conversation)
- Validity matrix: TypeScript const map of (tournamentFormat × contestType) valid combinations, code-side per architecture-rules
- Provider adapter normalization: each provider's getLiveScores produces typed SportEventParticipant<Category>Detail rows; ProviderStatEvent validation at bus boundary
- Scoring rule functions: (DetailRows, ContestRules) → ContributionRows pure functions per (category × contestType)
- DTO surface: canonical entity DTO per entity (one canonical full-shape DTO; permission-driven thin variants); Pick/Omit-derived mutation inputs
- Migration plan: one Prisma migration; drop 3 zombie pick tables + drop ParticipantSeasonRecord + reshape roster_picks → contest_entry_picks + add detail/contribution tables; no backfill (per no-data-clean-rework rule)
- Frontend implications: TanStack Query keys factory for new hierarchy; thin/full DTO consumption (entry list ↔ leaderboard transition); sport-polymorphic rendering pattern
- Test surface: FAPI scenario for live-scoring (covers rop.15); integration tests; unit tests per scoring rule
- Phase 4 child stories: slice ordering per audit §9 Q17 (provider performance shape → DTO redesign → mapper layer → per-contest scoring lock → rollup vs recalc unification → frontend state-layer split → test coverage closure)
- Remaining open questions (Q4-Q7 scoring pipeline mechanics, Q9 mock-feed SDK, Q10 bus validation, Q13 cache invalidation, Q14 MSW migration, Q15 FAPI scenario specifics, Q16 re-parent existing rop.*)

Anchored to the rules landed by PR #22 (rop-1xz):
- Strongly typed end-to-end principle
- One canonical DTO per entity (permission-driven variants only)
- Discriminated unions are physical (not nullable-conditional)
- Make impossible states unrepresentable at storage layer
- Open-ended additive substrate design
- No-data clean reworks
- Validity matrices live in code

Workflow: branch + PR + Pass 1 Riley + Pass 2 cross-model from Codex App. Doc-only; no Sage / Archie triggers (no security or code-architecture surface yet — that comes in Phase 4 implementation slices).

After Phase 2 design plan lands and you review/sign-off, Phase 3 user-review gate is satisfied and Phase 4 implementation slices spawn under rop.78 with rop.78.<N> IDs.
close_reason: Closed via PR #23 (merge commit 8dcce074). Files changed: plans/117-league-contest-substrate-redesign.md (1000 lines, 20 sections — Phase 2 design plan deliverable for pool-master-rop.78). Distills the rop.78 substrate-audit conversation into the canonical design plan, anchored to the rules landed by PR #22 (rop-1xz). Key decisions: 8 substrate tables for golf-roster scope (Sport, Participant, SportEvent, SportEventParticipant, SportEventParticipantGolfRound, Contest, ContestEntry, ContestEntryPick, ContestEntryPickGolfRosterContribution + ContestSportEvent M:N join) plus future-work specs for 6 other sports × 5 contest types; unified ContestEntryPick with denormalized contestType column enabling partial-index uniqueness per (category × contestType); per-(category × contestType) contribution tables; TypeScript const-map validity matrix; typed LiveScoreResult discriminated union at provider-adapter boundary (resolves rop.1 + Q10 ProviderStatEvent validation); stat-event-driven scoring with per-contest advisory lock + single transaction per (contest, stat event); permission-driven thin/full DTO split for ContestEntry; one Prisma migration with corrected ordering (rename roster_picks → contest_entry_picks before any FK creation); 12 Phase 4 implementation slices rop.78.3-.14 in dependency order with existing rop.* defects re-parented per audit §8.2. Pass 1 Riley self-check (commit ebd75443) found 4 MEDIUM + 2 LOW: participants-rename contradiction (resolved by NOT renaming — relaxed naming rule), missing audit Q-disposition table (added §17 mapping all 17 audit Q's), missing Beads parent-child dep to rop.78 (added via bd dep add --type parent-child), imprecise §9 cross-reference (tightened) — all addressed in fix-up commit 02df9269. Pass 2 Codex/Archie review (commit 02df9269) caught 3 HIGH design-plan issues that would have become Phase 4 migration/implementation blockers: (1) plan claimed 4 pre-redesign pick tables to drop but current schema only has roster_picks (contest_picks / bracket_predictions / draft_picks already dropped in earlier migrations; draft_pick_histories has 22 active call sites and is out-of-scope for this redesign); (2) migration ordering created contribution table FK before roster_picks → contest_entry_picks rename; (3) partial unique indexes WHERE contest_type = 'X' were unimplementable since ContestEntryPick didn't store contest_type — Postgres can't predicate on joined parent columns. Resolved by rewriting §4.3 (forward-looking unification, no zombies to drop), §13 (current-schema verification + corrected drop list + reordered migration steps), and §7.1 + §4.2 (denormalized contest_type onto ContestEntryPick with consistency-guarantee plan). All addressed in fix-up commit e8d0f9b0. Final approval from Codex App on e8d0f9b0. Phase 3 user-review gate: this design plan is the deliverable to be reviewed before any rop.78.<N> Phase 4 implementation slice spawns. Audit §9 question disposition: 12 of 17 questions resolved in plan body (Q1, Q2, Q3 by §12.1 canonical DTOs; Q4 §11.4 per-contest advisory lock; Q5 §11.3 stat-event-driven only; Q6 §11.5 single transaction; Q7 §11.6 no batching; Q8 by-design via §6 + §8 physical separation; Q9 §10.5 mock-feed SDK deletion; Q10 §10.3 LiveScoreResult Zod validation; Q11 §14.3 React Query / Zustand split; Q12 §14.1 query-keys factory; Q13 §14.2 hook-wrapper invalidation; Q14 §15.4 page-by-page MSW; Q15 §15.1 FAPI scenario; Q16 §16.2 fold-in plan; Q17 §16.1 12-slice ordering); 5 deferred to Phase 4 slices (lock derivation, tiebreaker semantics, retry/backoff, sport-name granularity migration, snake-draft fold-in). CI green on final head: lint/typecheck, mock build, poolmaster build/unit, service build/coverage.

title: Codify typed-model rules + product scope from substrate audit conversation
description:
Capture the durable model-typing rules that emerged from the pool-master-rop.78 substrate audit conversation, plus the product-scope statement (PoolMaster IS NOT fantasy / DFS / sportsbook / prop bets).

Rules to land (extractable from the conversation, all aligned to the principle: strongly typed end-to-end, no amorphous shapes):
1. Type alignment across layers (Prisma = service-layer interfaces = DTO Zod schema)
2. One canonical DTO per entity (no per-page variants; permission-driven exceptions only)
3. Mutation inputs derive from canonical DTO via Pick/Omit
4. DTO variants are permission-driven, not view-driven
5. Discriminated unions are physical (separate tables per (axis × axis) where data shapes diverge), not nullable-conditional columns
6. Make impossible states unrepresentable at the storage layer
7. Schema-as-documentation (every column applies to every row)
8. No bare noun reused for two distinct concepts (naming convention)
9. Substrate designs are open-ended-additive (new sports/types are additive, never alter-existing)
10. No-data clean rework (when no production data to preserve, prefer clean reworks over compatibility shims)
11. Validity / compatibility matrices live in code as typed const maps when small / infrequently changed

Files to update:
- rules/domain-model-conventions-rules.md (major addition — principle at top + 8 new sub-sections)
- rules/model-change-rules.md (no-data clean rework rule)
- rules/architecture-rules.md (validity-matrix-in-code rule)
- requirements/product-requirements/domain-concepts.md (PoolMaster scope — what we are not)

Workflow: branch + PR + Pass 1 marker + Pass 2 cross-model. Doc-only; no Sage / Archie triggers.

Parent: blocks Phase 2 of pool-master-rop.78 — these are the rules the substrate design plan will cite.
close_reason: Closed via PR #22 (merge commit 74b438d4). title: [rop.14.5] Type contests.dto.ts:220 latestPerformance — provider integration entanglement
description:
**Severity:** HIGH (inherited) · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/shared/dto/contests.dto.ts:220 — `latestPerformance: JsonObjectSchema.describe('Latest provider-backed performance snapshot…')`
- packages/core-api/src/mappers/contests.mapper.ts:72 — `latestPerformance: Record<string, unknown>` (the mapper TYPE itself uses Record)
- packages/core-api/src/mappers/contests.mapper.ts:216 — passthrough to DTO
- packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/service.ts:392, :926 — `normalizeLatestPerformance(value: unknown): Record<string, unknown>` (normalizer that returns opaque records)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/history/history-service.ts:153 — same passthrough

**Problem:** `latestPerformance` is provider-backed performance data attached to a contest entry's picked participant. The current normalizer returns an opaque `Record<string, unknown>` because the underlying provider response shape varies by provider (mock-contest-feed-provider, the-odds-api, openf1, espn, pga-tour) and isn't normalized in the service layer.

**BLOCKED on:**
1. The League/Contest contract architecture design phase
2. **Provider integration decisions** — what's the canonical normalized shape for performance data across all sport providers? This is upstream of the DTO fix.
3. Adjacent defects: rop.16 (mock-feed-provider hand-rolled SDK — same provider integration issue from a different angle), rop.1 (production scoring path decoupled from live ingestion — affects what "performance" even means at the contract layer).

**Suggested fix (post-design + post-provider-normalization):**
1. Define a canonical `LatestPerformanceSnapshotDto` schema in shared/dto. Likely fields: provider id, observation timestamp, sport-specific stat keys (or a typed union per sport), source feed event id.
2. Update `normalizeLatestPerformance` in `contests/service.ts` to enforce the canonical shape (validate via Zod parse, log + drop unknown fields).
3. Replace `JsonObjectSchema` reference in contests.dto.ts:220.
4. Regenerate OpenAPI + SDK.

**This is the largest of the 5 rop.14 children** — it can't be fixed without first deciding the provider integration story. Defer until rop.16, rop.1 progress AND the broader League/Contest design phase have produced a coherent answer.

title: [rop.14.4] Type LeagueBulkOperationResponseSchema (copy-season / import-members)
description:
**Severity:** HIGH (inherited) · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:347 (`LeagueBulkOperationResponseSchema = JsonObjectSchema`)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/routes.ts:484 (used by `/contests/copy-season`)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/routes.ts:501 (used by `/members/import`)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/bulk-handler.ts (the two handlers)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/bulk-service.ts (`copyLastSeason` returns `BulkContestCopyResult`; `importMembersFromCsv` returns a different result shape)

**Problem:** Two endpoints with TWO different response shapes share a single opaque `JsonObjectSchema` schema. Frontend consumers have no compile-time guarantee about either shape; the generated SDK can't distinguish copy-season results from import-members results.

**BLOCKED on the League/Contest contract architecture design phase + a design decision specific to this site.** Options:
1. **Split into two distinct schemas** — `LeagueCopySeasonResponseSchema` and `LeagueImportMembersResponseSchema`. Cleanest; each route gets its own typed contract.
2. **Discriminated union** — single `LeagueBulkOperationResponseSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('operation', [...])`. Compact but unusual.
3. **Generic "bulk operation result" envelope** — `{ created: T[], errors: { ... }[], skipped: T[] }` parametrized per operation. Most architecturally clean; matches typical bulk-operation patterns.

The decision interacts with: (a) what other bulk operations might land in this area later, (b) whether the frontend wants a single error-handling code path or per-operation handling.

**Suggested fix (post-design):** Adopt option 1, 2, or 3 from the design phase, define the schemas, add mappers from `BulkContestCopyResult` and the import-members result type, replace `JsonObjectSchema` references in both routes, regenerate OpenAPI + SDK.

title: [rop.14.3] Type LeagueDashboardResponseSchema.contests field
description:
**Severity:** HIGH (inherited) · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:334 — `contests: z.array(JsonObjectSchema).describe('Contest summaries included in the dashboard payload.')`

**Problem:** Same shape as rop.14.2 — the dashboard endpoint emits `Contest[]` domain objects directly (no DTO mapper). Frontend has no typed contract for the contest summaries on the commissioner dashboard.

**BLOCKED on the League/Contest contract architecture design phase.** The right "dashboard contest summary" DTO shape is one of several open questions:
- Is it identical to a list-view contest summary?
- Does it carry standings / status / participant counts that other views don't need?
- Should it be reused for the league-home contest list, the contests-page list, etc., or kept dashboard-specific?

These are entangled with the broader League/Contest domain modeling work that the recurring rop.14, rop.18, rop.20, rop.62, rop.63, rop.7, rop.8, rop.11, rop.12 defect cluster suggests is needed.

**Suggested fix (post-design):**
1. Adopt the agreed-upon ContestSummaryDto shape from the design phase.
2. Add a mapper for the dashboard handler to use.
3. Replace `z.array(JsonObjectSchema)` with `z.array(ContestSummaryDtoSchema)`.
4. Regenerate OpenAPI + SDK.

title: [rop.14.2] Type LeagueDashboardResponseSchema.league field
description:
**Severity:** HIGH (inherited) · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:332 — `league: JsonObjectSchema.describe('League summary payload driving the dashboard header.')`

**Problem:** The dashboard endpoint emits a `League` domain object directly (no DTO mapper applied). The committed contract uses `JsonObjectSchema` to hide the domain shape from typed consumers.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates `rules/architecture-rules.md §2` Contract-First and `rules/service-rules.md §4` (mapper-at-route-boundary). The dashboard is a primary commissioner surface; lack of typed contract is a recurring cause of frontend drift.

**BLOCKED on the League/Contest contract architecture design phase** (proposed parent epic: see [TBD] design-epic). The dashboard payload's `league` field could be:
- The existing `LeagueSummaryDtoSchema` (lighter — used by list views)
- The existing `LeagueDetailDtoSchema` (richer — used by detail view)
- A new `LeagueDashboardSummaryDto` (purpose-specific shape) — likely the right answer but requires investigation of what fields the dashboard UI actually consumes

This decision should be made as part of the broader contract redesign, not piecemeal here.

**Suggested fix (post-design):**
1. Adopt the agreed-upon DTO shape from the design phase.
2. Add a `mapDashboardLeague(domain) → dto` mapper.
3. Update `leagues/dashboard-handler.ts` to map before sending.
4. Replace `JsonObjectSchema` in `LeagueDashboardResponseSchema.league` with the new schema.
5. Regenerate OpenAPI + SDK; verify frontend dashboard consumer compiles.

title: [rop.14.1] Type LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema using AuditLogEntry interface
description:
**Severity:** HIGH (inherited from parent rop.14) · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:292 (`LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema = JsonObjectSchema`)
- packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:328 (`LeagueAuditEntriesResponseSchema` — the response that wraps the array)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/audit-service.ts:13 (`AuditLogEntry` interface — the canonical shape)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/audit-handler.ts (3 routes returning `{ entries }`: getLeagueAuditLog, getMemberAuditLog, getContestAuditLog)

**Problem:** `LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema` is currently aliased to `JsonObjectSchema` (i.e., `z.record(z.unknown())`). The OpenAPI export and generated SDK expose the audit log entry as an arbitrary JSON object, so frontend consumers reading commissioner audit trails have no field-level types. The actual shape is well-defined in the service layer (`AuditLogEntry` interface — id, leagueId, contestId?, actorId, action, category, description, beforeState?, afterState?, reason?, ipAddress?, createdAt) but isn't reflected in the contract.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates `rules/architecture-rules.md §2` Contract-First. Audit entries are a real client-facing surface (commissioner UI), and the typed shape is already known.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Replace `LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema = JsonObjectSchema` with a proper Zod schema mirroring the `AuditLogEntry` interface fields.
2. Decision needed: what to do with `beforeState`/`afterState` (genuinely opaque snapshots of arbitrary domain entities). Options: (a) keep as `z.record(z.unknown()).optional()` with documented "audit snapshot — opaque by design" description; (b) discriminate per category and type each variant. Option (a) is simpler and matches the audit log's design intent.
3. Add a mapper if the route currently returns the domain object verbatim (verify in `audit-handler.ts`).
4. Regenerate `openapi.json` + `hey-api/*` via `npm run api:refresh`.

**Tractable independently** of the broader League/Contest architecture work — the audit entry shape is already decoupled from league/contest domain mutations. This is the only one of the 5 rop.14 children that can ship without waiting on the architectural design phase.

**Defect protocol:** failing-test-before-fix is the OpenAPI export comparison — before the fix, the generated SDK type for `LeagueAuditEntryDto` is `{ [key: string]: unknown }`; after the fix, it has typed fields. The newly-enabled `npm run api:validate` (rop.17) will surface any priority-route regressions.
notes:
User decision 2026-05-04: ship rop.14.1 NOW (don't wait for the substrate redesign in pool-master-rop.78). The audit phase (rop.78.1) will examine the shipped LeagueAuditEntry typing to verify it complies with the redesigned pattern that emerges from Phase 2. If non-compliant, a follow-up Phase 4 slice re-shapes it.
Codex Pass 2 follow-up: recorded the PR #21 Pass-1 self-corrections in the Beads row before merge. Contest audit-log route now applies mapLeagueAuditEntryToDto at packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/routes.ts, so the live contest endpoint no longer emits raw AuditLogEntry objects. The beforeState/afterState zodToJsonSchema description-dedup issue is fixed by using distinct inline z.record(z.unknown()) schema instances; regenerated openapi.json keeps BEFORE and AFTER descriptions distinct. Mapper coverage proof: tests/unit/core-api/leagues-audit-mapper.test.ts covers required fields, Date-to-ISO conversion, contestId, beforeState, afterState, reason, ipAddress optional-spread paths, reference passthrough for opaque snapshots, and every AuditCategory value; targeted coverage reported 100% statements/branches/functions/lines for leagues-audit.mapper.ts.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.14.1 in PR. Files changed: packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts (replaced LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema=JsonObjectSchema with typed schema mirroring AuditLogEntry interface; added LeagueAuditCategorySchema enum); packages/core-api/src/mappers/leagues-audit.mapper.ts (new — Date→ISO string conversion + per-route boundary mapping); packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/audit-handler.ts (3 handlers now apply mapper before reply.send); packages/shared/generated/openapi.json + packages/shared/generated/hey-api/* (regenerated via npm run api:refresh). Defect proof: before fix, GetLeagueAuditLogResponses[200].entries was Array<{[key: string]: unknown}>; after fix, fully typed (id/leagueId/contestId?/actorId/action/category enum/description/beforeState?/afterState?/reason?/ipAddress?/createdAt). beforeState and afterState remain z.record(z.unknown()) per audit doc section 2.3 — they're intentionally opaque snapshots of arbitrary domain entities depending on action category; typing them per category would require a tagged-union the audit log doesn't currently carry. Audit doc explicitly notes this slice will be re-examined post-Phase 2 design; if non-compliant with the redesigned pattern, follow-up Phase 4 slice re-shapes it. Gates: rules:check PASS; api:check PASS; api:validate PASS (146/146); turbo typecheck --force PASS; eslint --max-warnings 0 PASS; jest backend (62/592) PASS; test:poolmaster:unit (92/285) PASS; test:service:functional-api (10/60) PASS.

title: [D1] Update Fran self-check (Pass 1 / pre-PR) to include §A rules
description: Extend personas/fran.md implementer self-check / pre-PR checklist to cover the new react-ui-rules.md sections from §A: state management, query keys + invalidation, SDK/types only, component reuse, CSS variables, form state, state communication, logging, env+date utilities. Pass 1 should catch these before opening the PR so Pass 5 (Felix) only sees genuine misses.

title: [B3] Felix Pass 5 in workflow-rules.md §6 review-pass table
description: Add row to the review-pass table in rules/workflow-rules.md §6: Pass 5 = Felix, frontend discipline, conditional on PR touching clients/, posted via gh pr review from a separate App identity. Update title-by-slice-kind guidance if needed.

title: [B2] Felix tool wrappers (.claude/agents/felix.md, .codex/agents/felix.toml)
description: Add .claude/agents/felix.md thin pointer (Read personas/felix.md) and .codex/agents/felix.toml thin pointer. Mirror riley/sage wrapper shape.

title: [B1] Felix persona playbook (personas/felix.md)
description: Create personas/felix.md — frontend-discipline reviewer playbook. Findings categories: SDK (non-SDK API calls), TYPES (parallel hand-rolled types), REUSE (duplicated markup/CSS), THEME (inline theme styles, hardcoded colors), STATE (server data in Zustand, fetch loops, missing query invalidation), FORM (form-state divergence), A11Y (semantic HTML, labels, focus). Severity calibration matches Riley/Sage pattern: zero CRITICAL/HIGH = approve. Trigger: any PR touching clients/. Always-on for clients/ touch. Include review post template with persona+pass+model header per workflow-rules.md §6.

title: [A5] CSS variables and theming (no hardcoded colors; no inline style for theme values)
description: Add §X CSS Variables and Theming: all themable values (color, spacing, font-size, font-family, border-radius, shadow) come from CSS variables declared in a single theme file; no hardcoded design-system values in component CSS; no inline style={{ color/spacing/font/border }} — reserved for genuinely dynamic values (computed positions, animations) only. CRITICAL: hardcoded color or spacing in component styles. Source: hardcoded colors/spacing/fonts in component styles make app non-themeable.

title: [A4] Component reuse threshold (rule of two; no duplicated markup)
description: Add §X Component Reuse: second time the same markup appears, extract to a component (rule of two, not three). No duplicated page-level layouts. Shared layouts/list shells/form patterns live in features/shared/ui. HIGH: same JSX > 10 lines duplicated across files. Source: same HTML structure and CSS appears in dozens of pages instead of reusable React components.

title: [A3] SDK + types only (no hand-rolled fetch / parallel DTOs / own types)
description: Add §X SDK Discipline: frontend consumes only @<projectName>/shared exports; no hand-rolled fetch/axios/XMLHttpRequest; no parallel DTOs; no own response types; no custom React Query hook wrapping fetch when an SDK hook exists. CRITICAL: any non-SDK API call. Source: Fran has been writing parallel hand-rolled types and bypassing SDK with direct fetch in places.

title: [A2] Query-key factory + mutation invalidation discipline
description: Add §X Query Keys + Invalidation: per-feature query-keys.ts factory; queryKey shape ['poolmaster', '<resource>', ...filters]; no inline literal queryKey arrays; mutations list every invalidated key; mutation success → invalidate or setQueryData. CRITICAL: stale cache after mutation. Source: 35 inline queryKey arrays in pool-master frontend, no centralized factory.
close_reason: Superseded by pool-master-rop.78.9 (Phase 4 substrate frontend query-keys slice). Per option (a) cross-stream coordination decision: substrate epic absorbs the query-key factory rule. The other q8h stories (q8h.1 / .3-.21) remain open under the q8h epic and are independent of substrate work.

title: [A1] State-management discipline rule (React Query / Zustand / no Redux / no fetch loops)
description: Add §X State Management to rules/react-ui-rules.md: React Query owns server state; Zustand owns ephemeral UI state ONLY; no useState/useEffect fetch loops; no Redux. Server data does not get mirrored into Zustand. Stores expose domain actions, not raw setters. No persist/devtools/immer middleware without justification. CRITICAL: server data in Zustand. Source: pool-master frontend has 36 React Query usages and one Zustand store but zero rules saying which state lives where.

title: [EPIC] Frontend rule hardening + Felix and Perry reviewers
description:
Codify the desired frontend patterns in rules so Fran does it right the first time, and add two new reviewer lenses (Felix for frontend discipline, Perry for performance) so violations surface at PR time even when the implementer's self-check misses them.

## Why this exists

Diagnostic pattern: Fran has been reinventing wheels (own types, own fetch wrappers, duplicated markup/CSS, ad-hoc state management) because the rules don't enforce the desired patterns. Riley is a generalist; Sage covers security; Archie covers architecture. Frontend has no dedicated lens, and performance (N+1, indexes, blocking I/O, bundle size) has no lens at all.

## Companion plan

`plans/116-frontend-rule-hardening.md` — full root-cause analysis, defect-class → rule-gap mapping, ordered category list, and acceptance criteria.

## Categories

- **§A. Fran rule additions** (`rules/react-ui-rules.md`) — 9 stories
- **§B. Felix reviewer persona + wrappers + integration** — 4 stories
- **§C. Perry reviewer persona + wrappers + integration** — 4 stories
- **§D. Fran self-check update** — 1 story
- **§E. Optional automation scanners** — 3 stories

Total: 21 stories. Each is one rule-file edit, one persona file, one wrapper, or one scanner — independently reviewable.

## Order of operations

1. §A first (rule foundation)
2. §D second (Fran self-check matches new rules)
3. §B third (Felix — highest-ROI reviewer)
4. §C fourth (Perry — complementary lens)
5. §E last, optional (automation)

## Acceptance

- All §A rule sections in `rules/react-ui-rules.md` cross-referenced from `personas/fran.md`.
- Felix + Perry callable as Claude and Codex subagents.
- Pass 5 (Felix) and Pass 6 (Perry) added to `rules/workflow-rules.md §6` with trigger conditions.
- `AGENTS.md` and `docs/PERSONA-FLOW.md` surface both new reviewers.
- Plan file deleted on epic close per `rules/workflow-rules.md §0`.

title: A4: Add scripts/check-test-traceability.sh — UC/BR/defect ID grep
description:
**Category:** §A — Automation gate
**Source defects:** pool-master-rop.10 (backend tests missing IDs), pool-master-rop.21 (frontend display-copy tests)
**Closes prose ban from:** rules/testing-rules.md §1A Test Self-Documentation

## What

Create scripts/check-test-traceability.sh that scans test files (*.test.ts, *.test.tsx, *.spec.ts, tests/integration/**, tests/functional/**, tests/unit/**) for describe(/it( blocks lacking at least one of:
- UC-<id>
- BR-<id>
- pool-master-<id>
- rule: ... (only allowed in tests/helpers/ and pure-utility tests once C2 lands)

**CI is the authoritative gate.** Pre-commit hook is optional.

## Why

§1A already requires this in prose. ~30 backend test files and many frontend tests violate it. See plan 115 §Cause 1.

## Rollout — required warn-only baseline phase

Because traceability parsing has false-positive risk (comments above the describe block, multi-line patterns, indirect references), this gate ships in **two slices**:

**Slice 1 (this story):**
- Script lists offender file:test-name pairs.
- Initial run on main produces a baseline count (recorded in closing note).
- CI runs the script in **warn-only** mode for at least one full slice cycle.
- During the warn-only phase, record the false-positive rate from at least 5 manual spot-checks.

**Slice 2 (separate follow-up story, opened at slice 1 close):**
- Convert to fail-on-new once the false-positive rate is documented as acceptable (<5%).
- Cannot proceed to fail-on-new until the cleanup epic pool-master-rop.71 closes.

## Acceptance (this story)

- Script implementation lands.
- Baseline count recorded.
- CI step exists in warn-only mode.
- Documentation in rules/testing-rules.md §1A so the rule and the gate cross-reference.
- Follow-up story opened for the fail-on-new transition.

## Out of scope

- The actual sweep of existing tests to add IDs (pool-master-rop.10, .21).
- Changes to the §1A rule text (that's story C2).
notes: Closed A4. Added scripts/check-test-traceability.{mjs,sh}, wired npm run rules:check:test-traceability into rules:check and CI as warn-only baseline. Validation: npm run rules:check passed; baseline reported 777 traceability gaps for future cleanup.

title: A3: Add scripts/check-openapi-fresh.sh and wire as required CI gate
description:
**Category:** §A — Automation gate
**Source defects:** pool-master-rop.17 (no CI gate on OpenAPI drift)
**Closes expectation from:** rules/architecture-rules.md §2 Contract-First

## What

Add a dedicated npm run api:check script (separate from npm run lint, which would be too expensive to run this on every invocation):

1. Runs npm run api:refresh into a temporary directory (not the working tree).
2. Diffs the generated openapi.json and packages/shared/generated/hey-api/* against the committed versions.
3. Fails if any diff exists.

Wire api:check into:
- The required local gate set in rules/testing-rules.md §3 (alongside typecheck, eslint, jest, etc.)
- CI workflow as a required blocking step

**Do not bundle into npm run lint** — it's too expensive to run on every lint invocation and would be skipped in practice.

## Why

§2 already says 'OpenAPI generation must be treated as part of the build contract, not optional documentation,' and the slice-completion checklist in workflow-rules.md §1 includes both api:refresh and api:validate. Neither is enforced — see plan 115 §Cause 1 and pool-master-rop.17.

## Acceptance

- npm run api:check exists and exits 0 when generated artifacts are fresh, 1 otherwise.
- Output diff is human-readable so the developer can fix locally and re-run.
- Listed in rules/testing-rules.md §3 Required Local Quality Gates.
- CI runs the check as a blocking step before tests.
- Documentation in package.json describes how to fix a failure (npm run api:refresh && commit).

## Out of scope

- Replacing JsonObjectSchema fields (pool-master-rop.14) — that's contract-first cleanup epic.
notes: Closed A3. Added scripts/check-openapi-fresh.{mjs,sh}, added npm run api:check, made OpenAPI export support OPENAPI_OUTPUT_PATH, wired api:check into CI, refreshed generated OpenAPI/hey-api artifacts. Validation: npm run api:check and npm run api:validate passed.

title: A2: Add scripts/check-route-discipline.sh (the §10 grep set)
description:
**Category:** §A — Automation gate
**Source defects:** pool-master-rop.9, .27, .28, .29
**Closes prose ban from:** rules/service-rules.md §10 Pre-Commit Self-Review

## What

Create scripts/check-route-discipline.sh that runs the grep patterns documented in service-rules.md §10 against packages/core-api/src/modules/*/routes.ts and handler*.ts:

- additionalProperties: true in route schemas
- SuccessSchema on routes returning domain data
- { type: 'object', properties: } inline schemas
- prisma.* in handler/route files
- reply.send(await prisma in handler/route files
- .map(( in handler/route files

**CI is the authoritative gate.** Pre-commit hook integration may follow but is optional. Despite the §10 section title (Pre-Commit Self-Review), enforcement must run in CI.

## Why

§10 already lists these greps verbatim and tells the developer to run them. Nothing automated does. See plan 115 §Cause 1.

## Acceptance

- Script invocation matches the §10 grep set exactly.
- CI invocation checks the full module tree.
- Output names the file:line and the offending pattern for fast triage.
- Land warn-only initially; flip to fail-on-new after pool-master-rop.74 (backend refactor) closes.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring drafts/routes.ts (pool-master-rop.9) and the seven other mapper-less modules (pool-master-rop.35) — that's the cleanup epic.
- Pre-commit hook installation.
notes: Closed A2. Added scripts/check-route-discipline.{mjs,sh}, wired npm run rules:check:route-discipline into rules:check and CI as warn-only baseline. Validation: npm run rules:check passed; baseline reported 112 route-discipline findings.

title: A1: Add scripts/check-no-mocked-api.sh and wire as CI gate
description:
**Category:** §A — Automation gate
**Source defects:** pool-master-rop.4 (30+ tests use vi.mock('@/lib/api'))
**Closes prose ban from:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §7 Banned Test Patterns

## What

Create scripts/check-no-mocked-api.sh that fails on any occurrence of vi.mock('@/lib/api') or vi.mock('@/lib/api-client') in clients/*/src/**.

**CI is the authoritative gate.** Wire the script into the CI workflow as a blocking step. Pre-commit hook integration is optional convenience that can be added separately and must not be treated as a substitute.

## Why

The rule already exists in rules/react-ui-rules.md §7 verbatim:
> vi.mock('@/lib/api-client') or equivalent module-level replacement of the runtime API client for new tests

But ~30 tests violate it because no script enforces it. See plan 115 §Cause 1.

## Acceptance

- Script exists and exits 1 on a hit, 0 otherwise.
- CI step exists and is required for merge.
- Initial run on main is documented (count of existing offenders) so the cleanup epic pool-master-rop.71 can target the count to zero.
- Land as **warn-only on existing offenders** initially; convert to fail-on-new once cleanup of rop.71 begins.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring the existing 30+ offending tests — that's pool-master-rop.4 in the cleanup epic.
- MSW handler scaffolding — also pool-master-rop.4.
- Pre-commit hook installation.
notes: Closed A1. Added scripts/check-no-mocked-api.{mjs,sh}, wired npm run rules:check:no-mocked-api into rules:check and CI as warn-only baseline. Validation: npm run rules:check passed; baseline reported 30 mocked generated API boundary usages.

title: Rule-enforcement hardening (post-review)
description:
Convert PoolMaster's coding/testing/architecture rules from prose-only guidance into automated, enforceable gates. Triggered by the 2026-05-02 cross-stack code review (epic `pool-master-rop`), which surfaced 67 file-anchored defects against rules that already existed in `rules/*.md` but had no detection mechanism.

## Why this exists

The diagnostic finding from `pool-master-rop`: most of the defects (mocked SDK in 30+ tests, 12× duplicated `extractErrorMessage`, 8 modules without mappers, ~120 inline `isRootAdmin` checks, etc.) violate rules that **already exist** in the rule files. The rules describe forbidden patterns; nothing automated catches them.

This epic adds five categories of rule changes:

- **§A. Convert prose bans into automated gates** (8 scripts/lint rules)
- **§B. New rule sections covering uncovered patterns** (10 sections)
- **§C. Tighten escape-hatch language** (4 edits)
- **§D. Periodic codebase-wide review obligation** (1 process addition)
- **§E. Make Riley non-optional and detectable** (1 process change)

Total: 24 reviewable stories. Each story is one rule-file edit, one script, or one ESLint rule — independently reviewable so the user can accept, reject, or refine each one before any are landed.

## Companion plan

Plan: `plans/115-rule-enforcement-hardening.md` — full root-cause analysis, defect-class → rule-gap mapping, worked example of how a defect slipped through, and categorization rationale.

## Order of operations (recommended)

1. **Land §A first** (the automation gates) before working any of the eight thematic cleanup epics under `pool-master-rop.68–.75`. Without the gates, cleanup work just keeps catching up to fresh duplicates.
2. **Then §C** (tightening) — it's prose-only, low-risk, and removes loopholes that the gates rely on.
3. **Then §B** (new rule sections) — these are larger conceptual additions; review individually.
4. **Then §D and §E** (process changes) — these require collaboration discipline more than code change.

## Acceptance

- Every story closes with the rule file or script committed and the relevant CI/lint configuration updated.
- The companion plan is updated as scope changes.
- When all stories close, the plan file is deleted (per `workflow-rules.md §0`); durable rules live in `rules/`.

## Children

See child stories. Each is grouped by category (A1–A8, B1–B10, C1–C4, D1, E1) and cites the source defect(s) from `pool-master-rop` that motivated it.
notes:
Closed 2026-05-03. All 25 child stories closed.

The eight rule-enforcement gates are live in CI (committed in PR #1,
final Riley marker gate in PR #2). Five rule files were updated;
personas/riley.md gained the marker section; .github/pull_request_template.md
gained the marker block. GitHub branch protection on main was configured
during this epic and dogfooded by the merging PRs themselves.

Remaining cleanup: delete plans/115-rule-enforcement-hardening.md per
workflow-rules §0 — durable patterns are codified in the rule files.
Tracking via a small follow-up PR.

title: Test traceability & coverage gaps
description:
Cross-cutting epic to (a) restore the frontend test tier from module-mock to MSW, (b) make every retained test reference a UC/BR/defect ID per testing-rules §1A, and (c) close the negative-path coverage gap on page-level frontend tests.

## Goals
- MSW handlers replace vi.mock('@/lib/api') in all 30+ frontend page tests.
- Hand-shaped DTO fixtures replaced with builders typed against generated SDK responses.
- Every backend describe block references a UC/BR/defect ID.
- Every page test covers loading, query-error, mutation-error rollback, and permission-denied at minimum.
- app.test.tsx-style copy-only tests are either rewritten as router-bootstrap tests or deleted.

## Scope and slicing (measured 2026-05-03)

The traceability sweep alone (rop.10 + rop.21) is 777 violations across 138 unique test files — averaging ~5.6 violations per file because most files have many `it(` blocks under one `describe(` that a single prefix can cover. The 777 number sounds enormous; the actual mechanical work is closer to 30-50 file edits.

**The traceability sweep expands into 12 sub-slices** (7 under rop.10 backend, 5 under rop.21 frontend). Each sub-slice is one directory, one PR, 20-90 minutes of mechanical work. Do not try to bundle them — see each child story for its own slicing breakdown.

The non-traceability work (rop.4 MSW migration, rop.22 negative-path coverage, rop.23 typed fixtures) does not have the same mechanical character. Those are real behavioral changes per test; they need careful per-file review.

## Recommended sequencing

1. **rop.4 — install MSW; replace vi.mock at module level.** Foundational — every behavioral frontend test in subsequent stories depends on this seam being correct.
2. **rop.23 — typed test-data builders against generated SDK shapes.** Once MSW lands, immediately follow with typed fixtures so future test changes don't drift from the SDK.
3. **rop.10 + rop.21 — traceability sweep.** Mostly mechanical (12 slices total per their bodies). Can run in parallel with rop.22 since neither blocks the other.
4. **rop.22 — add error/loading/permission coverage to page tests.** Higher cognitive load; do after MSW + typed fixtures are in place.

## When the traceability gate flips to fail-on-new

Once rop.10 and rop.21 both close (777 → 0 or near-zero), story `pool-master-1y8.4` slice 2 flips `rules:check:test-traceability` from warn-only to fail-on-new. That conversion slice is already documented and is blocked by this epic closing.

## Note

Several CRITICAL/HIGH defects in other epics ship with failing-test-first proofs (e.g., rop.6 in the Sync epic). Those tests are part of those epics, not this one. This epic is about test infrastructure and existing test debt.

## Children
See child defects.

title: Contract-first enforcement (OpenAPI / SDK / DTO drift)
description:
Cross-cutting epic to make contract-first behavior actually enforced, not conventional. Today the rules expect OpenAPI / DTOs / generated SDK / mappers to stay in sync, but nothing fails the build when they drift.

## Goals
- CI gate that fails when committed openapi.json or generated SDK is stale.
- Replace JsonObjectSchema (untyped passthrough) with real DTO shapes.
- Wire the mock-contest-feed-provider's own generated SDK into its consumer (or delete it).
- Replace inline JSON Schema in route bodies with zodToJsonSchema.
- Type the error envelope and validation issue payloads instead of shipping z.unknown().

## Recommended sequencing
1. rop.17 — api:check / api:validate gate in turbo + CI.
2. rop.14 — replace JsonObjectSchema fields with real DTO shapes (LeagueSummaryDto, ContestSummaryDto, audit entry, etc.).
3. rop.16 — consume the mock-feed package's generated SDK in mock-contest-feed-adapter (or delete the unused generated/ directory).
4. rop.28 — replace the inline JSON Schema for /scoring/config/validate with zodToJsonSchema(ScoringConfigSchema).
5. rop.54 — type ScoringConfigValidationIssueSchema and use it in the response.
6. rop.53 — rename ScoreUpdatedEvent.teamId → entryId.

## Children
See child defects.

title: Hand-shaped test fixtures duplicate generated DTO shapes — silent contract drift
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** testing, sdk-bypass · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/root-admin-manage-leagues-page.test.tsx:36-65 (seedLeagues hand-typed)
- features/leagues/leagues-page.test.tsx:73-92
- features/contests/contest-detail-page.test.tsx:99-117 (buildEntry factory)
- features/auth/auth-home-page.test.tsx:70-88 (buildUser)

**Problem:** Each test hand-types DTO objects (leagueRelationship: { commissioner: false }, iconKey: 'TROPHY', createdAt: '2026-04-10...'). If AdminListLeaguesResponses[200]['leagues'][number] adds a required field, only TypeScript will catch it — and only on the test files that import the type. The hand-rolled vi.fn()-returned envelope ({ data: { leagues: [...] } }) is also untyped.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §7 Frontend Test Data Rule: 'Frontend tests must use exported contract shapes from the generated SDK/types. Do not invent alternative test object shapes.'

**Suggested fix:** Define test builders that return values typed as AdminListLeaguesResponses[200]['leagues'][number], run them through Zod schemas where they exist, and have MSW handlers consume them so the request/response wiring still executes.

title: Page-level frontend tests cover only happy paths — error/loading/permission paths missing
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** testing · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx (1,040 lines, 9 tests, all happy path)
- features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx (520 lines, 9 tests, all success-state)

**Problem:** No test asserts what MyTeamPage renders when leagueQuery.isError, when inactivateTeamMutation fails, when the user lacks owner authority but lands on the page, or when an optimistic cache update needs to roll back after a failed mutation.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/testing-rules.md §1 Core Testing Standard: 'every documented negative/error/permission use case must be represented by at least one automated test.' §10 Frontend lists Loading/Error/Empty + Error handling for backend error states as required.

**Suggested fix:** For each page test file, add at minimum: (a) query-error renders the error state, (b) mutation rejection surfaces error copy without losing user input, (c) loading skeleton appears, (d) permission boundary renders read-only / access-denied. Reference UC IDs.

title: app.test.tsx and other frontend tests assert only display copy — no behavior, no UC ID
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** testing · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- clients/poolmaster/src/app.test.tsx:1-15 (entire file: 1 it-block asserting two strings)
- features/app-shell/not-found-page.test.tsx
- features/app-shell/error-boundary.test.tsx
- many ui-primitive tests

**Problem:** app.test.tsx renders <App /> and asserts the marketing string 'run your league...' is in the DOM — there is no behavior under test, no use-case/business-rule/defect ID, no negative path. If the entire auth-home component were replaced with that string in a <div>, the test would still pass.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/testing-rules.md §1A Test Self-Documentation requires every new test to reference a use-case/business-rule/defect ID; §5 Test Proof Rules requires tests to 'prove the behavior they claim to cover, not just that the page renders'.

**Suggested fix:** Either replace app.test.tsx with a real router-bootstrap test (e.g., visiting /welcome while unauthenticated redirects to /) or delete it. Audit the other copy-only tests. Add UC/BR/defect IDs to all retained describe/it blocks.

## Actual scope (measured 2026-05-03 by `scripts/check-test-traceability.mjs`)

**138 violations across the frontend test tree.** Per-directory breakdown:

| Directory | Violations |
|---|---|
| `clients/poolmaster/src/lib/` | 27 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/` | 24 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/` | 13 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/` | 13 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/` | 12 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/` | 12 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/account/` | 12 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/` | 11 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/features/shared/` | 9 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/routes/` | 4 |
| `clients/poolmaster/src/app.test.tsx` | 1 |
