This story has a dual purpose: most violations are pure documentation gaps (mechanical adds), but some files (notably `app.test.tsx`, `not-found-page.test.tsx`, `error-boundary.test.tsx`) are also low-value display-copy-only tests that should be **rewritten or deleted**, not just have a UC ID stamped on top.

## Slicing (revised 2026-05-03)

This story expands into 5 slices. Each slice runs the traceability scanner against its scope, decides per file whether to (a) add the missing reference or (b) rewrite/delete the test if it's a §5-violating display-copy-only test, and watches the global warn-only count drop.

| Slice | Scope | Violations | Estimated effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `clients/poolmaster/src/lib/` | 27 | 30-45 min |
| 2 | `clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/` | 24 | 30-45 min |
| 3 | `clients/poolmaster/src/features/{teams, auth}/` | 26 | 30-45 min |
| 4 | `clients/poolmaster/src/features/{root-admin, contests, account, app-shell}/` | 47 | 60-90 min — includes app-shell display-copy audit |
| 5 | `clients/poolmaster/src/{routes, features/shared, app.test.tsx}` | 14 | 30-60 min — includes the canonical `app.test.tsx` decision (rewrite or delete) |

Each slice should:
1. Run `node scripts/check-test-traceability.mjs --warn-only 2>&1 | grep '<directory>'` to list its targets.
2. **For each test file:** decide whether the test exercises real behavior or is display-copy-only (per `rules/testing-rules.md §5 Test Proof Rules`).
   - **Real behavior** → add a UC/BR/defect ID describe-block prefix or leading comment per §1A.
   - **Display-copy-only** → rewrite as a behavioral test or delete (do not stamp an ID on a low-value test).
3. Re-run the scanner to confirm the count dropped by the expected amount.
4. Run the affected suites to confirm tests still pass.
5. Commit. One slice = one PR.

## Out of scope

- The backend portion of the 777-violation total (covered by `pool-master-rop.10`, 639 violations across `tests/`).
- Adding negative-path test coverage to existing happy-path tests (covered by `pool-master-rop.22`).
- Replacing `vi.mock('@/lib/api', ...)` with MSW (covered by `pool-master-rop.4`).
- The fail-on-new transition for the traceability gate (story `pool-master-1y8.4` slice 2; blocks until both rop.10 and rop.21 close).

## Acceptance

- All 5 slices land.
- `npm run rules:check:test-traceability` shows zero or near-zero frontend findings.
- The display-copy-only tests have been resolved (rewritten or deleted), not papered over with IDs.
- Story `pool-master-1y8.4` slice 2 can proceed (alongside rop.10 closing).

title: useEffect mirrors server query data into form state — overwrite-on-refetch hazard
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** fragility, rules-violation · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:114-129, 131-146
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-entry-page.tsx:257-268 (entryName + tiebreaker drafts)
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:192-211 (teamName)

**Problem:** Three effects sync leagueQuery.data → setDetailsName/setDetailsDescription/setIconDraftKey. If the query refetches while a commissioner is mid-edit (focus, network reconnect, mutation success), their unsaved input is silently overwritten by the server snapshot. Same anti-pattern in contest-entry-page and my-team-page.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §5 Use Effect Only For External Synchronization and 'Do not mirror props into state unless intentionally creating a user-editable draft' — and even then the sync needs an open-flag guard, not blind data-deps.

**Suggested fix:** Open the edit modal/dialog with the current server value seeded as defaultValues in React Hook Form (or a key={league.id + '|' + edit-open} reset), and stop syncing on every leagueQuery.data change.
notes: Progress update before PR: regression tests added for pool-master-rop.20 in league detail, contest entry, and my team page tests. Failing proof observed before production fix: focused Vitest command npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- league-detail-page.test.tsx contest-entry-page.test.tsx my-team-page.test.tsx failed on broken code with league details receiving 'Server Snapshot League' instead of 'Unsaved League Name' and contest entry receiving 'Server Snapshot Entry' instead of 'Unsaved Sunday Charge'. After fix, the same focused command passed (3 files, 26 tests). Required gates run so far: npm run rules:check PASS with existing warn-only baselines; npm run api:check PASS; npx eslint ... PASS; npx turbo typecheck --force PASS after nullable guard; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit PASS; npm run test:poolmaster:unit PASS; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api PASS. Required coverage gate DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:coverage:service:merged is currently blocked by unrelated dirty backend test changes in tests/integration/core-api/contract-verification-root-admin.integration.ts (2 failures around ingestion root-admin auth status expectations). Leaving Beads in_progress until coverage/Riley are clean.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.20 for PR #7. Defect: pool-master-rop.20. Epic: pool-master-rop.73. Files changed: clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx; clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx; clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-entry-page.tsx; clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-entry-page.test.tsx; clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx; clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx; .beads/issues.jsonl. Fix: league detail, contest entry, and team-name drafts are no longer overwritten by same-entity query refetches; league/team edit drafts now reseed on entity identity changes and cannot save a stale draft to the wrong entity. Defect proof: focused PoolMaster tests failed before the production fix with server snapshot values overwriting unsaved drafts, then passed after the fix. Added identity-change regression coverage after Riley review. Gates: focused PoolMaster suite passed (3 files, 28 tests); full PoolMaster unit suite passed (92 files, 283 tests); full turbo typecheck passed; full ESLint passed; git diff --check passed; service functional API and merged service coverage previously passed locally on the clean branch; PR CI was green before the final Beads closeout and is expected to rerun on push. Riley re-review found no findings and confirmed prior blockers resolved. Residual risk: broader MSW/test-fixture debt remains tracked separately under pool-master-rop.71.

title: Twelve duplicate extractErrorMessage implementations despite a shared lib/errors.ts
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** duplication-util · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location (canonical):** clients/poolmaster/src/lib/errors.ts:27

**Duplicates:**
- features/root-admin/root-admin-run-event-sync-page.tsx:31
- features/root-admin/root-admin-manage-leagues-page.tsx:14
- features/root-admin/root-admin-run-sport-sync-page.tsx:27
- features/root-admin/root-admin-sync-dashboard-page.tsx:29
- features/root-admin/root-admin-content-configuration-detail-page.tsx:33
- features/root-admin/root-admin-manage-teams-page.tsx:14
- features/leagues/manage-league-modal.tsx:60
- features/leagues/create-league-modal.tsx:59
- features/contests/contest-entry-page.tsx:46
- features/account/user-page.tsx:47
- features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:59

**Problem:** The header comment of lib/errors.ts already names the cleanup as pool-master-dxd.17, but most pages still ship local copies that drift in default fallback copy and miss codeMessages support.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates the 'extract repeated JS to shared utils' standard; leaves user-facing error copy inconsistent across surfaces.

**Suggested fix:** Replace each local extractErrorMessage with import { extractErrorMessage } from '@/lib/errors', passing { fallback, codeMessages } where domain-specific copy is needed.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.19 in PR #13. Files changed: scripts/check-no-duplicate-extract-error-message.mjs (new); package.json (rules:check chain); 13 frontend feature pages in clients/poolmaster/src/features/* delegated to canonical extractErrorMessage at clients/poolmaster/src/lib/errors.ts. Three drift patterns consolidated: Pattern A wrappers (league-detail-page, my-team-page) replaced with direct canonical calls + per-feature constants (LEAGUE_DETAIL_ERROR_CODE_MESSAGES, TEAM_PAGE_FALLBACK_ERROR); Pattern B (6 root-admin pages) replaced with canonical + { fallback }; Pattern C (5 modal/page sites) replaced with canonical + { fallback: '...' }. Defect proof via rule-enforcement scanner: scripts/check-no-duplicate-extract-error-message.mjs exits 1 with 13 findings on broken tree, exits 0 after fix; now permanent hard-fail CI gate. Note: Beads description said 12 duplicates, actual count was 13 (missed create-contest-page.tsx). Behavior preserved at every call site (fallbacks + codeMessages). Gates: rules:check PASS (incl. new no-duplicate-extract-error-message hard-fail); api:check PASS; eslint --max-warnings 0 PASS; turbo typecheck --force PASS; jest backend (62 suites/592 tests) PASS; test:poolmaster:unit (92 suites/283 tests) PASS; test:service:functional-api PASS. Riley Pass 1 self-check: zero CRITICAL/HIGH; two LOW (scanner exclude pattern hardening + coverage gate documentation). Codex Pass 2: requested change to revert .beads cross-contamination of rop.64; addressed in this fix-up commit. Residual risk: none for this slice; cross-surface error-message inconsistency is product-copy work tracked separately.

title: Zustand session-store duplicates the TanStack Query me-cache (server data in two stores)
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** fragility, rules-violation · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/session-store.ts:31
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-provider.tsx:21-84

**Problem:** The current authenticated user is held in both useQuery(['poolmaster','auth','me']) and useSessionStore, then a useEffect mirrors meQuery.data into the Zustand store. Any consumer can read either source, and they can drift.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §5 URL, Cookie, And Store Ownership: 'Zustand is for client-side UI/application state, not for server data already owned by TanStack Query' and 'Do not duplicate the same state across URL, query cache, and Zustand'. §5 Use Effect Only For External Synchronization also forbids using useEffect to derive render data from query results.

**Suggested fix:** Make the TanStack Query me-cache the single source of truth. Drop the Zustand user/sessionId mirror and the useEffect sync. Expose a stable selector via queryClient.getQueryData(...) for non-React callers.
close_reason: Closed by folded Phase 4 slice rop.78.11. The auth session-store Zustand mirror was deleted, zustand was removed from the active PoolMaster webapp dependency graph, consumers now read/write the TanStack Query auth cache, and docs/rules state that server data must not be mirrored into client stores.

title: OpenAPI regeneration (api:refresh / api:validate) is not enforced by CI or turbo
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- package.json:52-55 (scripts exist)
- turbo.json (no api task)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (no api step)
- .github/pull_request_template.md:45 (asks but does not enforce)

**Problem:** Contract-first relies on the developer remembering to run api:refresh after route/DTO changes. Nothing fails the build if the committed packages/shared/generated/openapi.json and packages/shared/generated/hey-api/* are stale. CI never runs api:validate.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates rules/architecture-rules.md §2: 'OpenAPI generation must be treated as part of the build contract, not optional documentation.' Stale generated SDK surface ships unnoticed.

**Suggested fix:** Add an api:check script that exports OpenAPI to a tmp path, diffs against committed openapi.json, and fails on diff. Wire into turbo build and CI workflow before tests run.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.17 in PR #14. Files changed: package.json (root build and build:service now chain api:check + api:validate after workspace builds; build:poolmaster intentionally remains frontend-only because api:check requires generated Prisma Client); .github/workflows/ci.yml (added api:validate step in lint-typecheck after existing api:check); .beads/issues.jsonl. Defect proof: with corrupted committed openapi.json, npm run build exited 0 before this slice and now exits 1 with 'Generated API artifacts are stale. Run npm run api:refresh.' Clean npm run build passes with 146/146 priority operations validated. Codex Pass 2 initially found build:poolmaster broke CI because poolmaster-build does not run prisma generate; fixed in c5435d2e by removing api gates from build:poolmaster while preserving protection in npm run build, build:service, and required lint-typecheck CI. Gates reported/verified: rules:check PASS; api:check PASS; api:validate PASS; turbo typecheck PASS; eslint PASS; backend jest PASS; PoolMaster unit PASS; service functional API PASS; re-review verified build:poolmaster PASS, api:validate PASS, prisma generate + api:check PASS. Residual risk: direct npx turbo run build still bypasses root API gates by design; required documented path is npm run build and CI lint-typecheck.

title: mock-contest-feed-provider exposes its own SDK that no consumer uses (hand-rolled interfaces)
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/openapi-ts.config.ts
- packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/generated/hey-api/* (unused)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/mock-contest-feed-adapter.ts:355 (raw fetch)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/mock-contest-feed-adapter.ts:17-124 (hand-rolled interfaces ScenarioSummaryResponse, EventListResponse, EventDetailResponse, FeedSnapshotResponse, ContestantRecord, ContestantDelta)

**Problem:** The mock-feed package generates a hey-api SDK and the contracts are duplicated as hand-rolled local interfaces inside mock-contest-feed-adapter.ts. Provider drift is invisible to TypeScript and to CI — the SDK is generated but not imported anywhere.

**Why it's wrong:** The mock provider is supposed to be the seam between 'mock provider' and 'production code', but the seam is asserted with hand-typed interfaces rather than the generated contract. Violates rules/architecture-rules.md §2 Contract-First.

**Suggested fix:** Have mock-contest-feed-adapter.ts consume @poolmaster/mock-contest-feed-provider/generated/hey-api and delete the local interface duplicates. Add the package to packages/core-api/package.json as a workspace dep. If the mock provider is intentionally HTTP-only, delete its openapi-ts.config.ts and generated/ directory.
notes:
Reserved 2026-05-04 by Claude session — picking up sequentially after pool-master-rop.14 (which follows pool-master-rop.17 PR #14). Sequence: rop.14 → rop.16 → rop.10 batch. Codex should continue with frontend slices and not claim these in parallel; this avoids the .beads/embeddeddolt concurrency hazard documented in workflow-rules.md §1 and contention on packages/shared/generated/* during rop.14's regen.
2026-05-04: in scope of new umbrella epic pool-master-rop.78 (League / Contest / Scoring substrate redesign). The mock-feed-provider hand-rolled SDK is part of the broader provider-integration substrate that the audit (rop.78.1) will examine. RESERVATION REVISED: do NOT pick this up as an independent slice — it should be addressed as a Phase 4 slice in pool-master-rop.78 after the design phase decides the canonical provider-normalization shape.

title: No FAPI/integration test for the mock-feed → participant → entry-score data flow
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** testing-architecture · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** tests/functional/*.functional.ts (no ingestion.functional.ts or scoring.functional.ts); tests/integration/core-api/ingestion-persistence.integration.ts covers persistence only.

**Problem:** The 'mock-feed → ingestion → participant upsert → stat event → contest recalc → entry score' path is the most architecturally critical flow in the system. There is no test that walks this end-to-end through the real adapter, real ingestion callbacks, real event bus, and real ContestScoringRecalculationService. Unit tests cover layers in isolation; integration tests cover persistence; FAPI suites cover league/contest/auth journeys but not scoring updates.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates rules/testing-rules.md §6 Required Use-Case Coverage Mapping and §9 Integration Test Depth Requirement. The most likely place for silent breakage has the weakest end-to-end proof.

**Suggested fix:** Add a FAPI test that: spins up the mock-feed provider in-process (or against canned scenarios), seeds a contest with entries and roster picks, triggers POST /api/v1/ingestion/scores/..., then asserts updated ContestEntry.totalScore and standingsPosition. Cover at least one happy path, one partial-data path (some participants without picks), one provider-down path.

title: Several response DTOs leak JsonObjectSchema (untyped passthrough) into the API contract
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:292,332,334,347 (LeagueAuditEntryDtoSchema, LeagueDashboardResponseSchema.league/contests, LeagueBulkOperationResponseSchema)
- packages/shared/dto/contests.dto.ts:220 (latestPerformance)

**Problem:** These DTOs declare the response shape as z.record(z.unknown()) ('arbitrary JSON object'). The OpenAPI export and generated SDK lose all field-level typing for those payloads. Frontend code accessing dashboard.league.name has no compile-time guarantee. This is a hand-rolled contract escape hatch.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates rules/architecture-rules.md §2 Contract-First ('Backend routes must describe real request and response payloads') and rules/model-change-rules.md. The fields are shaped — the backend just hasn't typed them.

**Suggested fix:** Replace each JsonObjectSchema field with the actual response schema (e.g., LeagueSummaryDtoSchema, ContestSummaryDtoSchema, a real audit-entry schema). If genuinely opaque, document why in the schema description.
notes: Split 2026-05-04 into 5 child defects (rop.14.1 — rop.14.5) after investigation revealed the 4 dashboard / bulk-op / latestPerformance / beforeState sites are symptomatic of broader League/Contest contract architecture issues recurring across rop.14, rop.18, rop.20, rop.62, rop.63, rop.7, rop.8, rop.11, rop.12. Per user direction (2026-05-04): the architectural pieces need investigate → design → review → implement. Only rop.14.1 (LeagueAuditEntry typing) is independently tractable; rop.14.2 — rop.14.5 should block on the design phase. See child stories.

title: scheduled-event-reader returns wrong status filter for EVENTPARTICIPANTS (pre-event hydration broken)
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** integration (defect) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/scheduled-event-reader.ts:38-45 (called from ingestion-scheduler.ts:425)

**Problem:** The if/else reads 'if EVENTRESULTS use COMPLETED/OFFICIAL, else use IN_PROGRESS.' But the scheduler also asks this reader for EVENTPARTICIPANTS. Result: participant sync only fires when the event is already IN_PROGRESS, which is after the field-lock window. Pre-event participant hydration is broken via this reader.

**Why it's wrong:** Field hydration must happen before lock for picks to work; this filter delays participant fetches until the event has already started.

**Suggested fix:** Treat EVENTPARTICIPANTS as a separate case using SCHEDULED (and IN_PROGRESS for late hydration) within a configurable lookahead window from the event's startDate/releaseAt.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: add a failing unit regression in tests/unit/core-api/scheduled-event-reader.test.ts proving EVENTPARTICIPANTS includes pre-event scheduled events, then update packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/scheduled-event-reader.ts to treat EVENTPARTICIPANTS separately from live scores/results. Validation: focused unit test first, then required backend gates.
close_reason: Fixed scheduled participant hydration event selection. Scheduled field sync now passes the configured lookahead window into the scheduled-event reader, and EVENTPARTICIPANTS selects scheduled events inside that window plus in-progress events for late hydration. Updated tests/unit/core-api/scheduled-event-reader.test.ts with pool-master-rop.13 regression coverage and tests/unit/core-api/ingestion-scheduler.test.ts for scheduler wiring. Red proof: focused scheduled-event-reader test failed before implementation because from/to were not accepted and the reader could not express scheduled-window participant selection. Green proof: focused ingestion reader/scheduler tests, npx turbo typecheck --force, root eslint, root jest, functional API tests, PoolMaster unit tests, and service merged coverage all passed.

title: Stat events trigger contest-wide recalculation per event with no dedup or backpressure
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** integration (idempotency / backpressure) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/consumer/stat-event-consumer.ts:86-117
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/score-publisher.ts:13-33

**Problem:** Each ProviderStatEvent published becomes a stat.received event; the consumer fans out to every active contest the participant is in, and runs recalculateContest() per contest per event. A single live-score sync (pollLiveScores) emits N stat events for N participants → for a single contest of size N entries × R picks, that's potentially N×R recalculations of the same contest within the same batch. No coalescing, no debouncing, no per-contest mutex. Failures inside one handler are propagated synchronously by Promise.all in EventBus.publish.

**Why it's wrong:** No protection against thrashing. Each ingestion sweep produces O(N²) wasted work and amplifies the concurrency hazard in B-5.

**Suggested fix:** Replace per-event recalc with a per-contest debounced job (collect affected contestIds across a short window, then recalc each affected contest once). Or move the recalc to the onLiveScores ingestion callback boundary so it runs once per ingestion sweep.

title: Contest-level scoring recalculation lacks an outer transaction (partial-failure inconsistency)
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** integration (atomicity) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/contest-scoring/contest-scoring-recalculation-service.ts:164-185

**Problem:** recalculateContest() loops over all entries and calls entryScoringResultService.replaceEntryScoringResult() per entry. Each per-entry call is transactional, but the outer loop is not. A failure on entry N leaves entries 1..N-1 with new scores/positions and N..end with old scores/positions — partially correct standings with no rollback signal to the caller. Standings displayed to users will be a mix of two recalcs.

**Why it's wrong:** Silent inconsistency that the caller does not know about. recalculateContest returns 'changes' based on the post-loop state but the throw stops the iteration.

**Suggested fix:** Wrap the per-entry loop in a single prisma.$transaction((tx) => ...) so the recalc is all-or-nothing. Pass tx down through ContestEntryScoringResultService. Alternatively, return a structured partial-failure result and document the semantics.

title: Backend tests broadly missing UC/BR/defect IDs (testing-rules §1A)
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** testing · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** ~30 backend test files, sampled:
- tests/unit/core-api/scoring-service.test.ts
- tests/unit/core-api/contest-scoring-recalculation-service.test.ts
- tests/unit/core-api/contest-entry-scoring-result-service.test.ts
- tests/integration/core-api/scoring-read.integration.ts
- tests/integration/core-api/contest-scoring-recalculate.integration.ts
- tests/functional/leagues.functional.ts
- tests/functional/auth.functional.ts

**Problem:** No describe block, test name, or leading comment references a use-case, business-rule, or defect ID across the sampled suites.

**Why it's wrong:** Direct violation of testing-rules §1A: 'Every new test must reference one of: a documented use-case ID, a documented business rule ID, or a defect ID for regression tests.' AGENTS.md Non-Negotiables makes this merge-blocking.

## Actual scope (measured 2026-05-03 by `scripts/check-test-traceability.mjs`)

**639 violations across the backend test tree.** Per-directory breakdown:

| Directory | Violations |
|---|---|
| `tests/unit/core-api/` | 269 |
| `tests/unit/scoring-service/` | 123 |
| `tests/unit/draft-service/` | 113 |
| `tests/functional/` | 58 |
| `tests/integration/core-api/` | 46 |
| `tests/unit/shared/` | 25 |
| `packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/` tests | 5 |

The 639 number is misleading — most files have 5-10 `it(` blocks under one `describe(`, and a single describe-block prefix covers all of them. The actual work is ~30-50 test files with mechanical edits.

## Slicing (revised 2026-05-03)

This story expands into 7 slices. Each slice runs the traceability scanner against its scope, adds the missing `UC-`/`BR-`/`pool-master-` references (typically as describe-block prefixes), and watches the global warn-only count drop. **Do not attempt as a single PR — 639 mechanical edits across 30+ files is unreviewable.**

| Slice | Scope | Violations | Estimated effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `tests/unit/scoring-service/` | 123 | 60-90 min |
| 2 | `tests/unit/draft-service/` | 113 | 60-90 min |
| 3a | `tests/unit/core-api/` — auth, leagues, contests, squads | ~135 | 60-90 min |
| 3b | `tests/unit/core-api/` — everything else | ~134 | 60-90 min |
| 4 | `tests/integration/core-api/` | 46 | 30-60 min |
| 5 | `tests/functional/` | 58 | 30-60 min |
| 6 | `tests/unit/shared/` | 25 | 20-30 min |
| 7 | `packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/` tests | 5 | 10-20 min |

Each slice should:
1. Run `node scripts/check-test-traceability.mjs --warn-only 2>&1 | grep '<directory>'` to list its targets.
2. Add the describe-block prefix or leading comment per `rules/testing-rules.md §1A`. Reference the relevant UC/BR/defect ID — if unsure, use `// rule: ...` for genuinely infra-only tests (acceptable per §1A even after C2 lands, since these directories are the target).
3. Re-run the scanner to confirm the count dropped by the expected amount.
4. Run the affected suites to confirm tests still pass (no behavior change).
5. Commit. One slice = one PR.

## Out of scope

- Adding new test cases or assertions (this is documentation only).
- The frontend portion of the 777-violation total (covered by `pool-master-rop.21`, 138 violations across `clients/poolmaster/src/`).
- The fail-on-new transition for the traceability gate (story `pool-master-1y8.4` slice 2; blocks until both rop.10 and rop.21 close).

## Acceptance

- All 7 slices land.
- `npm run rules:check:test-traceability` shows zero or near-zero backend findings.
- Story `pool-master-1y8.4` slice 2 can proceed (alongside rop.21 closing).
notes: Reserved 2026-05-04 by Claude session — picking up sequentially after pool-master-rop.14 (which follows pool-master-rop.17 PR #14). Sequence: rop.14 → rop.16 → rop.10 batch. Codex should continue with frontend slices and not claim these in parallel; this avoids the .beads/embeddeddolt concurrency hazard documented in workflow-rules.md §1 and contention on packages/shared/generated/* during rop.14's regen.

title: drafts/routes.ts: 1535 lines with direct prisma access and no service/mapper layer
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** service-rules · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/drafts/routes.ts (1535 lines; direct prisma calls at lines 185, 305, 325-367, 641, 1083-1215, etc.)

**Problem:** Routes file is 1535 lines, contains direct prisma.contest.findUnique, prisma.rosterPick.findMany/create/delete, prisma.contestConfiguration.findUnique, and inline .map(...) shaping that builds DTO-shaped responses. There is no service.ts, handler.ts, or drafts.mapper.ts.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates service-rules §3 (one domain per module, route → handler → service → mapper), §4 (mapper file requirement), §5 (keep persistence concerns out of handlers), and §10 (forbidden patterns: prisma.* in routes, .map(( in routes).

**Suggested fix:** Carve a DraftService and drafts.mapper.ts; pull all Prisma access into the service via repositories. Move inline response shapers into the mapper.

## Slicing (revised 2026-05-02 per reviewer feedback)

This story expands into a sequence of slices, each its own commit/PR. Each slice must keep all existing tests green; the route file shrinks one route at a time. **Do not open a single PR for the whole refactor — it cannot be reviewed honestly.**

1. **Scaffold slice.**
   - Create empty `packages/core-api/src/modules/drafts/service.ts` (`DraftService` class skeleton).
   - Create empty `packages/core-api/src/mappers/drafts.mapper.ts` (no exported functions yet).
   - Wire `DraftService` instance into the route file's setup (constructor injection from index.ts).
   - No behavior change. Tests still pass.

2. **Per-route migration slices** (one slice per route — approximately 10 routes total in the file). For each route in `drafts/routes.ts`:
   - Move the handler logic into a method on `DraftService`.
   - Move the route's inline `.map(( … ))` shaping into `drafts.mapper.ts` as a named function (e.g., `mapDraftSessionToDto`).
   - Move `prisma.*` calls into the service (or into a thin repository if the call is reused across methods).
   - Replace the route handler body with a thin call into the service + mapper.
   - Run `npm run api:check` and the relevant FAPI tests for that route.
   - Commit. One route migrated per commit.

3. **Error envelope unification slice.** Delete the parallel `{ error, message }` flat shape used internally; route everything through `createErrorEnvelope`. This closes rop.29 in passing. May also fold into individual route slices when natural.

4. **Cleanup slice.** Confirm:
   - `grep -n "prisma\\." packages/core-api/src/modules/drafts/routes.ts` returns zero hits.
   - `grep -n "\\.map((" packages/core-api/src/modules/drafts/routes.ts` returns zero hits.
   - The §10 grep allowlist (when story `pool-master-1y8.2` lands) does not need a drafts exemption.

**Estimated total: ~12 slices over the lifetime of this story.** Each slice is reviewable in 30-45 minutes; the whole refactor is unreviewable as one PR.

## Sequencing within the parent epic

Per the parent epic's slicing guidance (rop.74), this story is intended to be **the last large refactor in the epic**, so the migration pattern is well-established by then via rop.27 (smallest first) and rop.35 (one mapper-less module at a time).

## Out of scope for this story

- The pattern itself — that's established by the smaller migrations under rop.27 and rop.35.
- Deletion of the §10 grep allowlist exemption — that's covered by the cleanup slice above.

title: replaceEntryScoringResult is a delete-then-create transaction with concurrency hazard
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/contest-scoring/contest-entry-scoring-result-service.ts:25-130
- called from packages/core-api/src/modules/contest-scoring/contest-scoring-recalculation-service.ts:177-184

**Problem:** For each entry, the service deletes all contestEntryParticipantScoreEvent + contestEntryParticipantScore + contestEntryPrizeAward rows, then recreates them. Two concurrent stat events for the same contest each trigger a full recalculateContest that calls this for every entry. With EventBus.publish running handlers via Promise.all and no per-contest serialization or row-level locking, concurrent recalculations race: one transaction's delete can race the other's create, leaving partial or duplicated state. Default Postgres isolation (READ COMMITTED) doesn't prevent this.

**Why it's wrong:** No idempotency or sequence control on the score-update pipeline. Architecture-rules §4 demands explicit isolation; here entry-level isolation per concurrent recalculation isn't.

**Suggested fix:** Serialize per-contest recalculation (advisory lock or a contest-scoped queue), or replace delete-then-recreate with an idempotent upsert keyed by (entryId, rosterPickId, scoringRuleId). Add a defect-driven integration test that pumps two stat events concurrently and asserts the final ledger is consistent.

title: StandingsRollup periodic loop is dead code; duplicate rank assignment with recalc service
description:
**Severity:** HIGH · **Category:** integration / duplication · **Reviewers:** Brad (B-2) + Archie (A-9)

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/rollup/standings-rollup.ts:51-130
- packages/core-api/src/index.ts:256 (start)

**Problem:** StandingsRollup.startPeriodicRollup() is started, but registerContest() is never called from any production code path (confirmed by repo-wide grep). rollupAll iterates activeContestIds, which stays empty forever. The 30-second interval is a no-op. The exposed ScoringHealthResponse (rollupRunning, activeContests) silently lies. Manual triggerRollup works, but it duplicates rank assignment already done by ContestScoringRecalculationService.recalculateContest — two assignRanks implementations, two paths writing ContestEntry.standingsPosition.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §1 ban on no-op service surfaces. ScoringHealthResponse advertises a feature that is wired but dead. Duplicate orchestration creates inconsistent updates to the same row at different times.

**Suggested fix:** Either delete the periodic-rollup machinery in favor of the event-driven recalc flow, or wire registerContest/unregisterContest to contest lifecycle transitions and unify rank assignment via one shared assignRanks helper. Update ScoringHealthResponseSchema to reflect what is actually running.

title: Cross-stack code & architecture review (2026-05-02)
description:
Cross-stack code & architecture review of PoolMaster (backend, frontend, integration) conducted on 2026-05-02 by the Brad/Fran/Archie persona team. This epic collects every concrete file-anchored defect surfaced during the review.

## Scope of review

- packages/core-api (Fastify routes, services, prisma access, mappers)
- packages/shared (DTOs, OpenAPI generation, generated SDK, domain types, mappers, events)
- packages/mock-contest-feed-provider (boundary & integration handoff)
- clients/poolmaster (full React/Vite app + tests)
- cross-stack integration (mock-feed → ingestion → participant → entry score recalc)

## Headline findings (CRITICAL)

1. **Mock-feed is the only allowed sport-data provider in production.** `provider-bindings.ts` throws unless the provider id is `mock-contest-feed`; four real adapters are exported but unreachable. This is mock-data-in-prod via configuration, even though the mock service itself is a clean external boundary.
2. **Ingestion mutating routes are unauthenticated** (no `adminAuth`). Any authenticated user can trigger sport-wide ingestion, score polling, and result fetch.
3. **JWT secret falls back to a hardcoded dev string in three places.** If `JWT_SECRET` is unset in any deployed environment, JWTs are signable by anyone with the repo.
4. **Frontend test tier is collapsed onto module mocks.** ~30 page tests use `vi.mock('@/lib/api', ...)` instead of MSW; the request/response/auth-retry/CSRF/trace-id wiring is never exercised. Banned by `rules/react-ui-rules.md §7`.
5. **Live score ingestion does not actually feed contest scoring.** `publishStatEvents` emits events; `ContestScoringRecalculationService` reads `SportEventParticipantSourceData` written exclusively by participant-sync; the two paths never converge.
6. **`ScoringService.getEntryScore` returns a fabricated zero-score object for missing entries** — synthetic-fallback in production code, locked in by a unit test that asserts the wrong behavior.

## Recurring themes

- The ingestion/scoring backbone is the highest-risk surface and the least defended (no auth, no atomicity at the contest level, no end-to-end FAPI test, dead `score.updated` events, dead `StandingsRollup` periodic loop, no dedup/backpressure on stat events).
- Contract-first is conventional, not enforced (no CI gate on OpenAPI drift, multiple DTOs leak `JsonObjectSchema`, the mock-provider's own SDK is unused, pagination is per-feature).
- Authorization is partially centralized: `requireCommissioner`/`requireLeagueMembership` exist, but ~120 inline `request.authUser?.isRootAdmin === true` checks dominate handler volume.
- Local copies of helpers and components in the frontend (`extractErrorMessage` 12×, `formatRelativeToPar` 2×, `formatDateTimeDisplay` 2×, raw `<button>` JSX bypassing the shared `Button`, raw `<input>` bypassing `FormField`).
- Page-level tests cover happy paths only; loading, query-error, mutation-error rollback, and permission-denied paths are absent. Test self-documentation (UC/BR/defect IDs per `rules/testing-rules.md §1A`) is broadly missing across both backend and frontend.
- Several modules ship without mapper files and shape DTOs inline in routes (drafts is the worst case at 1535 LOC with direct prisma access).

## Severity rollup

- CRITICAL: 6
- HIGH: 18
- MEDIUM: 29
- LOW: 14
- Total: 67 file-anchored defects (children of this epic).

## How to execute

Each child is a stand-alone defect with location, problem, rule violated, and suggested fix. Treat related defects as one slice where the underlying change is shared (e.g., the synthetic zero-score and the test that locks it in must ship together as a defect-fix slice with a failing-test-first proof per `rules/testing-rules.md §3`). Do not weaken tests to keep current behavior green — the rules explicitly forbid that.

Reviewers: Brad (backend), Fran (frontend), Archie (architecture/integration).

title: Coverage report fails when integration tests require SMTP
description: The service coverage report CI lane runs integration coverage without an SMTP sink. Invite-by-email endpoints now perform real mail delivery, so integration tests receive 502 provider failures instead of expected 201 responses.
acceptance_criteria: Service merged coverage passes in local and CI environments without requiring a developer-managed SMTP daemon.
notes: Starting fix: reproduce/investigate coverage failure and add a test-owned SMTP sink for integration suites.
close_reason: Fixed by adding a shared test SMTP sink used by integration and functional API coverage harnesses. Verified focused failing contract suite and full npm run test:coverage:service:merged pass locally.

title: Create standard server error display bar
description: Create a standard user-facing server error display pattern for API failures. It should show helpful user-safe messages, support details/debug metadata when appropriate, and avoid exposing raw GUID-heavy backend errors as the primary user message.
acceptance_criteria: Shared ServerErrorBar or ServerErrorPanel component exists with tests. It accepts generated-client/API errors and renders a user-safe message, optional retry action, optional support/debug details, and accessible alert semantics. At least two pages or modals migrate from inline/raw error rendering to the shared server-error display.
notes: Starting first P1 implementation batch: shared server-error display, async query-state page wrapper, and collection/DataGrid page template. These are foundational for later mutation/modal/detail refactors.
close_reason: Implemented shared ServerErrorBar/ServerErrorPanel with generated-client error message extraction, retry action support, optional debug details, accessible alert semantics, focused tests, and migrated two admin list pages to the standard server error display.

title: Enforce semantic theme token usage in feature code
description: Add automated checks that prevent raw Tailwind color scale classes or raw brand colors in feature code outside the shared theme/primitives layer.
acceptance_criteria: A lint/test/script check fails when feature TSX/CSS uses disallowed raw color classes or literal brand colors. Shared primitives/theme files remain allowed where appropriate. Existing violations are either migrated or explicitly allowlisted with justification.
close_reason: Added lint:theme-tokens guardrail that scans feature code outside shared UI for raw Tailwind color scale classes and literal color values. Removed remaining raw feature color classes by deleting unused sync status class helpers and moving team icon themes to semantic CSS variables/classes. Validation: lint:theme-tokens, root lint, focused team/join/admin tests, turbo typecheck, and PoolMaster unit suite passed.

title: Create shared collection page and DataGrid page template
description: Create a reusable collection/list page template for pages whose main surface is a shared DataGrid or row-list: header, actions, grid tile, filters, empty state, row navigation, and optional create action.
acceptance_criteria: CollectionPage/DataGridPage component exists with tests. At least two root-admin manage/list pages migrate. The template preserves existing DataGrid sort/filter behavior and keeps feature-specific columns owned by each feature.
notes: Starting first P1 implementation batch: shared server-error display, async query-state page wrapper, and collection/DataGrid page template. These are foundational for later mutation/modal/detail refactors.
close_reason: Implemented explicit DataGridPage/CollectionPage templates on top of the shared async page shell and migrated root-admin users plus content configuration list pages from the legacy ManagementListPage name.

title: Create shared mutation action workflow pattern

title: Inventory and introduce shared PoolMaster UI primitives before theme adoption
description: Create a focused inventory of repeated PoolMaster web UI patterns and introduce the highest-value shared React primitives before adopting the Sunday Prime theme. Scope includes reusable surfaces/tiles, buttons/action rows, modal shell, form fields, status badges, data grid/table styling, page headers, empty/loading/error states, and selectors/dropdowns. Feature pages should migrate incrementally to semantic primitives so theme values remain centralized and feature code does not own raw styling decisions.
notes: Inventory from current PoolMaster webapp scan: 1) Surface/Tile/Card primitive: repeated rounded-[2rem]/border/bg-card containers across league detail, team detail, contests, account, root-admin, route guards. 2) Button/ActionLink primitive: repeated primary, secondary, ghost, destructive, disabled, icon-only, and link-button classes across app shell, contests, team/league/account modals, admin pages. 3) Modal/Dialog primitive: repeated Radix dialog shell/overlay/content/footer patterns in league, team, account, icon picker flows; needs consistent sizing, scroll behavior, focus, close actions. 4) FormField/Input/Select/Textarea primitive: repeated label/input/select/textarea styling across auth, create contest, league/team/account modals, root-admin config/sync pages. 5) StatusBadge/Chip primitive: repeated uppercase rounded badges for active/inactive/open/live/locked/complete/failed/success and lifecycle states; should use semantic tones only. 6) PageHeader/BreadcrumbHeader primitive: repeated admin and feature page title tiles, breadcrumbs, eyebrow labels, loading/error copy; should standardize title, optional crumb, optional actions. 7) DataGrid/Table primitive: AdminDataGrid exists, but table/filter/header/status styling should become theme-aware and reusable beyond root-admin, including sync history and contest/history lists when table-like. 8) Empty/Loading/ErrorState primitive: repeated rounded cards with muted text for loading, missing league, failed loads, empty list states. 9) DefinitionList/MetricGrid primitive: repeated small summary tiles for members, active contests, tier progress, contest rules, sync stats. 10) Selector/Dropdown/Menu primitive: league selector, account menu, app nav dropdowns, filters, and action menus repeat behavior/styling and should share menu surface/item/focus patterns. Priority sequence: implement primitives for Tile, Button, Modal, FormField, StatusBadge, PageHeader, EmptyState, MetricGrid, then theme-aware DataGrid/Menu primitives; migrate pages incrementally, starting with AppShell, profile/league/team details, admin grids, contest pages.
