title: EventBus.publish uses Promise.all — first handler error rejects whole publish
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/shared/events/event-bus.ts:19-22

**Problem:** await Promise.all(handlers.map((h) => h(event))). If a single subscriber throws (or rejects), the publish promise rejects and the publisher (e.g., score-publisher.ts:31) propagates the error up to the ingestion callback, marking the whole job FAILED. Other subscribers may have already run partially, others not at all.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates rules/architecture-rules.md §4 Domain Event Bus: 'Subscribers must not assume emission order or delivery guarantees beyond at least once.' Today, a misbehaving subscriber takes the publisher down, no isolation per subscriber.

**Suggested fix:** Use Promise.allSettled; log per-handler failures; do not propagate. Add a metric/log for handler failure rate. Consider a per-subscriber timeout.

title: Root-admin checks scattered across handlers (~120 occurrences) instead of centralized authority
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** duplication (auth) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** Repo-wide: 'request.authUser?.isRootAdmin === true' appears 120 times across handlers (sampled: leagues/handler.ts:59,72,76,123,154,218; squads/handler.ts:26,54,133,165,191).

**Problem:** Two distinct patterns coexist: requireCommissioner/requireLeagueMembership preHandlers (good — leagues/permissions.ts), and ad-hoc 'request.authUser?.isRootAdmin === true' checks inline in handlers to 'view as root admin'. The inline pattern means every handler that returns league-scoped data has to remember to fork its query and DTO mapping by viewer authority.

**Why it's wrong:** High-risk repetition: one missed handler leaks data or denies legitimate root-admin access. service-rules expects authority centralized at the route boundary or in service inputs.

**Suggested fix:** Introduce a viewerAuthority resolver (member, commissioner, root-admin) computed once per request in a preHandler and consumed by handlers/services as a typed enum. Centralize 'scope-relaxing' rules so adding a new admin-visible field doesn't require touching 12 handlers.

title: List endpoints inconsistently paginate (admin paginated; leagues/contests/squads return raw arrays)
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM (kept as the umbrella framing for epic pool-master-rop.77) · **Category:** contract-first · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- Paginated (uses `PaginatedSchema`): `packages/shared/dto/admin.dto.ts:44,343`, `packages/shared/dto/standings.dto.ts:11,38`
- Not paginated (raw arrays): `packages/shared/dto/leagues.dto.ts:302,307`; `contests.dto.ts:268`; `squads.dto.ts:79`

**Original problem (filed 2026-05-02):** Admin listings use `PaginatedSchema` (page/pageSize/total/totalPages). League, contest, squad, member, notification listings return `z.array(...)` with no pagination envelope. Cross-cutting inconsistency.

**Direction (revised 2026-05-02 per project owner):** PoolMaster's data scale does not justify pagination anywhere. Eliminate it from the existing endpoints rather than standardize on it. The non-paginated endpoints' raw-array shape is the right pattern; admin and standings are the outliers.

**Why it's wrong:** Two inconsistent envelope shapes for the same kind of payload. Future endpoints have no clear precedent. Frontend has to special-case each list shape.

**Suggested fix:** See child stories of epic pool-master-rop.77 for the migration plan. This story is kept as the umbrella defect; concrete migration work is split per endpoint.

**Cross-references:**
- Story `pool-master-1y8.13` (B5) — the rule change that codifies the no-pagination direction in `service-rules.md`.

title: isPublicRoute matches by literal path string — token-prefix routes mis-classified silently
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility (auth) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/plugins/auth-guard.ts:41-67

**Problem:** PUBLIC_ROUTES is a Set<string> keyed on exact 'METHOD path' strings, with regex helpers for token-style paths. It is hand-maintained alongside route registrations elsewhere. There's no compile-time link between this set and route registration, so a renamed/removed auth route silently becomes 'private and broken' or 'still public after removal'. path.startsWith('/health') would also match a route named '/healthcheck-fake'.

**Why it's wrong:** Cross-cutting auth fragility — duplication of routing knowledge in two places.

**Suggested fix:** Drive public-route classification from a route metadata flag (e.g., schema.public: true) and read it from Fastify's route registration. Replace startsWith('/health') with an exact match.

title: Operational timing computes 'X days prior at HH:MM' in UTC, not in event/league timezone
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility (time-zone) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/events/operational-timing.ts:120-149

**Problem:** applyRelativeRule uses setUTCDate and setUTCHours on the event's start date. A timing policy of '1 day prior at 12:00' resolves to 12:00 UTC = 04:00 PT / 13:00 CET. There is no conversion from league/user timezone (User.timezone exists but is not consulted). Field-lock and release times will land at confusing local times for non-UTC commissioners and members.

**Why it's wrong:** Cross-cutting time-zone fragility; user-facing scheduling uses UTC math only.

**Suggested fix:** Make the timing policy carry an IANA timezone (or resolve to the league's timezone) and compute the wall-clock offset there before converting back to UTC for storage.

title: Participant.status uses workflow-style enum for soft-delete lifecycle (should be isActive)
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** rules-violation · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/prisma/schema.prisma:530 (Participant.status: PrismaParticipantStatus @default(ACTIVE))
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/persistence/ingestion-persistence.ts:435,464,549,553

**Problem:** Participant.status encodes an active/inactive distinction. Per rules/domain-model-conventions-rules.md §2, status is reserved for workflow/business-state and isActive: boolean is the convention for soft-delete-style lifecycle. Other entities (League, Squad, LeagueSquad, User) use isActive correctly — Participant is the outlier.

**Why it's wrong:** Direct violation of documented domain-model convention; per-entity divergence the rules file warns against.

**Suggested fix:** Migrate Participant.status to isActive: boolean (with a deprecation/migration story for the enum), or document the workflow distinction in the schema if Participant truly needs more than active/inactive (e.g., RETIRED, BANNED).

title: score.updated events are emitted but have no subscribers (dead event surface)
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** integration (dead code) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/consumer/stat-event-consumer.ts:103-114 emits; no eventBus.subscribe<...>('score.updated', ...) exists in packages/core-api/src or packages/shared.

**Problem:** Every recalculated entry produces a score.updated event with timestamps and old/new ranks. Nothing listens. Wasted work and incorrect documentation in stat-event-consumer.ts:7 ('Publishes score.updated events for changed entries').

**Why it's wrong:** Misleading event-bus surface. Future subscribers will subscribe expecting all the fields populated, but emission semantics may have rotted. Violates 'documentation describes the codebase that exists' in rules/architecture-rules.md §6.

**Suggested fix:** Delete the unsubscribed emission, or add an actual subscriber (e.g., a notification service driven by rank changes).

title: Eight modules registering routes without a corresponding mapper file
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** service-rules · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/{admin,client-logs,drafts,history,ingestion,invitations,scoring,team-invitations}/ — none have a <module>.mapper.ts under packages/core-api/src/mappers/.

**Problem:** Service-rules §4 mandates a dedicated mapper file per module that registers Fastify routes (config and health excepted). Eight modules violate this. Several (drafts is the worst — see B-9) shape DTOs inline in routes/handlers.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §4: 'Inline .map() transformations in route handlers or handler files are not acceptable as a substitute for a dedicated mapper.'

**Suggested fix:** Carve a mapper per module. Move all .map((entity) => ({ … })) shaping out of handlers/routes.

title: Adapter healthCheck and several catches swallow errors with empty catch blocks
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/odds-api-adapter.ts:210-218
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/openf1-adapter.ts:189
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/pga-tour-adapter.ts:269
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/espn-adapter.ts:211
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/ingestion-scheduler.ts:336,355,506,518

**Problem:** '} catch {' blocks discard the error and substitute generic strings ('Health check failed', 'Schedule sync sweep failed'). Operators get no signal as to whether the upstream was down, returned 4xx, or threw a JSON parse error.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §7: 'Catch exceptions only to add context, translate domain errors, or handle expected failures. Do not swallow backend errors to preserve a fake success path.'

**Suggested fix:** catch (err) and pass the error into the structured logger before substituting a generic status.

title: Rankings persistence resolves provider mapping by externalId only (cross-provider collision)
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/persistence/ingestion-persistence.ts:629-632

**Problem:** persistRankings looks up participantProviderMapping by externalId alone (findFirst({ where: { externalId } })), ignoring providerId. If two providers ever issue the same external id (almost guaranteed across vendors), rankings are attributed to a random one.

**Why it's wrong:** The ProviderRanking payload omits providerId, so persistence guesses. Architecture-rules §4 demands explicit boundaries.

**Suggested fix:** Add providerId to ProviderRanking and look up by the unique (providerId, externalId) index. If providerId can't be passed through, attribute via the calling adapter context.

title: Pervasive 'as any' casting around Prisma JSON columns + lost-update race in rankings
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/persistence/ingestion-persistence.ts:123,137,353,436,465,550,554,578,579,663,665,676,686

**Problem:** 13+ uses of 'as any' to coerce metadata/normalizedData/rankings into JSON columns. (existing.rankings as any[]) is then mutated in place and written back without a transaction (lines 663-679) — a clear lost-update race when concurrent ranking syncs touch the same participantSeasonRecord.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §1 (avoid any) plus undocumented JSON shape assumption. Concurrent rankings update is unsafe.

**Suggested fix:** Define typed shapes for the JSON payloads in packages/shared/dto or packages/shared/domain. Wrap the rankings read-modify-write in prisma.$transaction with a unique-constraint upsert and merge logic that doesn't depend on prior in-memory state.

title: Module-level mutable state in PollConfigService and IngestionConfigService
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/admin/poll-config-service.ts:42
- packages/core-api/src/modules/admin/ingestion-config-service.ts:51

**Problem:** Both files declare 'let currentConfig' at module scope. The exported getPollIntervalConfig() reads it. If two service instances are constructed (tests, multi-tenant, accidental duplicate buildApp), they share state. Service classes are then thin wrappers around globals.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §3 (services own business logic) and the spirit of avoiding hidden global state. Breaks test isolation.

**Suggested fix:** Move state into the class instance. If the global getter is required by a Fastify plugin, inject the service instance instead.

title: Generic BAD_REQUEST/INTERNAL_ERROR codes for known domain errors
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** service-rules · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/core/error-handler.ts:65-74

**Problem:** inferErrorCode maps several named domain errors (ContestOperationError, ContestEntryOperationError, InvitationInvalidError, MemberOperationError, SquadOperationError, ContestManagementError) to the placeholder code 'BAD_REQUEST', and falls back to 'INTERNAL_ERROR'/'BAD_REQUEST'.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §7 explicitly bans transport-only placeholder codes when the domain reason is known: 'Intentional application errors must use stable, descriptive, domain-specific codes rather than transport-only placeholders such as BAD_REQUEST, FORBIDDEN, or NOT_FOUND.'

**Suggested fix:** Each domain error class should carry a domain-specific code (e.g., CONTEST_OPERATION_INVALID, INVITATION_TOKEN_INVALID). Update the registry mapping accordingly.

title: drafts routes use a flat {error,message} payload internally — parallel envelope
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** contract-drift · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/drafts/routes.ts:128-136, 940, 979, 984, 988, 1004, 1167, 1252, 1269

**Problem:** Internal helpers and call sites build payloads as { error: 'CODE', message: '...' }. sendWithStatus translates that into the proper envelope, but the local payload type does not match the wire shape and only one helper performs the translation. Easy to bypass by sending an unwrapped payload through reply.status(...).send(...).

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §7 standard error envelope is { error: { code, message, details? } }. Two parallel payload conventions in the same module is fragile.

**Suggested fix:** Unify on the standard envelope from createErrorEnvelope (already imported on line 29). Remove the flat-shape intermediate type entirely.

title: Inline JSON-Schema with additionalProperties:true on /scoring/config/validate
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** contract-drift · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/routes.ts:35-70

**Problem:** Request body schema is hand-written JSON Schema with multiple additionalProperties:true arrays/objects (stat_rules, position_rules, bonus_rules, penalty_rules, multiplier_rules, bracket_round_rules, tiebreaker_config) instead of using zodToJsonSchema(ScoringConfigSchema).

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §10 explicitly lists additionalProperties:true and inline { type: 'object', properties: } in route files as forbidden. The corresponding Zod schema (ScoringConfigSchema) already exists.

**Suggested fix:** Replace inline body schema with body: zodToJsonSchema(ScoringConfigSchema). Generated client gets a real type instead of Record<string, unknown>.

title: Inline contest-management adapter inside route module (direct prisma + inline shaping)
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** service-rules · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/contest-management/routes.ts:44-71

**Problem:** The contestManagementModule route file constructs an inline findById adapter that does direct prisma.sportEvent.findUnique and locally shapes the result into a domain object. Precisely the 'no inline shaping' anti-pattern from service-rules §10.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates service-rules §3 (database access stays behind service/repository boundaries) and §4 (no inline .map/inline shaping in route files).

**Suggested fix:** Move into PrismaSportEventRepository next to the other adapters in packages/core-api/src/adapters, and inject into the service.

title: OddsApiAdapter is unconditionally constructed with empty-string sentinel API key
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** mock-data-in-prod · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/adapters/odds-api-adapter.ts:73-74,221-228
- packages/core-api/src/index.ts:77 (instantiated unconditionally)
- POST /api/v1/ingestion/odds/:sport (route registered regardless)

**Problem:** Constructor falls back to process.env.ODDS_API_KEY ?? ''. With no key, fetches go out as apiKey=, which the upstream rejects as 401. The endpoint is wired regardless of configuration. The 401 will bubble up as an Error, leaking provider URL info.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §1 explicitly bans 'Shipping sentinel fallback values such as '', UNKNOWN, or similar invented placeholders in API-facing service output.' Plus the route is registered even when nothing is configured.

**Suggested fix:** Throw at construction if ODDS_API_KEY is missing, or skip registration of oddsAdapter and the /odds/:sport route when no key is configured.

title: Suspicious 'as unknown as PrismaClient' cast in contest-entry-scoring-result-service
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/contest-scoring/contest-entry-scoring-result-service.ts:38

**Problem:** const tx = transactionClient as unknown as PrismaClient; casts the Prisma.TransactionClient to the full PrismaClient, bypassing the type system. Silently allows nested transactions or operations not supported inside a transaction.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §1 (avoid any) plus the general ban on 'as unknown as' (service-rules §4).

**Suggested fix:** Type all tx-using helpers as Prisma.TransactionClient and remove the cast.

title: ScoringService.getBreakdownContext always returns an empty Map (sentinel stub)
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/service.ts:188-193

**Problem:** A private method that takes contestId and entryId, ignores both via _ prefix, and returns a fresh empty Map. Used by getEntryScore to populate contextLabel on every breakdown — meaning contextLabel is always null in the API response, regardless of contest configuration.

**Why it's wrong:** Sentinel/synthetic-fallback pattern banned by service-rules §1. The DTO field is documented contract surface, but its implementation is a stub.

**Suggested fix:** Either implement context resolution from ContestConfiguration / scoring rules, or remove contextLabel from ScoreBreakdownDto until the value is computed for real.

title: JWT secret defaults to a hardcoded fallback in three places
description:
**Severity:** MEDIUM (rescoped from CRITICAL on 2026-05-02 — see note below) · **Category:** duplication / hardcoded constant · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/plugins/auth-guard.ts:74
- packages/core-api/src/plugins/admin-auth.ts:19
- packages/core-api/src/modules/auth/auth-service.ts:88

**Problem (coding issue):** The literal string `'poolmaster-dev-secret-change-in-production'` is hardcoded as a `??` fallback in three different files. Any change has to be made three places; they have already drifted in *when* they read `process.env.JWT_SECRET` (admin-auth at module-load time, the others at function-call time). This is the duplicated-hardcoded-constant pattern the project owner has explicitly called out as unacceptable.

**Why it's wrong:** Three independent fallbacks for the same value. Even if the dev fallback is intentional (it is — see scope note), it should not be retyped in three files.

**Suggested fix (coding-only):** Introduce one bootstrap helper (e.g., `getJwtSigningSecret()` in `packages/core-api/src/config/`) that reads `process.env.JWT_SECRET` once. The three call sites import the helper. Whether the helper falls back to a dev string or throws is a separate decision — for now, keeping the dev fallback inside the helper is fine. The coding defect is the duplication, not the policy.

**Scope note (2026-05-02):** This was originally filed as CRITICAL with a recommendation to hard-fail on missing `JWT_SECRET` in non-local runtimes. The project owner has confirmed that strict secret hygiene is not required while the app is still being built, so the security-hardening half of the original recommendation is removed. The duplicated-hardcoded-constant half remains as a coding-quality defect.

title: Email delivery and themed system messages
description: Coordinate the PoolMaster email lane: themed Prime Time Commissioner template registry, AWS SES deployed delivery, league invite delivery, registration plus league join confirmation, contest entry confirmation, and contest started summary messages.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Send contest started summary emails
description:
## User story
As a commissioner or league member with contest entries, I want a contest-started email when a contest begins so I know the contest is live and can jump to the leaderboard or entry list.

## Expected behavior
When a contest transitions to started/in-progress, PoolMaster sends a themed Prime Time Commissioner email summarizing the contest and entries. The email should include league name, contest name, event name/start time where available, number of submitted entries, relevant team/member entry context where appropriate, and a link to the contest leaderboard or entry list page. This uses the shared themed template registry and configured mail provider.

## Notes
Trigger timing should be tied to the real contest lifecycle transition rather than a page view. Delivery should avoid duplicate sends if lifecycle sync runs repeatedly while the contest is already in progress. Depends on the themed template registry and mail-provider lane.
acceptance_criteria:
- Send contest started summary email when a contest first transitions to started/in-progress.
- Use the shared themed Prime Time Commissioner email template registry.
- Include contest, league, event/start context, entry summary, and leaderboard/entry-list link.
- Avoid duplicate sends across repeated lifecycle/sync processing.
- Add tests for trigger behavior, template data mapping, delivery handoff, and duplicate prevention.
notes: Starting implementation. Scope: wire contest-started summary emails to the real contest lifecycle transition to ACTIVE/started, use CONTEST_STARTED_SUMMARY template/provider, include league/contest/event/start context and entry summary, prevent duplicate sends across repeated lifecycle processing, and add unit coverage.
close_reason: Implemented contest-started summary emails from ingestion-driven lifecycle transitions. When an ingested sport event becomes IN_PROGRESS, linked OPEN/LOCKED contests transition to ACTIVE and send CONTEST_STARTED_SUMMARY through the shared mail provider to active commissioners and active team owners with submitted entries. Repeated syncs do not resend because email delivery only happens after the guarded status update succeeds. Provider failures are logged without failing ingestion. Validation: root typecheck, root lint, full unit suite, service functional API with DATABASE_URL, and poolmaster unit passed.

title: Send account registration and league join success email
description:
## User story
As a new member, I want a confirmation email after successfully registering an account and joining a league so I know my account and league membership were created.

## Expected behavior
After a user successfully accepts a league invitation and joins the league, PoolMaster sends a themed Prime Time Commissioner email confirming the account/league join outcome. The email should include member name/email, league name/code, team name where applicable, and a link back to the league. This uses the shared themed template registry and configured mail provider.

## Notes
This story covers the successful registration plus league join flow. It should be idempotent enough to avoid duplicate sends on page refresh/retry if the membership was already accepted. Depends on the themed template registry and mail-provider lane.
acceptance_criteria:
- Send success email after invitation acceptance creates or reactivates league membership for a newly registered or existing invited user.
- Use the shared themed Prime Time Commissioner email template registry.
- Include league name/code, team name where applicable, and league link.
- Avoid duplicate sends on harmless retry/refresh where no new acceptance occurs.
- Add tests for trigger behavior, template data mapping, and delivery handoff.
notes: Starting workflow wiring after mail provider landed. Scope: send the shared LEAGUE_JOIN_SUCCESS template after invitation acceptance creates/reactivates membership, include league link and team name when available, avoid duplicate sends by only sending after successful pending invitation acceptance, and add unit coverage.
close_reason: Implemented league join success confirmation email after invitation acceptance creates/reactivates membership. Uses shared themed LEAGUE_JOIN_SUCCESS template, configured mail provider, league deep link, team name when available, duplicate avoidance through pending-invitation acceptance semantics, and failure logging that does not unwind the completed join. Validation: root typecheck, root lint, full unit suite passed.
