title: Adopt User Profile details/action layout for League and Team Details
description: Product/UI story. Standardize details-oriented pages around the User Profile page paradigm: primary details/status tiles on the left and a right-side action menu for lower-frequency actions. User Profile is the reference layout and should remain the pattern source. Apply this to League Details and Team Details so users see the same details/action structure across account, league, and team surfaces. League Details requirements: keep a League Details tile on the left; remove implementation note text that says 'League Home now owns the commissioner-editable identity fields directly instead of sending commissioners to a separate modal.' Move league status into the League Details tile, matching the User Profile details/status pattern. Replace the current inline League Invitations tile with a right-side action menu item named Invite Members. Invite Members opens a modal that contains the invitation flow; modal title/tile label becomes 'Invite Members'; replace existing invite helper text 'Commissioners and root admins can reuse the live invite flows directly from League Home.' with 'Invite new members to join the <league name> league'. League lifecycle actions should follow the User Profile convention: show Inactivate League when active; show Activate League and Delete League according to the existing lifecycle/delete rules when inactive; delete must keep the existing confirmation protections. Add a Leave League action button that opens a modal explaining the consequence, with Confirm and Cancel actions. After leave completes, show confirmation and an OK action. OK must move the user to another league from their league list when one exists; if no leagues remain, sign the user out. Team Details requirements: reorganize Team Details/Team Home to use the same left details/status tile plus right action-menu paradigm as User Profile and League Details. The Team Details tile is read-only: remove 'You can update your team name and choose a built-in icon here.'; show the team icon next to the read-only team name; show team status in the details tile; show team owners in the detail summary with each owner on their own row as <name>. Remove the Active Entry Management tile entirely because it duplicates navigation that belongs in the top menu. Move editable/less frequent work into the action menu. Team action menu items: Change Team Name, Change Team Icon, Manage Owners, Inactivate Team when active, and Delete Team conditionally when existing lifecycle/delete rules allow it. Preserve existing team lifecycle/delete rules, owner permissions, icon behavior, and any inactive/delete follow-up rules already defined. Acceptance: League Details, Team Details, and User Profile share the same page structure; details/status are readable on the left; less frequent actions live in the right action menu; Team Details is read-only and shows icon/name/status/owners; Active Entry Management tile is gone; Team edit/lifecycle/delete actions are modal/action-menu driven and conditionally available; League Invitations are modal-driven via Invite Members; lifecycle/leave/delete flows are modal-driven and permission-aware; no implementation-note copy is visible; tests cover commissioner/member/root-admin states and leave-league routing/logout behavior.
notes: Starting implementation batch. Sequence: first rework League Details and Team Details to the User Profile details/actions pattern, then replace the app shell button row with header dropdown navigation and split active contests/history.
close_reason: Implemented. League Details and Team Details now use the User Profile-style details/actions layout with modal-driven low-frequency actions; Team Details is read-only and removes the duplicate active entry management tile. App shell now uses header dropdown navigation with updated branding, icon-only notifications, no Help button, my-contests filter link, and split Active Contests / Contest History routes. Verification: npx turbo typecheck --force; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api after npm run db:test:reset; npm run test:poolmaster:unit.

title: Reorganize app navigation into a single header menu bar
description: Product/UI story. Replace the current two independent rows of navigation buttons with one consistent top navigation bar that sits in the application header next to the league selector. Keep the username/account dropdown as the home for Profile, Manage for root admin, and logout. Remove the standalone Help button for now. Keep notification access in the header, but render it as a compact inbox icon button instead of a large text button. Update branding in the header: eyebrow becomes 'Prime Time Commissioner' and primary brand line becomes 'ultimate office pool manager'. Starting menu model: My Team dropdown includes Team Details (/league/:leagueCode/team), My Contests mapped to the League Contests experience with a my-entries/my-team filter applied, and My History (/league/:leagueCode/history). League dropdown includes League Details (/league/:leagueCode), Teams and Owners (/league/:leagueCode/teams), Active Contests, Contest History, and Create Contest (/league/:leagueCode/contests/new, commissioner only). Active Contests and Contest History must be split into separate pages/routes because contest history will grow over time and should not live on the active-contest operational surface. Existing commissioner Manage Contests route (/league/:leagueCode/contests/manage) should remain accessible from the Active Contests surface rather than staying as a separate global header button unless product review decides otherwise. Out-of-menu routes remain deep links/drill-ins: contest detail, contest entry, contest manage/edit, join/invite routes, welcome, account/profile, root-admin manage pages, sync/config pages. Acceptance: app shell has one header navigation system; old second-row button nav is removed; menu items are permission-aware and disabled/hidden consistently when no league is selected; My Contests opens active/league contests with the user's entries filter applied; Active Contests and Contest History are distinct pages and distinct menu items; Help button is removed; notification button is compact icon-only with accessible label; current league context is preserved across all menu navigation; tests cover member, commissioner, and root-admin header states.
notes: Starting implementation batch. Sequence: first rework League Details and Team Details to the User Profile details/actions pattern, then replace the app shell button row with header dropdown navigation and split active contests/history.
close_reason: Implemented. League Details and Team Details now use the User Profile-style details/actions layout with modal-driven low-frequency actions; Team Details is read-only and removes the duplicate active entry management tile. App shell now uses header dropdown navigation with updated branding, icon-only notifications, no Help button, my-contests filter link, and split Active Contests / Contest History routes. Verification: npx turbo typecheck --force; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api after npm run db:test:reset; npm run test:poolmaster:unit.

title: My Profile account summary wraps core identity fields
description: Manual QA/layout defect. The left Account Summary tile puts name/email/username/status/member-since/auth-provider/role in a grid where core identity fields wrap awkwardly. Expected behavior: keep name, email, and username in the top account summary tile with each field on its own full-width line so normal values do not wrap; create a second tile for member since/status and role/auth method. Rename Auth Provider to Method. Actual behavior: identity fields share columns and wrap visually.
notes: Starting batch fix. Scope: My Profile summary layout only; add frontend regression for identity fields and secondary account info tile.
close_reason: Fixed My Profile copy, account summary layout, profile email editing, and username change with uniqueness validation. Verified with focused account/UI tests plus typecheck, lint, unit, PoolMaster unit, and functional API gates.

title: My Profile hero copy uses implementation label and stale account wording
description: Manual QA/UI copy defect. My Profile currently shows an eyebrow label 'User' and helper text 'Manage your profile, preferences, password, and account lifecycle.' Expected behavior: remove the 'User' label and change helper text to 'Manage your user profile, preferences, login, and account information.' Actual behavior: copy is less clear and uses account lifecycle language that does not match the desired user-facing wording.
notes: Starting batch fix. Scope: My Profile copy only; add frontend regression and update page copy.
close_reason: Fixed My Profile copy, account summary layout, profile email editing, and username change with uniqueness validation. Verified with focused account/UI tests plus typecheck, lint, unit, PoolMaster unit, and functional API gates.

title: Manage Teams shows redundant broken header filters above grid filters
description: Manual QA defect. Root-admin Manage Teams shows two filtering surfaces: a top header tile with team search, league code filter, and lifecycle filter, plus the AdminDataGrid column filters. The top filters are confusing and the team search does not work reliably, while the grid filters are the intended current behavior. Expected behavior: remove the top header filtering controls for now and leave the grid column filters as the only filtering path. A true backend/global search can be reintroduced later if/when a real search backend exists. Actual behavior: both filter sets are visible, and the top search creates confusion.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: add a frontend regression proving the redundant header filters are absent, then remove the query-backed header filter state/UI while keeping the admin team list endpoint load and grid filters intact.
close_reason: Removed redundant query-backed header search/league/lifecycle filters from Manage Teams and left AdminDataGrid column filters as the only filtering UI. Added pool-master-85r regression coverage.

title: Remove redundant entry progress section from contest entry page
description: Feature enhancement / UX cleanup. On the contest entry page, the second left-side section that tracks entry progress is unnecessary and duplicative with the participant selection UI. The participant selection already tracks total selections per tier, and the entry page already tracks total selections. Expected behavior: remove the separate progress-tracking section completely. Use the tier selection UI as the source of progress truth, and add highlighting/attention treatment for unfinished tiers that do not yet have enough selections. Actual behavior: the page shows a redundant progress section in addition to tier-level selection progress, creating unnecessary visual noise.
close_reason: Removed the redundant Selection progress section from the contest entry page and highlighted incomplete tier cards in the lineup builder. Covered by pool-master-jw0 assertions in contest-entry-page.test.tsx.

title: Move contest history from League Home to League Contests page
description: Feature enhancement / IA cleanup. Contest History should live on the League Contests page under the active contests section, not on League Home. Expected behavior: League Contests contains active contests first and contest history below it; League Home no longer shows Contest History. Actual behavior: Contest History is currently shown on League Home while League Contests is not the primary contest/history surface. This should be coordinated with pool-master-7j3, which moves the active league contest list from League Home to League Contests.
notes: Starting Batch 1 implementation: completed contest history moves under active contests on the League Contests page.
close_reason: Batch 1 complete. Completed contest history now lives under active contests on the League Contests page, and the completed-history tile was removed from League Home.

title: Move league contest list from League Home to League Contests page
description: Feature enhancement / IA cleanup. The League Contests page currently acts as a placeholder that sends users back to League Home, while the actual league contest information/list lives on League Home. This should be reversed: League Contests should be the home for league contest information, contest cards/list, contest boards, and relevant contest actions. Remove the contest list/information from League Home, and remove the current placeholder tile on League Contests that only says to use League Home and has an Open League Home button. Expected behavior: clicking League Contests shows the league's contests directly. League Home should not duplicate the contest list. Actual behavior: League Contests is a placeholder and League Home contains the contest list.
notes: Starting Batch 1 implementation: League Contests becomes the primary contest list surface and League Home is thinned.
close_reason: Batch 1 complete. /league/:leagueCode/contests now renders the real League Contests page with active contest cards and contest board links, and League Home no longer owns the contest list.

title: Deleting a league should return admin to Manage Leagues list
description: Manual QA/navigation defect. After a root-admin/admin successfully deletes a league, the app should return to the root-admin Manage Leagues list. Expected behavior: successful league deletion navigates to the manage/league list so the admin can continue reviewing leagues. Actual behavior: post-delete navigation does not return to the Manage Leagues list.
close_reason: Fixed LeagueDetailPage delete success handling so root-admin deletions return to /manage/leagues and refresh the manage-leagues cache. Covered by pool-master-hna regression in league-detail-page.test.tsx.

title: Deleting a user should return admin to Manage Users list
description: Manual QA/navigation defect. After a root-admin/admin successfully deletes a user, the app should return to the root-admin Manage Users list. Expected behavior: successful user deletion navigates to the manage/user list so the admin can continue reviewing users. Actual behavior: post-delete navigation does not return to the Manage Users list.
close_reason: No code change needed: RootAdminUserAccountPage already navigates to /manage/users with replace:true after successful adminDeleteUser. Marking stale after verification during the P2 navigation batch.

title: Manage Leagues should use shared grid with column filters
description: Feature enhancement / UI consistency. The root-admin Manage Leagues page still uses a card/tile list with a single 'Search by league name' input. It should use the new shared grid component, consistent with the Teams grid, and support column-level client-side filters instead of broad search. Move the data currently embedded in league tiles into separate grid columns, including league name, league code, lifecycle/status, member count, active contest count, and description or other relevant detail. Expected behavior: Manage Leagues presents a filterable grid with column filters and row navigation to League Home. Actual behavior: Manage Leagues shows large cards/tiles and a single search box.
close_reason: Implemented Manage Leagues on the shared AdminDataGrid with client-side column filters and row navigation to League Home; focused Vitest coverage proves the old search box is gone.

title: Account delete dependency error should name linked team and league
description: Manual QA/UX defect. When deleting a user account that still owns or belongs to league-scoped team data, the delete modal shows an internal dependency GUID in the end-user error message: 'ACCOUNT_DELETE_DEPENDENCIES_EXIST: Account still owns or belongs to league-scoped data: ede1c85a-d02e-40f4-9de6-1b5a28b984b8'. This is not helpful to an admin. Expected behavior: internal GUIDs may be logged for diagnostics, but the end-user message should identify the blocking dependency in domain terms: 'Account cannot be deleted because it's still an owner of team <teamName> in league <leagueName-leagueCode>'. The team name should hyperlink to the team homepage and the league name/code should hyperlink to the league homepage. Actual behavior: the modal shows the internal error code plus GUID with no team or league name and no navigation path to resolve the dependency. Screenshot evidence includes delete attempt for playwright-member-1777372702386-o2mzgv@example.test.
close_reason: Implemented structured delete dependency details from the service, returned them in the admin delete error envelope, and rendered a user-safe modal message with team and league links. Focused backend and webapp tests cover the blocked-owner path.

title: Use Teams-style grid and column filters on Manage Users
description: Feature enhancement. Update the root-admin Manage Users page to use the same grid/table component and interaction paradigm as the Manage Teams page. Replace the current single Search users functionality with column-level client-side filtering. Expected outcome: user directory rows are presented consistently with the teams list/grid, and root-admins can filter users by relevant columns such as username, email, name, lifecycle/status, and root-admin state without triggering the current broad search workflow. This story is separate from the P1 defect pool-master-h61, which tracks the current search causing ROOT_ADMIN_SESSION_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Implemented Manage Users on the shared AdminDataGrid with client-side column filters and canonical name links; focused Vitest coverage proves no server-side search call is used.

title: User Directory should link username instead of showing Open User Page button
description: Manual QA/UI consistency defect. On the root-admin User Directory list, navigation to a user currently uses a separate 'Open User Page' button. This differs from the team list navigation paradigm. Expected behavior: the username itself is a hyperlink to the user's detail page, consistent with the team list UI/navigation pattern, and the separate Open User Page button is removed. Actual behavior: the list shows an Open User Page button instead of making the username the navigable element.
close_reason: Changed User Directory rows so the display name is the canonical /users/:id link and removed the separate Open user page action column. Covered by root-admin-manage-users-page.test.tsx.

title: Manage teams list shows implementation helper text under team name
description: Manual QA/UI copy defect. On the root-admin Manage Teams list page, each team row/card shows the text 'Open Team Home to manage lifecycle.' under the team name. This appears to be implementation/helper copy rather than end-user facing content and should be removed. Expected behavior: the manage teams list shows the team name and relevant operational fields/actions without this helper sentence. Actual behavior: the helper text appears under the team name.
close_reason: Removed the implementation helper copy under team names on the Manage Teams grid. Covered by root-admin-manage-teams-page.test.tsx.

title: Ingestion job failure ERROR log loses error message
description: Logging defect found during QA sync investigation. CloudWatch for the failed EVENT_PARTICIPANTS_SYNC at 2026-04-28T10:30:08.760Z logs an ERROR message 'Ingestion job failed' with fields jobType=EVENT_PARTICIPANTS_SYNC, providerId=mock-contest-feed, sport=GOLF, eventExternalId=golf-relative-manual-test-20260427t102759z, but the structured error field is serialized as an empty object: error={}. The useful message only appears later inside job.errorLog/manual sync payload. Expected behavior: the primary ERROR log for an ingestion job failure includes the error message/name/stack or equivalent diagnostic fields so live CloudWatch triage has immediate signal. Actual behavior: the primary ERROR log loses the error details.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: add a scheduler unit regression proving the primary failure log includes a useful error message/name instead of an empty object, then adjust ingestion scheduler failure logging to use the logger error serializer/explicit message fields. Validation: focused ingestion scheduler unit test, then broader gates after batch.
close_reason: Fixed. Added pool-master-4k2 regression coverage proving failed ingestion jobs log errorMessage/errorName. Red step observed in ingestion-scheduler focused unit test: expected errorMessage/errorName, received only raw error. Green step: focused ingestion-scheduler unit test passes after scheduler emits explicit message/name fields and keeps err for structured serializers.

title: Contest entry details page shows unnecessary Team Home button
description: Manual QA/UI defect. On the contest entry page, Entry details currently shows a Team Home button. This button should be removed from the entry details surface because the page is focused on the contest entry workflow and the Team Home navigation is unnecessary there. Expected behavior: Entry details does not render a Team Home button. Actual behavior: Entry details includes a Team Home button.
notes: Starting fix. Add frontend regression that Entry Details does not render the Team Home link, then remove the link from ContestEntryPage.
close_reason: Fixed. Added a frontend regression proving Contest Entry Details does not render the Team Home link and removed that link from ContestEntryPage. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- contest-entry-page.test.tsx.

title: Wire themed system email templates
description: Add a simple source-controlled email template registry for Prime Time Commissioner system emails. Initial templates should cover league member invites, successful account registration plus league join, contest entry completed, and contest started. This is not a database-backed template manager and does not include root-admin UI management in the first slice. HTML email bodies should use Prime Time Commissioner branding and the current PoolMaster/Prime Time Commissioner theme styling where email-client-safe, with text fallbacks for every template.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add static templates for league member invite, registration plus league join success, contest entry completed, and contest started.
- Templates render subject, text, and HTML from typed template data.
- HTML templates use Prime Time Commissioner branding and theme styling where email-client-safe.
- Service email delivery uses the registry instead of inline string assembly.
- No database persistence, SES template registry dependency, or root-admin template-management UI is required for the first implementation.
- Template rendering has unit coverage.
notes: Starting implementation of themed source-controlled email template registry. Scope for this slice: typed renderers for league member invite, registration plus league join success, contest entry completed, and contest started summary; Prime Time Commissioner themed HTML plus text fallback; unit coverage. Delivery/provider wiring remains in downstream SES/workflow stories.
close_reason: Implemented typed Prime Time Commissioner themed system email template registry with HTML/text renderers and unit coverage for league invites, registration plus league join success, contest entry completed, and contest started summary. No existing service email delivery string assembly exists to migrate; SES/workflow stories will consume this registry.

title: Plan SES-backed PoolMaster system email delivery
description: Create a narrative plan for SES-backed PoolMaster system email delivery covering provider abstraction, configuration, delivery logging, local/dev behavior, tests, deployment, and rollout sequencing for league invite and contest entry completed emails.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a plan file under plans/ that references pool-master-gs4.
- Cover SES provider architecture and non-delivering local/dev behavior.
- Cover template registry/rendering, logging, tests, configuration, rollout, and open questions.
- Reference league invite, contest entry confirmation, and template stories.
notes: Create a narrative implementation plan for SES-backed system emails, including provider abstraction, configuration, delivery logging, local/dev behavior, tests, deploy rollout, and relationship to existing stories pool-master-7ij and pool-master-95b.
close_reason: Created plans/112-ses-system-email-integration.md covering SES provider architecture, local/dev behavior, static template registry, logging, tests, configuration, rollout, and open questions for league invite and entry-completed emails.

title: Send contest entry confirmation emails
description:
## User story
As a contest entrant, I want a confirmation email after completing or updating an entry so I have a reliable receipt of my selected lineup and tiebreaker.

## Expected behavior
When an entry is completed/submitted, PoolMaster sends the entrant a themed Prime Time Commissioner email containing contest name, league name, team/entry name, selected participants grouped by tier, tiebreaker, edit/entry URL where applicable, and relevant lock/start context. The email uses the shared system email template registry and the configured mail provider.
acceptance_criteria:
- Send contest entry completed email through the shared mail-delivery abstraction.
- Use the shared themed template registry, not inline strings.
- Include selected lineup grouped by tier and the tiebreaker.
- Include a deep link back to the entry or contest page.
- Use Prime Time Commissioner branding and theme styling in the HTML body.
- Add tests for template data mapping, delivery handoff, and provider failure handling.
notes: Starting contest entry confirmation email wiring. Scope: trigger from contest entry update/final-save path when the roster is complete and a tiebreaker is saved; use shared CONTEST_ENTRY_COMPLETED template and configured mail provider; include league/contest/team, selections grouped by tier, tiebreaker, deep link; log provider failures without unwinding the saved entry; add unit coverage.
close_reason: Implemented contest entry completion receipts from the entry update/final-save path. The service sends CONTEST_ENTRY_COMPLETED through the shared mail provider only when the entry has the required roster selections and a saved tiebreaker; receipt includes league, contest, team, entry, tiered selections, tiebreaker, and entry deep link. Provider failures are logged without unwinding the saved entry. Validation: root typecheck, root lint, full unit suite passed.

title: Send league invitation emails through AWS SES provider
description:
## User story
As a league commissioner, I want invite-by-email to send an actual email through AWS SES in deployed environments so invitees can discover and accept the league invitation without needing the commissioner to manually copy/paste an invite link.

## Current behavior
The league invitation API creates pending email invitation records and returns them in the response, but the service does not call a mail provider. Manual QA observed that invite-by-email does not deliver an email. Code inspection confirms invitation records are created without SES or local SMTP delivery.

## Expected behavior
When invite-by-email succeeds, PoolMaster sends a league member invitation email containing Prime Time Commissioner branded and theme-styled HTML, text fallback, league name, inviter context where appropriate, accept URL/invite code, expiration context, and any commissioner message supported by the request. Local/dev should continue to support Mailpit-friendly delivery; QA/prod should use AWS SES.

## AWS SES scope
Implement SES as the deployed mail provider, including runtime configuration and infrastructure. This story should cover Terraform for SES identity/domain or sender verification, DKIM/MAIL FROM decisions where applicable, IAM permissions for the service task role, SES region/configuration-set settings if used, environment variables/secrets, and deployment documentation. SES template registry support should be evaluated, but v1 delivery may send app-rendered subject/text/html produced by the PoolMaster template registry.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a mail-delivery abstraction with local/dev and AWS SES implementations.
- Configure QA/prod to use SES via Terraform and runtime environment settings.
- Add required IAM permissions for the deployed service to submit email through SES.
- Document SES identity/domain verification, sender address, reply-to behavior, region, sandbox/production considerations, and deployment variables.
- Wire league invite-by-email to submit rendered template output after invitation records are created.
- Use Prime Time Commissioner branding and the current theme styling in the HTML email body where email-client-safe.
- Log delivery attempts and provider failures without logging sensitive message content.
- Decide and document whether API success means invitation record creation, delivery submission, or both.
- Add tests for provider selection/configuration, SES provider invocation, invitation delivery handoff, and failure handling.
notes: Starting implementation after pool-master-98i template registry landed. Scope: mail-delivery abstraction, local/dev and SES providers, provider selection/config tests, invitation handoff using shared template registry, Terraform/env/docs for SES configuration. Downstream contest/account workflow stories remain separate.
close_reason: Implemented mail-delivery abstraction with SMTP and SES providers, provider configuration, SES invocation logging/failure handling, league invite email handoff using the themed template registry, Terraform SES identity/IAM/env config, and email delivery docs. Validation: root typecheck, root lint, full unit suite passed. Terraform validate was attempted but local AWS provider plugin failed to instantiate.

title: Run Event Sync requires manual event id instead of valid loaded event selection
description:
## Summary
The root-admin Run Event Sync page currently requires typing an Event ID manually. That is not practical for manual QA or operations because root admins should not need to know provider/internal event IDs.

## Expected behavior
Replace the free-text Event ID entry with a dropdown/select populated from actual events already loaded into PoolMaster.

## Valid event rule
The selectable events should be valid for EVENT sync functionality: actual loaded events whose sport/feed/status make them eligible for event-level sync. The option label should show enough context to choose correctly, such as event name, sport, start date/time, lifecycle/status, provider/external ID if needed, and participant/score readiness state where available.

## Current evidence
clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/root-admin-run-event-sync-page.tsx passes eventSyncEventId.trim() into adminSyncProviderEventData, and tests currently use typed IDs such as golf-masters-2026.

## Suggested implementation notes
- Use generated SDK/listEvents or add a backend/admin event-list endpoint if the existing event list lacks the fields/status needed for root-admin sync selection.
- Disable event-sync submission until a valid loaded event is selected.
- Keep sport/feed validation aligned with sync config rather than hardcoded sports/feed workarounds.
- Add frontend coverage proving free text is gone and only selectable loaded events can be submitted.
notes: Implemented Run Event Sync dropdown selection backed by listEvents instead of free-text Event ID. Event list contract now exposes provider externalId for event-level sync submission and includes OFFICIAL event status for result-ready events. UI filters selectable events by selected event-sync feed status, submits the provider externalId, and has regression coverage proving typed IDs are gone. Targeted verification passed: poolmaster page test, event service/handler unit test, api:refresh, api:validate. Targeted events integration test could not run because Prisma cannot connect to local Postgres from Node even though psql can connect; full gates/push deferred until batch completion.

title: Expose service and webapp version metadata for QA debugging
description:
## Summary
Add a first-class version metadata capability for debugging deployed QA/prod issues.

## Current state
- PoolMaster webapp builds already emit dist/version-info.json via clients/poolmaster/scripts/write-version-info.mjs.
- The webapp has getVersionInfo()/getEmbeddedVersionInfo() in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/version-info.ts.
- CI sets webapp/service SHA metadata during the webapp build.
- No core-api /version endpoint was found during intake.
- Existing webapp version-info.json does not clearly include generated client SDK/package version metadata.

## Requested enhancement
- Add a service-tier /version endpoint that returns API/service version info, git SHA, build number/build time, environment, and other operational metadata useful for debugging.
- Keep/extend webapp version-info.json with webapp version, git SHA, build number/time, asset release prefix, environment, client SDK/generated contract version if available, and the service version/SHA the webapp was built against.
- Document how to use both endpoints/artifacts when debugging failed-to-load or stale-deploy issues.

## Acceptance notes
- Endpoint should be unauthenticated if appropriate for diagnostics, but avoid exposing secrets or infrastructure internals.
- Response shapes should be typed/shared and covered by tests.
- Update README/docs for where to check deployed webapp metadata and service metadata.
notes: Implemented public service version endpoint at /version and /api/v1/version, webapp version-info client SDK/build-number metadata, CI task/webapp build stamping, generated OpenAPI/client updates, docs, and targeted unit coverage. Full gates and push are deferred until the requested batch is complete.

title: Document sync behavior guide
description: Create a durable docs/ guide explaining PoolMaster sync types, provider/manual/scheduled behavior, lookahead/time windows, event lifecycle transition timing, and participant stat sync conditions for QA and root-admin testing.
notes: Starting docs slice. Scope: inspect ingestion scheduler, provider interface/adapters, admin sync routes/services, event operational timing, and tests; create a durable docs/ sync guide for QA/root-admin use. No production behavior changes expected; validation will be doc review plus link/path checks.
close_reason: Created docs/SYNC-GUIDE.md covering sync entry points, feed behavior, returned job/run records, default windows, generated relative golf event windows, event readiness, and live-score trigger conditions. Validation: traced scheduler/provider/persistence/admin route source files and reviewed the rendered markdown content. Created follow-up defects pool-master-8yh and pool-master-940 for dashboard job persistence and provider timing metadata gaps discovered during documentation.

title: Cleanup pass after Contest Board consolidation: remove remnants and review suspected leftovers
description:
Tech-debt cleanup slice for pool-master-dxd.13. Runs AFTER both pool-master-dxd.13.1 (Brad backend) and pool-master-dxd.13.2 (Fran frontend) have merged. The Contest Board consolidation deletes MyEntriesPage, thins League Home, enriches listContestEntries, and tightens getEntry visibility — that touches enough load-bearing surface area that a follow-up grep-and-prune pass is worth its own slice.

This bead is the cleanup punch-list. Each item below is either a 'verify and remove' or a 'verify and keep, document why.' Do not assume any item is dead without grepping; some look unused but are in fact load-bearing for an unrelated flow.

PURPOSE
=======
Confirm no orphans, broken imports, dead code, stale tests, or naming drift survives the consolidation, and surface anything that should be revisited now that the Contest Board owns the unified entry-display contract.

================================================================
BACKEND REMNANTS — verify still needed or remove
================================================================
1. GET /api/v1/contests/:contestId/entries/me (getMyEntry, packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/service.ts:226-236; routes.ts:189-202).
   - After consolidation, the Contest Board derives 'my entries' client-side by filtering the enriched listContestEntries response on squadId. getMyEntry's only caller may be gone.
   - VERIFY by grepping the regenerated SDK for getMyContestEntry usage in clients/poolmaster/src.
   - If unused: deprecate the operation via OpenAPI (mark as deprecated, do not delete the route until a release boundary is agreed). Note in handler/service that callers should switch to listContestEntries with client-side filter.
   - If still used (e.g., by a flow I haven't audited): document the remaining caller in a route-level comment.

2. GET /api/v1/contests/:contestId (getContest, contestsByIdModule).
   - Was previously called per-contest from LeagueDetailPage and MyEntriesPage just to read contestConfiguration.maxEntriesPerSquad. Those call sites are deleted by dxd.13.2.
   - VERIFY remaining callers — likely CreateContestPage, ManageContestsPage, contest-management module. Document those as the surviving consumers.
   - If only commissioner/admin flows consume it, consider whether the response shape can be slimmed (defer; not in this cleanup's scope unless trivial).

3. ContestEntryDetailDtoSchema participants[] becomes optional after dxd.13.1.
   - VERIFY all backend consumers (mappers, service code, contract tests) tolerate the optional case.
   - If any code-path defensively assumed participants was non-null, fix or remove that defensiveness.

4. Visibility predicate dedup.
   - The 'pre-event-start' predicate (contest.status === 'OPEN') will exist in listEntries AND getEntryDetail after dxd.13.1.
   - If it's a literal in two places, extract to a single helper (e.g., contestPicksRevealed(contest)) with a comment naming the rule.

================================================================
FRONTEND REMNANTS — verify and prune
================================================================
5. clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-routing.ts — buildLeagueEntriesPath.
   - After dxd.13.2 redirects /league/:leagueCode/entries, the helper has zero legitimate consumers.
   - GREP: rg 'buildLeagueEntriesPath' clients/poolmaster/src — should return only the helper definition.
   - REMOVE the helper and its export. Delete any related route constant.

6. data-testid='my-entries-*' selectors in tests and E2E.
   - GREP: rg 'my-entries-' clients/poolmaster — should be empty after dxd.13.2 deletes the page.
   - If anything still references them (esp. e2e/ specs), update or delete those tests in this slice.

7. Query keys named ['poolmaster', 'my-entries', ...].
   - GREP: rg "'my-entries'" clients/poolmaster — confirm all uses were inside MyEntriesPage and are now gone. The Contest Board uses ['poolmaster', 'contest-entries', contestId].
   - If any mutation in another file still invalidates the dead key, replace with the new one.

8. Mutation invalidation keys in LeagueDetailPage and TeamHome.
   - createContestEntryMutation and renameContestEntryMutation invalidations need to point at the live Contest Board cache key, not the deleted my-entries key.
   - VERIFY each onSuccess invalidation references a key that some page actually subscribes to.

9. extractErrorMessage helper duplication (also tracked as pool-master-dxd.17).
   - dxd.13.2 should have moved this to clients/poolmaster/src/lib/errors.ts.
   - VERIFY the inline copies in auth-home-page.tsx, league-detail-page.tsx, my-team-page.tsx are gone.
   - If any survived, finish the migration here. Then close pool-master-dxd.17.

10. setRecentLeagueCode call sites.
    - MyEntriesPage was a caller. After deletion, check the helper's remaining callers (LeagueDetailPage, MyTeamPage, Contest Board if it adds one).
    - Document the callers in a comment so future refactors keep the recent-league signal correct.

11. clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-detail-page.tsx vs the new Contest Board.
    - dxd.13.2 says 'repurpose existing ContestDetailPage'. After landing, verify only ONE file owns this route. If a stale ContestDetailPage and a new ContestBoardPage both exist, pick one and delete the other.

12. isHistoricalContest helper.
    - Currently duplicated in MyEntriesPage and LeagueDetailPage. After consolidation, single home — likely the Contest Board or a contest-status helper module.
    - Move to clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-status.ts (or similar) and re-export from the one place that needs it.

13. ListContestEntriesResponses type narrowing.
    - The enriched response has optional participants[] and required picksRevealed/picksCount. Anywhere the frontend destructures or maps over entries should handle the undefined case explicitly.
    - GREP: rg 'response\.data\.entries' OR 'entries\[' in clients/poolmaster/src and audit each site.

================================================================
TESTING / E2E REMNANTS
================================================================
14. clients/poolmaster/src/features/entries/ directory entirely gone.
    - VERIFY: ls clients/poolmaster/src/features/entries 2>&1 | grep 'No such file'.

15. e2e/ specs referencing /entries route or my-entries selectors.
    - GREP: rg '/entries' clients/poolmaster/e2e — update or delete.

16. Test fixtures or QA browser harness (pool-master-xw5 epic) referencing MyEntriesPage flows.
    - Coordinate with QA harness work to make sure no fixture seeds a test expecting MyEntriesPage's behavior.

================================================================
SUSPECTED REVIEW AREAS (not necessarily defects, worth a look)
================================================================
17. Other 'fan-out per contest' patterns elsewhere in the webapp.
    - GREP for: rg -nE 'Promise\.all\(.*contests' clients/poolmaster/src — confirm the Contest Board is the only place that legitimately fans out, and that's per-entry expansion (not per-contest).

18. Query key naming convention drift.
    - We have 'poolmaster' / 'leagues' / 'league-contests' / 'league-teams' / 'contest-entries' / 'contest-entry' etc. Now is a good moment to write a short 'query keys cheat sheet' rule (rules/react-ui-rules.md addendum?) before more pages adopt inconsistent shapes.

19. /league/:leagueCode/entries redirect — measure whether it's ever hit.
    - The redirect protects bookmarks, but if logs show zero hits after a release window, the redirect can later be removed and the path can 404 cleanly. (Not a defect; a future-cleanup signal.)

20. Memory file feedback_my_vs_league_scope.md and feedback_no_my_leagues_page.md.
    - Cross-reference both with the final implementation to confirm they remain accurate guidance for future work. Update if the consolidation altered any pattern they describe.

21. Authority-on-same-page treatment for the spotlighted 'my' rows.
    - Confirm the visual treatment (star/bold/etc.) and the create-entry/rename affordances satisfy the rule from feedback_list_to_home_authority_pattern.md. If not, raise a UX bead — do not silently leave a regression.

22. Live-update cadence on the Contest Board.
    - When picksRevealed === true, the participants[] data is live. The Contest Board needs an explicit staleTime / refetchInterval / websocket choice (TBD). Current default react-query staleTime is 0 — almost certainly wrong for a leaderboard. Document the chosen cadence in this slice's notes and link to where it lives in code.

23. Any backend test that previously read 'all entries see picks' as the contract should now read 'pre-start picks hidden from non-owners.'
    - Audit tests/functional and tests/integration for entry-detail assertions and confirm they reflect the new visibility rule rather than bypassing it.

================================================================
DEFINITION OF DONE
================================================================
- Each numbered item above is either resolved (with a one-line note) or explicitly marked 'kept, see explanation.'
- bd note recording the resolution per item.
- Quality gates per AGENTS.md pass.
- pool-master-dxd.17 (extractErrorMessage duplication) closes via item 9 if extracted in dxd.13.2 and confirmed here.

================================================================
SCOPE GUARDRAILS
================================================================
Out of scope for this bead:
- The 'live-update cadence' decision itself (item 22 documents what was picked, not picks anew).
- The 'query keys convention' rule write-up (item 18 surfaces it; the actual rule update is its own bead if the team chooses).
- Anything that turns into a UX-shape change (would belong with Pam, not here).
notes:
2026-04-28 stale-status cleanup: moved back from in_progress to open. This cleanup checklist is still valid work (for example buildLeagueEntriesPath still exists with only helper/test references), but it is not actively being implemented in the current batch.
Starting Batch 4 cleanup implementation after Batch 1 commit bff8802. Scope: verify/prune Contest Board consolidation remnants and record per-item cleanup notes before close.
Batch 4 cleanup resolution: (1) getMyContestEntry has no webapp callers; kept route for legacy/functional coverage and marked the OpenAPI operation deprecated with regenerated SDK artifacts. (2) getContest still has live contest board/manage/detail callers; kept. (3) optional participants are handled on Contest Board with hidden/empty states; audited listContestEntries consumers. (4) backend visibility helper already exists as contestPicksRevealed. (5) removed dead buildLeagueEntriesPath helper/test. (6-7) no my-entries selectors/query keys remain in webapp. (8) live invalidations target contest-entries. (9) extractErrorMessage migration already complete. (10) documented setRecentLeagueCode route-context callers. (11) one ContestDetailPage owns the Contest Board route. (12) extracted shared contest-status helper. (13) current frontend entry consumers tolerate missing participants. (14) no entries feature directory. (15-16) no webapp e2e entries-route/my-entries references found. (17-21,23) no blocking remnants found in this slice. (22) Contest Board polls contest entries every 30 seconds only while contest status is ACTIVE.
close_reason: Batch 4 cleanup implemented: stale helpers removed, contest status logic centralized, Contest Board live polling made explicit, legacy my-entry operation deprecated in OpenAPI, and checklist audit recorded in notes.

title: Icon picker modal markup is duplicated between LeagueDetailPage and MyTeamPage
description:
## Summary
Both LeagueDetailPage and MyTeamPage now embed ~110 lines of identical Radix Dialog scaffolding for the change-icon flow (open/close handlers, draft state, current-icon tile, grid of options, save/cancel buttons, error/pending states).

## Suggested approach
Extract '<IconPickerModal options={...} value={...} onChange={...} onSave={...} onClose={...} isPending={...} canEdit={...} renderOption={...} />' and drop the duplicated state plumbing in both pages.

## Files
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:945-1052
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:855-960
notes: Starting Batch 4 implementation: extract duplicated League/Team icon picker modal UI into a shared component with existing behavior preserved.
close_reason: Extracted duplicated league/team icon picker Radix modal markup into shared IconPickerModal while preserving page-specific save rules, icon rendering, and existing test ids.

title: extractErrorMessage is duplicated across pages with inconsistent special cases
description:
## Summary
'extractErrorMessage' (or a near-identical helper) is defined inline in at least three places with subtle drift:
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx:76-95 — generic 'Something went wrong' fallback.
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:64-91 — adds a special case for LEAGUE_LAST_COMMISSIONER_REQUIRED.
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:44-63 — different fallback copy.

The drift means a backend error code added to one page's whitelist is silently absent from the others.

## Suggested approach
Extract to '@/lib/errors.ts' that takes an optional code-to-message map for per-feature special cases.

## Files
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx:76-95
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:64-91
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:44-63
close_reason: Closed by pool-master-dxd.13.2: extractErrorMessage extracted to clients/poolmaster/src/lib/errors.ts; the inline copies in auth-home-page.tsx, league-detail-page.tsx, and my-team-page.tsx now delegate to the shared helper (special cases like LEAGUE_LAST_COMMISSIONER_REQUIRED travel through the codeMessages option).

title: League icon local state desyncs from leagueQuery cache after updateLeagueIcon
description:
## Summary
LeagueDetailPage tracks 'selectedIconKey' as local state that is hydrated by a useEffect on leagueQuery.data (clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:132-141), and updateIconMutation.onSuccess separately sets selectedIconKey AND calls syncLeagueCaches (:357-376). If the cache write produces a slightly different value than the mutation response, the local state wins until the leagueQuery effect re-runs.

## Suggested approach
Eliminate the redundant local state — read iconKey straight from leagueQuery.data. Keep only the modal-draft state ('iconDraftKey').

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:107-141, :357-376, :539-552
close_reason: Fixed. Removed duplicated selectedIconKey state from LeagueDetailPage; the current league icon is derived from leagueQuery/cache, while iconDraftKey remains the only local modal-edit draft. The draft sync now skips while the icon modal is open so unrelated query cache updates do not overwrite an in-flight icon selection. Existing updateLeagueIcon onSuccess writes the authoritative response through syncLeagueCaches. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- my-team-page.test.tsx league-detail-page.test.tsx.

title: AppShell and WelcomePage register the same leagues queryKey with conflicting retry policies
description:
## Summary
Both AppShell (clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.tsx:31-42) and WelcomePage (clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/leagues-page.tsx:16-25) register useQuery({ queryKey: ['poolmaster', 'leagues'] }) with different configurations:
- AppShell: retry: false, enabled: auth.isAuthenticated.
- WelcomePage: default retry, no enabled gate.

React Query merges by key, so behavior depends on mount order. This produces inconsistent retry behavior on auth loss, and at least one redundant request on every welcome-page view.

## Suggested approach
Extract a single 'useLeaguesQuery' hook (or a shared queryOptions object) used by both surfaces. Pick one retry policy and one enabled gate.

## Files
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.tsx:31-42
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/leagues-page.tsx:16-25
close_reason: Fixed. Extracted clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/use-leagues-query.ts and moved AppShell and WelcomePage onto the shared query key/fetcher/retry policy, so the same ['poolmaster','leagues'] query no longer has competing retry configuration. Added pool-master-dxd.15 regression coverage proving WelcomePage does not inherit retry behavior from QueryClient defaults. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- app-shell.test.tsx leagues-page.test.tsx.

title: MyTeamPage icon mutation onSuccess overwrites in-flight modal selection
description:
## Summary
In MyTeamPage, two writers update 'selectedIconKey':
1. updateTeamMutation.onSuccess sets it to the response iconKey (clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:225-243).
2. updateTeamIconMutation.onSuccess does the same (:245-264).

If a user opens the icon modal mid-flight (or fires the team-update flow then opens the modal before the response returns), the resolution overwrites the local 'selectedIconKey'. The downstream useEffect that resets 'iconDraftKey' only re-runs when 'selectedTeam' reference changes (:190-203), so the modal preview can fall out of sync with the underlying team state.

## Suggested approach
- Drop the local 'selectedIconKey' state and read directly from selectedTeam.iconKey (single source of truth), or
- Re-sync 'iconDraftKey' from 'selectedIconKey' inside an effect that depends on 'selectedIconKey' (not 'selectedTeam').

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx:73-264, :387-417
close_reason: Fixed. Removed duplicated selectedIconKey state from MyTeamPage; the current icon is now derived from the query-owned selectedTeam, with only iconDraftKey held locally for modal editing/new-team draft state. Team create/update/icon mutations update the ['poolmaster','league-teams', leagueId] cache from the authoritative mutation response instead of invalidating and briefly refetching stale list data. Added pool-master-dxd.14 traceability to the modal icon regression test. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- my-team-page.test.tsx.

title: League and Team icon editing should use modal flows from details tiles
description: Manual QA/UI defect. Icon editing currently lives inline in the main page layout. Expected behavior: show the current league icon in the League details tile with a Change icon action that opens a modal; saving the modal returns the user to the details page with the updated icon. Apply the same interaction paradigm to Team icon editing on Team Home. Actual behavior: icon selection is rendered inline on the page instead of a focused modal flow.
notes:
Frontend slice started 2026-04-25. Converting League Home and Team Home icon editing from inline pickers to modal-driven change-icon flows, with icon save returning to the page and focused frontend regression coverage for both surfaces.
Implemented locally on 2026-04-25. Moved League Home icon editing into a dedicated change-icon modal launched from the League details tile, and applied the same modal-driven icon flow to Team Home so icon save returns the user to the details surface with the updated icon. Added focused frontend coverage for both modal save flows. Validation: npx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx (from clients/poolmaster).
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. League Home and Team Home both use details-tile Change icon actions that open modal icon pickers, save through generated SDK mutations, and return to the details surface with updated icon state. Current modal tests cover both League and Team icon flows. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx/test and clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx/test.

Actual behavior: join policy and lifecycle are rendered in the top summary tile instead of the more specific lower tiles.
notes:

Removed join policy and lifecycle from the League Home summary tile, added join policy to the Commissioner invitations section, added current lifecycle status to the League lifecycle section, and added focused page coverage. Validation: npx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx (from clients/poolmaster).
Dispatched to worker on 2026-04-25 for frontend implementation/focused validation while main thread continues manual defect intake.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. League Home summary tile no longer renders Join policy or Lifecycle; Join policy is in the invitations section and lifecycle/current status is in the lifecycle section. Regression coverage exists in clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx.

Actual behavior: the user can land on a league page that does not resolve to an accessible league, producing a broken-context error state instead of automatic routing to a valid league homepage. Evidence: manual QA report accompanying the attached screenshot.
