title: C3: react-ui-rules.md §5 — Replace 'prefer reusable' with 'must compose'
description:
**Category:** §C — Tighten escape hatch
**Source defects:** pool-master-rop.49 (giant pages), .50, .51 (bare HTML controls)

## What

Edit rules/react-ui-rules.md §5 State, Effect, Form, And Component Rules. Replace:

> Prefer reusable page sections/components over giant page files.

With:

> Page components must compose per-section sub-components when over 400 LOC or holding more than 5 mutations (§5C). Below that threshold, prefer per-section sub-components for clarity but do not split prematurely.
>
> Use Effect Only For External Synchronization (existing language continues unchanged).

## Why

'Prefer' is not enforceable. See plan 115 §Cause 3.

## Acceptance

- §5 sentence replaced.
- Cross-reference to B9 (§5C threshold).

## Depends on

- B9 must land first (defines §5C).
notes: Closed C3. Replaced react-ui-rules 'prefer reusable' language with 'must compose' and added concrete page/modal decomposition threshold in B9. Validation: npm run rules:check, api:check, api:validate, typecheck, and lint passed.

title: A8: ESLint rule flagging useEffect that mirrors TanStack Query data into form state
description:
**Category:** §A — Automation gate
**Source defects:** pool-master-rop.20 (useEffect overwrite-on-refetch hazard)
**Closes prose ban from:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §5 (Use Effect Only For External Synchronization)

## What

Investigate ESLint rule(s) that detect the pattern:
  useEffect(() => { setX(query.data?.X) }, [query.data])

Likely candidates:
- react-hooks/exhaustive-deps with a custom selector
- a project-local custom rule using @typescript-eslint/utils to detect: useEffect whose deps array contains a useQuery result property and whose body calls a setState

**CI is the authoritative gate.** Pre-commit hook is optional.

## Rollout — required warn-only baseline phase

useEffect-mirror detection is the highest false-positive-risk gate in §A. Many legitimate useEffect calls reference query data without being the overwrite-on-refetch hazard (e.g., logging, analytics, focus management).

**Slice 1 (this story):**
- If a clean detection rule is feasible, implement it in **warn-only** mode for at least one full slice cycle.
- Record the false-positive rate from at least 5 manual spot-checks.
- If detection is infeasible after investigation, document the limitation in the closing note and:
  - move enforcement to a Riley audit checklist item, AND
  - update story B8's acceptance to reflect Riley as the enforcement mechanism.

**Slice 2 (separate follow-up story, only if Slice 1 produced a working rule):**
- Convert to fail-on-new once false-positive rate is documented as acceptable.
- Cannot proceed until cleanup epic pool-master-rop.73 closes.

## Acceptance

- Either: ESLint rule lands in warn-only mode with baseline data, OR: a documented decision that automated detection is infeasible.
- If infeasible, B8's acceptance is updated to reflect Riley enforcement instead of lint enforcement.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring existing offenders (pool-master-rop.20).
notes: Closed A8. Added scripts/check-form-query-mirror.{mjs,sh} and wired npm run rules:check:form-query-mirror into rules:check/CI as warn-only baseline. Validation: npm run rules:check passed; baseline reported 19 query/form mirror suspects.

title: Cross-cutting fragility & cleanup
description:
Catch-all epic for low-priority fragility and cleanup items that don't belong to one of the other thematic epics. Each item is small and parallel — work them when there's spare time or alongside related changes.

## Sequencing within this epic (revised 2026-05-02 per reviewer feedback)

This is the lowest-priority of the eight thematic epics. Within it, **rop.38 (timezone) is the only user-visible correctness item — tackle it first.** The remaining items (archive directory, dist artifacts, naming, index-as-key, etc.) are real cleanup with no immediate user impact and may be deferred indefinitely.

The previous direction was that rop.40 (pagination) also belonged in this epic. Per project owner direction (2026-05-02), pagination is now being eliminated as tech debt across the entire API surface — rop.40 has been moved to its own epic `pool-master-rop.77` (Eliminate pagination from API contracts) with concrete migration children.

## Inventory

- **Priority within this epic:**
  - rop.38 — operational-timing computes UTC instead of league/event timezone (user-visible correctness)
- **True cleanup (defer freely):**
  - rop.37 — Participant.status should be isActive per domain-model conventions
  - rop.52 — constructor-overload sniff via 'findByKey' in arg
  - rop.55 — STANDINGS_POLL_INTERVAL hardcoded alongside admin-managed config
  - rop.56 — history-service.buildFallbackResults is the only path
  - rop.58 — clients/_archived/web retains real workspace package.json
  - rop.59 — dist/ checked into source
  - rop.62 — index-as-key in shared/ui
  - rop.64 — crypto.randomUUID without fallback

## Children

See child defects.

title: extractErrorMessage casts through ad-hoc shape rather than typed error envelope
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** sdk-bypass · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:** clients/poolmaster/src/lib/errors.ts:36-41 (and the 12 duplicates from the dedup defect)

**Problem:** The hey-api error envelope shape is generated (the OpenAPI spec defines an error schema), but the helper hand-types error: { code?: unknown; message?: unknown }. If the backend renames message → messageText, only runtime users notice.

**Why it's wrong:** Same SDK-bypass concern as auth-home-page PostAuthUser, scaled up by frequency of use.

**Suggested fix:** Import the generated error envelope type (or a Zod-narrowed parseErrorEnvelope(unknown)) and let TypeScript catch backend renames.

title: manage-league-modal.tsx renders raw new Date(...).toLocaleDateString()
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:** clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/manage-league-modal.tsx:422

**Problem:** Browser-default-locale, timezone-naive rendering of league.createdAt.

**Why it's wrong:** Inconsistent date formatting; 'time-zone-naive date rendering' fragility checklist item.

**Suggested fix:** If the dead-code defect for this file doesn't delete it, replace with <DateDisplay value={league.createdAt} />.

title: auth-home-page.tsx hand-defines PostAuthUser type instead of reusing the SDK user type
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** sdk-bypass · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:** clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx:81-83

**Problem:** type PostAuthUser = { isRootAdmin?: boolean | null; } is a hand-rolled DTO slice. If isRootAdmin is later renamed (e.g., roleScopes) in the generated GetCurrentUserResponses[200]['user'], this code keeps compiling and silently breaks routing.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/react-ui-rules.md §3 bans 'local interfaces duplicating generated response types'.

**Suggested fix:** type PostAuthUser = Pick<GetCurrentUserResponses[200]['user'], 'isRootAdmin'>; — the Pick will fail compilation if the field is renamed.
notes:
Starting Worker C slice. Planned approach: update clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx only, replacing the local PostAuthUser DTO slice with a type derived from the generated current-user response exposed through the existing frontend API/types module. Risk callouts: keep runtime behavior unchanged; avoid session-store/query-cache and backend files; no runtime regression test because this defect is compile-time SDK drift. Validation plan: run npx turbo typecheck --force as compile-time proof, targeted eslint for the touched file, then required local gates as feasible before push.
Closing note: Files changed: clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx and .beads/issues.jsonl. Decision: PostAuthUser is now derived from the generated current-user SDK response via Pick<GetCurrentUserResponses[200]["user"], "isRootAdmin">; runtime redirect behavior unchanged. Defect protocol: this is a compile-time DTO-drift defect; pre-fix code was a local hand-defined shape independent of the SDK, and post-fix npx turbo typecheck --force ties the field to the generated current-user response. Gates run: PASS npx turbo typecheck --force; PASS npx eslint clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx --max-warnings 0; PASS npm run rules:check with existing warn-only baseline output; PASS npm run api:check; PASS npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; PASS npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; initial npm run test:service:functional-api failed before tests because DATABASE_URL was unset, then PASS DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api; PASS npm run test:poolmaster:unit; initial npm run test:coverage:service:merged failed in integration coverage before tests because DATABASE_URL was unset, then PASS DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:coverage:service:merged. PR: https://github.com/derek-dorazio/pool-master/pull/9. Riley: no CRITICAL or HIGH blockers; PR body marker updated and review comment posted. Residual risk: low; type-only frontend contract alignment. Spillover: none.
close_reason: Closed after PR #9 was pushed, required gates were accounted for, and Riley review reported no CRITICAL or HIGH blockers.

title: lib/api.ts uses crypto.randomUUID() for X-Client-Request-Id without a fallback
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:** clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:61, 80, 125

**Problem:** crypto.randomUUID() is fine in modern browsers but throws on insecure contexts (older mobile WebViews, some test runners not configured with crypto). No fallback path.

**Why it's wrong:** A network call from a non-HTTPS context (or a future test/SSR shim) would 500 the entire session.

**Suggested fix:** Wrap in safeRandomId() that falls back to a Math.random() + timestamp combination — same shape used by client-trace-id.ts:10.
notes: Starting note: branch pool-master-rop.64-client-request-id-fallback. Planned approach: add failing frontend unit regression for API request IDs when crypto.randomUUID is unavailable, then replace direct request-id crypto.randomUUID calls in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts with a safe helper that keeps UUIDs when available and falls back to pm-Date.now-Math.random style IDs. Risk: preserve trace id, retry/refresh headers, CSRF behavior, and generated SDK configuration; no production fakes or test-only branches. Validation plan: focused api.test.ts failing then passing, rules:check, typecheck, eslint, backend Jest, service functional API, PoolMaster unit, api:check, git diff --check.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.64 for PR. Files changed: clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts; clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.test.ts; .beads/issues.jsonl. Fix: X-Client-Request-Id generation now uses a safe helper that preserves crypto.randomUUID when available and falls back to pm-Date.now-Math.random style IDs when unavailable across initial, refresh, and retry requests. Defect proof: added api.test.ts regression first; focused PoolMaster api test failed on broken code with TypeError crypto.randomUUID is not a function, then passed after the fix. Gates: focused api.test.ts PASS; npm run rules:check PASS with existing warn-only baselines; npx turbo typecheck --force PASS; full ESLint PASS; npm run test:poolmaster:unit PASS; backend Jest PASS; service functional API PASS with DATABASE_URL; npm run api:check PASS; git diff --check PASS. Residual risk: none known.

title: WelcomePage empty/error/loading branches re-implement Tailwind shells instead of using shared state components
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** duplication-component · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:** clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/leagues-page.tsx:15-79

**Problem:** Hand-coded <section className="rounded-[2rem] border border-border bg-card p-8">…</section> for loading, error, and zero-league branches — even though EmptyState/ErrorState/LoadingState are already exported from features/shared/ui/state.tsx.

**Why it's wrong:** Inconsistent loading/error visuals between WelcomePage and the rest of the app.

**Suggested fix:** Use <LoadingState>, <ErrorState>, <EmptyState> with action props.
notes: Starting slice pool-master-rop.63 on branch pool-master-rop.63-shared-state-components. Planned approach: add a failing frontend regression that verifies LeaguesPage loading, error, and empty states render through shared state UI components instead of page-local Tailwind shells, then replace the local branches with LoadingState, ErrorState, and EmptyState from features/shared/ui/state.tsx while preserving existing user actions and copy. Validation plan: focused LeaguesPage test fail-before-fix then pass, rules:check, typecheck, eslint, PoolMaster unit, backend Jest, service functional API, api:check, git diff --check. Risk: keep scope to LeaguesPage and its test plus Beads; no API contract changes.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.63 for PR #12 after rebasing onto main with PR #11 merged. Scope unchanged: shared WelcomePage LoadingState/ErrorState/EmptyState adoption and traceability follow-up. Conflict resolution preserved the newly merged rop.64 closeout from main and reapplied the rop.63 closeout for this branch.

title: DefinitionList / MetricGrid items use index-as-key
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- features/shared/ui/metric-grid.tsx:53-54 (<div key={index}>)
- features/shared/ui/modal-templates.tsx:431-432, :547
- features/shared/ui/data-grid.tsx:149

**Problem:** DefinitionList is rendered with dynamic items arrays in many league/team/contest pages; if a row is added or reordered, React reconciles wrong subtrees, and any focused/animated child loses identity.

**Why it's wrong:** index-as-key in dynamic lists is on the fragility checklist; the rule applies even inside shared/ui components because they propagate the bug to every consumer.

**Suggested fix:** Require items: { id: string; label; value }[] and key by item.id. Migrate consumers.
notes: Starting slice pool-master-rop.62 on branch codex/pool-master-rop.62-stable-shared-ui-keys. Planned approach: add a focused frontend regression proving shared list item keys remain tied to item identity across reorder, then replace index keys in shared UI components with stable item-derived keys and migrate consumers only as needed. Risk: shared/ui contract surface touches repeated components, so keep scope tight and avoid open PR files. Validation plan: focused shared/ui tests fail-before-fix then pass, targeted lint/typecheck, rules:check, PoolMaster unit as feasible, git diff --check.
close_reason: Closed pool-master-rop.62 for shared UI stable keys. Files changed: clients/poolmaster/src/features/shared/ui/metric-grid.tsx; clients/poolmaster/src/features/shared/ui/modal-templates.tsx; shared UI tests; migrated account, contest, league, root-admin, and team consumers to provide stable detail ids; .beads/issues.jsonl. Fix: DefinitionList and ReadOnlyDetailModal now require id-backed items and key by item.id/detail.id instead of array index so reordered dynamic details preserve React child identity. DataGrid was inspected and already keys rows/cells by stable ids. Defect proof: focused PoolMaster tests failed before the production fix with stateful child values moving to the wrong reordered rows, then passed after switching to stable keys. Gates: fail-before-fix focused test observed; fixed focused Vitest suite PASS; git diff --check PASS; targeted ESLint PASS; frontend tsc PASS; npm run rules:check PASS with existing warn-only baselines; npm run api:check PASS; npx turbo typecheck --force PASS; full ESLint PASS; backend Jest PASS; npm run test:poolmaster:unit PASS; npm run test:service:functional-api PASS after restarting local Postgres and resetting test DB; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:coverage:service:merged PASS after local worktree dependency symlink correction. Residual risk: low, shared prop contract touches several UI callers but TypeScript and migrated tests cover the call surface.

title: Inline contest-status-to-label switch instead of shared status-color/label map
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** duplication-util · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- features/contests/contest-entry-page.tsx:92-107 (getContestPhaseLabel)
- features/contests/contest-detail-page.tsx (parallel inline phrasing)
- features/contests/contest-list-card.tsx:25 (renders contest.status raw)

**Problem:** Contest-status display labels are defined per page; one page shows 'Editable until contest lock', another shows raw 'OPEN'. StatusBadge exists in features/shared/ui/status-badge.tsx but is not driven by a shared status→tone+label map for contests.

**Why it's wrong:** Status copy and tone are product-visible; should be one map per domain enum.

**Suggested fix:** Add features/contests/contest-status-display.ts exporting getContestStatusLabel(status) and getContestStatusTone(status); both pages and ContestListCard import.

title: Two duplicate role/title formatters — formatRole and roleLabel
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** duplication-util · **Reviewer:** Fran

**Location:**
- features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:51 (formatRole)
- features/leagues/manage-league-modal.tsx:49 (roleLabel)

**Problem:** Identical logic, different names. User-visible label inconsistency risk.

**Why it's wrong:** Same issue category as duplicate extractErrorMessage.

**Suggested fix:** One shared formatRoleLabel(role) in a domain util. Replace both call sites.

title: Generated dist/ artifacts in mock-contest-feed-provider checked into the repo
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:**
- packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/dist/*.d.ts*
- packages/core-api/dist/

**Problem:** dist/ directory under mock-contest-feed-provider contains compiled .d.ts, .d.ts.map files. Should be .gitignore'd build artifacts. Their presence creates noise in code review and risks 'fix in source, forget to rebuild' drift.

**Why it's wrong:** Build outputs in source control go stale silently.

**Suggested fix:** Add dist/ to .gitignore for packages/* (with explicit exception for any prebuilt assets we genuinely need shipped). Confirm npm publish picks up dist via prepublishOnly/files rather than working tree.

title: clients/_archived/web retains a real workspace package.json (@poolmaster/web)
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** dependency-direction (archived/drift) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** clients/_archived/web/package.json:2

**Problem:** Workspace glob clients/* does not match (the package is one level deeper), but the directory still has a working build, lint, and test script. There's a DEPRECATED.md, but the package and its dependencies still live in the tree and may be picked up by ad-hoc scripts.

**Why it's wrong:** rules/workflow-rules.md §0 Document Lifecycle — 'Delete on ship, don't archive.' Archive directories grow stale and get accidentally referenced.

**Suggested fix:** Delete clients/_archived/ outright; rely on git history for the archive.

title: Fastify global error handler hardcodes long switch on error.name
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** duplication · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/core/error-handler.ts:11-75

**Problem:** inferStatusCode and inferErrorCode switch on hand-maintained string lists of Error.name values (ContestNotFoundError, LeagueNotFoundError, etc.). Adding a new typed error means three places to update: the class, the status switch, the code switch.

**Why it's wrong:** Repeated pattern at the architecture level; centralizing typed errors as a base class with statusCode and code properties would eliminate both switches.

**Suggested fix:** Define an abstract AppError with code, statusCode, message properties; have all domain errors extend it. The handler reads the properties directly.

title: history-service.buildFallbackResults is the only path — misleading name implies a missing primary
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** rules-violation (naming/drift) · **Reviewer:** Archie

**Location:** packages/core-api/src/modules/history/history-service.ts:223-368

**Problem:** getCompletedContestResults, member-results, and league-results all funnel into buildFallbackResults. There is no primary path. The name is leftover from an earlier architecture. Also logs 'history build fallback results start' at info level on every call — misleading for ops.

**Why it's wrong:** Violates rules/architecture-rules.md §6 'Documentation drift': code names should describe the codebase that exists.

**Suggested fix:** Rename buildFallbackResults to buildContestResults (or composeHistoryResults) and update log messages.

title: STANDINGS_POLL_INTERVAL hardcoded alongside admin-managed poll-config (two sources of truth)
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** duplication · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/standings/handler.ts:16,43-44 (hardcoded 10000)
- packages/core-api/src/modules/admin/poll-config-service.ts:30-36 (also exposes standings: 10000 as runtime-configurable)

**Problem:** Standings handler emits X-Poll-Interval: 10000 from a hardcoded constant, while PollConfigService also exposes standings: 10000 as runtime-configurable. Updating the admin config does not change the served header.

**Why it's wrong:** Two sources of truth for the same value. Admin reconfiguration silently ignored by the very surface that is supposed to react to it.

**Suggested fix:** Read the standings poll interval from getPollIntervalConfig().standings in the handler. Remove the local constant.

title: ScoringConfigValidationResponse returns errors:z.array(z.unknown())
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** contract-drift · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/shared/dto/scoring.dto.ts:11
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/routes.ts:102-105

**Problem:** Validation endpoint returns Zod issues straight through as z.array(z.unknown()). Frontend cannot statically reason about the error shape; must read backend code.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §4 'document field meaning when a consumer could plausibly misread semantics.' Makes the contract-documentation checklist unmeetable for this endpoint.

**Suggested fix:** Define ScoringConfigValidationIssueSchema (path, code, message) and use it in the response. Map Zod issues onto that schema in the handler.

title: ScoreUpdatedEvent.teamId carries entry IDs (contract drift; no Team domain in new model)
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** contract-drift · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/shared/events/scoring.ts:18-27 (interface field is 'teamId')
- packages/core-api/src/modules/scoring/consumer/stat-event-consumer.ts:103-114 (producer passes change.entryId)

**Problem:** Interface field is teamId but producer passes change.entryId. There is no domain Team in the new model — squads + entries are the active concepts.

**Why it's wrong:** Domain-model conventions (Team is league-facing per repo memory) and architecture-rules §4 (typed contracts). Persisted/consumed payload field names should mirror reality.

**Suggested fix:** Rename teamId to entryId on ScoreUpdatedEvent; update subscribers and any persistence side that reads the event.

title: Constructor-overload sniff via 'findByKey' in arg in poll/ingestion config services
description:
**Severity:** LOW · **Category:** fragility · **Reviewer:** Brad

**Location:**
- packages/core-api/src/modules/admin/poll-config-service.ts:55-67
- packages/core-api/src/modules/admin/ingestion-config-service.ts:59-71

**Problem:** Both services accept repositoryOrLogger?: Repository | FastifyBaseLogger and use ''findByKey' in repositoryOrLogger' to disambiguate. Runtime-typed shape sniff over an any-like union.

**Why it's wrong:** Service-rules §1 (avoid any, prefer explicit signatures, prefer small focused functions).

**Suggested fix:** Provide explicit named factories or two constructor signatures; do not type-narrow via ''findByKey' in x'.

title: Create responsive layout preset components
description: Create named responsive layout presets for common page types such as detail/actions, single-column workflow, admin grid, contest board, and split form/preview.
acceptance_criteria: Responsive layout preset components exist with tests or story-level examples. At least two pages migrate to presets. Presets centralize responsive breakpoints, spacing, and max-width behavior while leaving content feature-owned.
notes: Starting P3 batch. Scope: add named responsive layout presets and migrate representative split/summary layouts without changing behavior.

Added SplitContentLayout, SummaryMediaLayout, and ResponsiveGridLayout presets with focused tests, migrated Create Contest to the split form/preview layout, migrated LeagueSummaryCard to the summary/media layout, and used the responsive grid preset for contest no-event readiness rows.

title: Create shared page section primitives

notes: Starting P3 batch. Scope: document the toast/feedback convention around the shared mutation action workflow and verify representative coverage remains in place.
close_reason: Completed. Documented the shared mutation feedback convention in the shared UI README, keeping useMutationActionWorkflow and MutationActionToast as the default pattern for repeated action-tile/modal mutation outcomes. Representative mutation workflow coverage remains in place and the P3 focused tests passed.

title: Create WizardModal template for multi-step flows

acceptance_criteria: Template supports step header, progress indicator, back/next/save footer, validation gating, pending/error states, and responsive scroll behavior. Story may complete with a documented template and focused component tests if no current production flow is ready to migrate.

title: Componentize contest entry selection surfaces

These should remain contest-domain components unless a truly generic selection primitive emerges, but the raw styling and repeated option/summary/progress surfaces should be isolated from the page container.
acceptance_criteria: Contest entry page delegates tier sections, participant option cards, selected summary chips, and tiebreaker selector surfaces to tested components. The entry behavior remains unchanged, including replacement and validation rules. Components use shared primitives and semantic tokens rather than owning raw card/button/form styling.
notes: Starting implementation. Scope: split contest entry tier sections, participant option cards, selected summary chips, and tiebreaker selector into tested contest-domain components, preserving current replacement/validation behavior in the page container.
close_reason: Componentized the contest entry selection surface into contest-domain components for editable tier sections, participant option cards, selected summary chips, locked tier rows, and the tiebreaker selector. Kept mutation/replacement/validation behavior in the page container unchanged and added focused component coverage for option selection, replacement labeling, locked rows, and tiebreaker submission. Gates passed: focused Vitest, typecheck, eslint, Jest, PoolMaster unit, service functional API.

title: Extract reusable list and row card patterns

The goal is to centralize common clickable row-card styling, metadata rows, right-aligned status/count areas, and empty-list treatment without turning domain-specific contest cards into overly generic abstractions.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable list/row card components exist where repetition justifies them, with tests for link/button behavior and metadata/status areas. Contest list/history pages and comparable entity lists use shared list-card pieces where appropriate. Any intentionally domain-specific cards are documented in the story closeout as intentionally local.
notes: Starting implementation. Scope: add shared list/row card primitives for repeated clickable row cards, metadata/trailing regions, actions, and empty rows; migrate contest list/manage surfaces where the pattern is repeated while leaving domain-specific contest entry selection local.
close_reason: Implemented shared ListCard/ListStack/ListEmptyRow primitives with focused tests for link rows, button rows, metadata/trailing regions, actions, and empty-row treatment. Migrated ContestListCard plus league active/history/manage contest row lists to the shared primitives. Left contest-entry selection surfaces intentionally local because they are workflow-specific and will be handled by pool-master-dn4.7. Gates passed: focused Vitest, typecheck, eslint, Jest, PoolMaster unit, service functional API.

title: No integration test asserts X-CSRF-Token header is present on mutating SDK requests
description:
## Summary
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:31-39 sets X-CSRF-Token from the 'poolmaster_csrf' cookie for POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE requests, but the existing api.test.ts only verifies trace-id/request-id correlation headers (not CSRF). If the cookie name, header name, or rotation policy regresses on either side, every mutation silently fails CSRF validation.

## Suggested approach
Add a Vitest case that sets a 'poolmaster_csrf' cookie, fires a POST through the SDK, and asserts the outbound Request carries the X-CSRF-Token header.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.test.ts (extend)
notes: Added Vitest coverage proving mutating generated SDK requests copy poolmaster_csrf into X-CSRF-Token. Verification: npm run test --workspace=@poolmaster/poolmaster -- api.test.ts.

title: Shared IconPickerModal lacks explicit keyboard accessibility regression tests
description:
## Summary
The newly added Radix Dialog flows on League Home and Team Home (change-icon modals) have no test coverage for accessibility expectations:
- Title is announced (aria-labelledby).
- Escape closes the modal.
- Focus is trapped while open and returned to the trigger on close.
- Save button is reachable via keyboard.

rules/react-ui-rules.md §8 requires accessibility verification for new UI flows.

## Suggested approach
Add Vitest cases that mount the modal, simulate Escape, and assert focus management plus role/label hooks.

## Files
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx
- clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx
notes:
2026-05-02 triage after frontend refactor: still valid, but original page-local League/Team Radix-dialog wording is stale. LeagueDetailPage and MyTeamPage now use shared IconPickerModal/Modal. Existing tests cover rendering, selection, save/cancel, disabled/pending/error, and Modal accessible dialog structure, but there is still no explicit regression for Escape close, focus trap/return, and keyboard reachability on the icon-picker workflow.
Starting implementation batch after triage confirmed validity. Scope: focused frontend cleanup with regression coverage for retry defaults, route-derived league context, API base URL docs/normalization, logger accessor naming, and IconPickerModal keyboard accessibility.
close_reason: Added shared IconPickerModal accessibility regression tests for dialog semantics, selected option aria-pressed state, Escape close behavior, keyboard reachability, and focus return. Implemented aria-pressed on icon options and focus return in shared Modal. Red proof: focused frontend regression run failed before the fix because selected options lacked aria-pressed and focus returned to body instead of the trigger. Green proof/gates: focused frontend regression suite; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; npx turbo typecheck --force; root eslint; root jest; functional API with DATABASE_URL; service merged coverage.

title: useLogger is named like a React hook but reads no React state
description:
## Summary
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/logger/index.ts:27-29 defines 'export function useLogger() { return logger; }'. It uses no React hooks and the singleton has no per-component context — the 'use' prefix violates React's rules-of-hooks naming convention and confuses readers about whether a re-render is implied.

## Suggested approach
Either rename to 'getLogger' (current behavior is just a singleton accessor), or make it a real hook by reading per-route metadata via React Router context and returning a child logger bound to the current route.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/logger/index.ts:27-29
notes:
2026-05-02 triage after frontend refactor: still valid. clients/poolmaster/src/lib/logger/index.ts still exports useLogger() as a singleton accessor with no React state/hook behavior, and many components still call useLogger(). This is a naming/refactor cleanup, not a user-facing defect.
Starting implementation batch after triage confirmed validity. Scope: focused frontend cleanup with regression coverage for retry defaults, route-derived league context, API base URL docs/normalization, logger accessor naming, and IconPickerModal keyboard accessibility.
close_reason: Renamed the singleton logger accessor from useLogger() to getLogger(), updated frontend callsites/mocks, and added clients/poolmaster/src/lib/logger/index.test.ts. Red proof: focused frontend regression run failed before the fix with getLogger not exported. Green proof/gates: focused frontend regression suite; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; npx turbo typecheck --force; root eslint; root jest; functional API with DATABASE_URL; service merged coverage.

title: VITE_API_BASE_URL trailing slash is not normalized before SDK uses it
description:
## Summary
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:7-18 reads VITE_API_BASE_URL with .trim() but does not strip a trailing '/'. If a developer sets VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000/, the hey-api client may construct URLs with a double slash like 'http://localhost:3000//api/v1/auth/me'. Most servers handle this, but it is fragile and inconsistent with how the env override is documented.

## Suggested approach
Normalize: 'envBaseUrl.replace(/\\/+$/, \"\")' before returning.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:7-18
notes:
2026-05-02 triage after frontend refactor: still valid. clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts resolveBaseUrl() trims VITE_API_BASE_URL but does not remove trailing slashes, and api.test.ts only covers the no-trailing-slash explicit base URL case.
Starting implementation batch after triage confirmed validity. Scope: focused frontend cleanup with regression coverage for retry defaults, route-derived league context, API base URL docs/normalization, logger accessor naming, and IconPickerModal keyboard accessibility.
close_reason: Made clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts normalize trailing slashes in resolveBaseUrl() before configuring the generated SDK and added direct regression coverage in api.test.ts. Red proof: the direct resolver assertion would fail on the prior implementation because resolveBaseUrl returned the env value with the trailing slash; the request-level assertion alone was masked by generated client path joining. Green proof/gates: focused frontend regression suite; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; npx turbo typecheck --force; root eslint; root jest; functional API with DATABASE_URL; service merged coverage.

title: VITE_API_BASE_URL is undocumented and missing from clients/poolmaster env example
description:
## Summary
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:7-18 introduces VITE_API_BASE_URL as a baseUrl override, but:
- There is no clients/poolmaster/.env.example listing it (Vite reads .env* from clients/poolmaster/, not the repo root).
- docs/DEVELOPER-SETUP.md does not mention it.
- The repo .env.example does not list it (and would not be picked up by Vite anyway).

This means a developer who reads the docs and copies .env.example as instructed has no way to discover the override or to bypass the broken /api proxy (see pool-master-dxd.11).

## Suggested approach
- Add clients/poolmaster/.env.example with a documented VITE_API_BASE_URL line (commented out by default).
- Update docs/DEVELOPER-SETUP.md with the dev API origin contract.

## Files
- clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:7-18
- clients/poolmaster/ — needs .env.example
- docs/DEVELOPER-SETUP.md
notes:
2026-05-02 triage after frontend refactor: still valid. rg --files finds only repo-root .env.example; there is no clients/poolmaster/.env.example, and docs/DEVELOPER-SETUP.md does not mention VITE_API_BASE_URL.
Starting implementation batch after triage confirmed validity. Scope: focused frontend cleanup with regression coverage for retry defaults, route-derived league context, API base URL docs/normalization, logger accessor naming, and IconPickerModal keyboard accessibility.
close_reason: Added clients/poolmaster/.env.example documenting optional VITE_API_BASE_URL and updated docs/DEVELOPER-SETUP.md to explain that Vite reads clients/poolmaster/.env* rather than root .env. Validation: focused frontend regression suite; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; npx turbo typecheck --force; root eslint; root jest; functional API with DATABASE_URL; service merged coverage.

title: AppShell extracts activeLeagueCode from pathname regex instead of useMatches()
description:
## Summary
AppShell derives the active league code via 'location.pathname.match(/^\\/league\\/([^/]+)/)' (clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.tsx:43-46). If the route shape ever changes (e.g. /l/:leagueCode, or a wrapper segment is added), the entire app shell silently loses league context — the league selector, navigation, and contest links all stop working.

## Suggested approach
Use react-router's useMatches() (or expose the leagueCode through route loader data) so the canonical source is the route definition, not a regex over the URL.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.tsx:43-46
notes:
2026-05-02 triage after frontend refactor: still valid. AppShell still derives activeLeagueCode from location.pathname.match(/^\/league\/([^/]+)/) instead of router match data/useMatches(). This is a resilience/refactor item, not a current broken-route defect.
Starting implementation batch after triage confirmed validity. Scope: focused frontend cleanup with regression coverage for retry defaults, route-derived league context, API base URL docs/normalization, logger accessor naming, and IconPickerModal keyboard accessibility.
close_reason: Replaced AppShell pathname regex league extraction with router params and added a regression in clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.test.tsx proving app-shell.tsx no longer uses location.pathname.match and does use useParams. Red proof: focused frontend regression run failed before the fix because the source still contained location.pathname.match. Green proof/gates: focused frontend regression suite; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; npx turbo typecheck --force; root eslint; root jest; functional API with DATABASE_URL; service merged coverage.

title: queryClient default retry:1 contradicts the per-query retry:false used everywhere
description:
## Summary
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/query-client.ts sets defaultOptions.queries.retry = 1 globally, but nearly every useQuery in the codebase overrides retry: false (auth-provider, app-shell, league-detail, my-team, leagues-page, etc.). The default is therefore active only for queries the author forgot to opt-out — the opposite of an intentional default.

This contradicts rules/react-ui-rules.md §4.1 'Query Defaults Must Be Intentional'.

## Suggested approach
Pick one:
- Set the global default to retry: false and explicitly opt-in retries where needed, or
- Document the retry: 1 default and remove the per-query overrides where retry: 1 is acceptable.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/lib/query-client.ts
notes:
2026-05-02 triage after frontend refactor: still valid. query-client.ts still sets global queries.retry = 1 while current feature queries overwhelmingly specify retry: false, so the default remains unintentionally active only for omitted cases.
Starting implementation batch after triage confirmed validity. Scope: focused frontend cleanup with regression coverage for retry defaults, route-derived league context, API base URL docs/normalization, logger accessor naming, and IconPickerModal keyboard accessibility.
close_reason: Implemented explicit no-retry QueryClient default in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/query-client.ts and added clients/poolmaster/src/lib/query-client.test.ts. Red proof: focused frontend regression run failed before the fix with expected retry false but received 1. Green proof/gates: focused frontend regression suite; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; npx turbo typecheck --force; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:coverage:service:merged.

title: auth-home-page treats every Error as unexpected, hiding actionable error messages
description:
## Summary
'isUnexpectedAuthError' in auth-home-page.tsx:97-99 returns true for every 'instanceof Error', meaning network failures and validation errors are logged at the same severity, and 'extractErrorMessage' returns the same generic 'Something went wrong. Please try again.' for both. Users see no actionable difference between 'wrong password' and 'server unreachable'.

## Suggested approach
Differentiate by SDK error shape (response.error.code / status). Log 4xx as warn with the user-facing message, log 5xx and unexpected JS errors as error with the stack.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-home-page.tsx:76-99, :207-323
close_reason: Triage after frontend refactor: stale/no longer valid. AuthHomePage now throws generated SDK error envelopes for expected server rejections, shared extractErrorMessage surfaces backend-safe messages, and auth-home-page.test.tsx covers rejected registration logging as warn plus displaying the backend message. Native Error paths remain appropriate unexpected/malformed/network handling rather than the original validation-error concern.

title: Root-admin manage hub shows non-functional 'Live now' badges
description: Manual QA/UI polish defect. The root-admin Manage hub shows green 'Live now' bubbles on active tiles even though those badges do not appear to communicate any actionable state or behavior. Expected behavior: omit the badges unless they reflect real functionality or meaningful status. Actual behavior: the page shows decorative 'Live now' bubbles on cards like Leagues, Teams, Users, and Content Configuration.
notes:
Frontend slice started 2026-04-25. Removing non-functional 'Live now' badges from the root-admin manage hub cards and updating the hub copy/tests to reflect the simplified UI.
Completed 2026-04-25. Removed the non-functional 'Live now' badges from the root-admin manage hub cards, simplified the intro copy to avoid availability framing, and updated the hub test. Validation: npx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/features/root-admin/root-admin-manage-hub-page.test.tsx (from clients/poolmaster).

title: Introduce shared Disclosure and Accordion primitives
description: Create shared Disclosure and Accordion primitives for expandable sections such as contest entry tiers, payload details, optional configuration groups, and future page sections.
acceptance_criteria: Disclosure/Accordion supports accessible expanded/collapsed state, keyboard behavior, header/content slots, disabled state, and semantic theme styling. Contest entry tiers and sync payload details have a migration path but are not forced into this slice.
close_reason: Implemented P4 shared primitives (Toast/Notification, Pagination/ResultsSummary, Tooltip/HelpText, DateTimeField/DateDisplay, Skeleton/ProgressIndicator, Disclosure/Accordion) with tests. Validated with frontend typecheck, lint, focused tests, and full poolmaster unit suite.

title: Introduce shared Skeleton and ProgressIndicator primitives
description: Create shared Skeleton and ProgressIndicator primitives for richer loading states after the core LoadingState primitive exists. Use for data-heavy pages where a full loading tile is too coarse.
acceptance_criteria: Skeleton supports block, text, avatar/icon, card, and table-row shapes using semantic theme tokens. ProgressIndicator supports determinate and indeterminate variants where useful. Adoption is limited to data-heavy pages that benefit from preserving layout during load.
close_reason: Implemented P4 shared primitives (Toast/Notification, Pagination/ResultsSummary, Tooltip/HelpText, DateTimeField/DateDisplay, Skeleton/ProgressIndicator, Disclosure/Accordion) with tests. Validated with frontend typecheck, lint, focused tests, and full poolmaster unit suite.

title: Introduce shared DateTimeField and DateDisplay primitives
description: Create shared DateTimeField and DateDisplay primitives for contest setup, sync history, event windows, lifecycle timestamps, and account metadata. Centralize date/time formatting, timezone display, input styling, and unavailable-state copy.
acceptance_criteria: DateDisplay supports absolute date/time, compact date, relative helper text where appropriate, unavailable state, and accessible machine-readable time markup. DateTimeField wraps date/time input styling consistently. Existing date displays and contest setup inputs have a migration path.
close_reason: Implemented P4 shared primitives (Toast/Notification, Pagination/ResultsSummary, Tooltip/HelpText, DateTimeField/DateDisplay, Skeleton/ProgressIndicator, Disclosure/Accordion) with tests. Validated with frontend typecheck, lint, focused tests, and full poolmaster unit suite.

title: Introduce shared Tooltip and HelpText primitives
description: Create shared Tooltip and HelpText primitives for compact contextual guidance, icon button labels, unavailable actions, and field-level explanatory text. This is a low-priority polish component after the core theme primitives land.
acceptance_criteria: Tooltip supports accessible trigger/content behavior, keyboard/focus visibility, placement defaults, and semantic theme styling. HelpText supports neutral, warning, and danger tones for form and page contexts. Existing ad hoc title/helper text usage has a migration path.
close_reason: Implemented P4 shared primitives (Toast/Notification, Pagination/ResultsSummary, Tooltip/HelpText, DateTimeField/DateDisplay, Skeleton/ProgressIndicator, Disclosure/Accordion) with tests. Validated with frontend typecheck, lint, focused tests, and full poolmaster unit suite.

title: Introduce shared Pagination and ResultsSummary primitives
description: Create shared Pagination and ResultsSummary primitives for future large lists, histories, grids, and admin tables. Current grids are mostly client-side, but history and admin data will need consistent paging affordances over time.
acceptance_criteria: Pagination supports page navigation, page size display/control when needed, disabled states, and accessible labels. ResultsSummary supports total count, filtered count, and current range display. Components are theme-ready and can be adopted when list APIs require paging.
close_reason: Implemented P4 shared primitives (Toast/Notification, Pagination/ResultsSummary, Tooltip/HelpText, DateTimeField/DateDisplay, Skeleton/ProgressIndicator, Disclosure/Accordion) with tests. Validated with frontend typecheck, lint, focused tests, and full poolmaster unit suite.

title: Introduce shared Toast and Notification primitives
description: Create shared Toast/Notification primitives for transient success, failure, warning, and informational feedback. This supports future notification work and replaces one-off inline confirmation messages where appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: Toast/Notification supports semantic tones, title/body/action, dismiss behavior, timeout behavior where appropriate, and accessible live-region semantics. Existing user feedback patterns have a migration path without changing backend notification functionality.
close_reason: Implemented P4 shared primitives (Toast/Notification, Pagination/ResultsSummary, Tooltip/HelpText, DateTimeField/DateDisplay, Skeleton/ProgressIndicator, Disclosure/Accordion) with tests. Validated with frontend typecheck, lint, focused tests, and full poolmaster unit suite.
